title: Instance image glibc skew breaks rdv + better-sqlite3 (trixie builder vs bookworm runtime)
description: Native binaries compiled in the oven/bun:debian build stage (Debian trixie, glibc >=2.38/2.39) require newer glibc than the node:22-slim runtime (Debian bookworm, glibc 2.36). Live rdv-dev/rdv-0: 'rdv --version' -> rc=1 GLIBC_2.39 not found; better-sqlite3 .bun standalone-trace copy needs GLIBC_2.38 -> logging/analytics writes fail ('Log pruning failed' at startup). @libsql survives (ships prebuilt). Effect: agent hooks degrade to curl fallback (ensureRdvCli catch, src/server/index.ts:67), structured logging to logs.db drops. Fix: align runtime base to trixie-slim so glibc matches builder; strengthen the Dockerfile ABI smoke test (Dockerfile:318) to exec 'rdv --version' + load ALL better-sqlite3 copies so future base drift fails the build, not prod. Base branch: fix/instance-login-redirect (5ea6b5ac).
notes: Deeper than glibc alone: TWO drifts. (1) glibc — oven/bun:debian=trixie(2.41) built rdv needing GLIBC_2.39 vs node:22-slim bookworm 2.36. (2) Node ABI — bun 1.3.14 reports Node 24.3.0/NODE_MODULE_VERSION 137; better-sqlite3+node-pty source-built => ABI 137, unloadable on Node 22 (ABI 127). Fix = runtime node:24-trixie-slim (Node24/ABI137/glibc2.41), NOT 22-trixie-slim. Smoke test hardened: rdv --version + require() each standalone better-sqlite3 copy => future bun-ABI drift fails the build. Branch fix/instance-glibc-provisioning.
close_reason: Fixed + VERIFIED live on rdv-dev (image registry.joyful.house/homelab/rdv-instance:1631b92, branch fix/instance-glibc-provisioning). Runtime base -> node:24-trixie-slim (Node24/ABI137/glibc2.41) resolves BOTH drifts: rdv --version -> 'rdv 1.0.0' rc=0 (was GLIBC_2.39), better-sqlite3 LOADS+OPENS OK on the server's .bun copy (was NODE_MODULE_VERSION 137 vs 127), no GLIBC/log-pruning at startup. Hardened ABI smoke test (rdv --version + require each standalone better-sqlite3) now fails the build on future bun-ABI drift.

title: mobile: CF login WebView blocked by Google with disallowed_useragent 403
description:
When the CF Access challenge redirects to Google OAuth, Google detects the Android WebView UA marker (the '; wv)' substring) and returns 'Error 403: disallowed_useragent — This browser or app may not be secure.'

Reproduction: Add Server → CF challenge → click 'Sign in with Google' → blocked.

Affects: every user whose CF Access policy uses Google as an IdP. This is a hard blocker on the auth flow shipped in v0.3.8/v0.3.9.

Fast fix: override WebView userAgent to a Chrome-like UA without the 'wv' marker. Document the trade-offs.

Proper fix (follow-up): use system browser (Custom Tabs / SFSafariViewController via url_launcher) + Universal/App Links to bring the user back. Requires server-side Universal Link configuration.
close_reason: Proper fix shipped via PRs #289 (system-browser flow) + #290 (Android 12 splash safe-zone) + #288 (legacy logo)

title: Phase 6 Epic A — Cloudflare Access auth flow
description:
Currently the Add Server flow only runs a health probe and saves the URL. There is no Cloudflare Access challenge presented, so the app has no CF_Authorization cookie. Subsequent API calls fail (login API shape error #1) and the bridge-spike WebView (bug icon) shows a non-interactive CF challenge page (#0).

**Acceptance:**
- Adding a server opens an in-app WebView at the server URL and lets the user complete the CF Access challenge (Google SSO / OTP / etc.)
- After successful challenge, the native shell extracts the CF_Authorization cookie via WebView CookieStore (per spec §2)
- Cookie is persisted into FlutterSecureStorage keyed by server.<id>.cf_auth
- Dio API client injects the cookie on every request to that server
- Upon 401/403 from a saved server, app surfaces a Reauth flow that re-opens the CF challenge
- Bug-icon entry point (BridgeSpikeScreen) is hidden in release builds (debug-only)

title: Generated SSH key uses PKCS#8 PEM — OpenSSH rejects it as invalid format
description: generateKeypair() in ssh-connection-service.ts emits the private key as PKCS#8 PEM (-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----). OpenSSH refuses to load it: 'Load key: invalid format'. Need to emit native OpenSSH format (-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----) which is what ssh-keygen produces and OpenSSH always accepts. Node has no built-in OpenSSH export, but the raw 32-byte components are available via JWK export ('d' = priv, 'x' = pub).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #239. generateKeypair now emits native OpenSSH private key format (-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----) by hand-encoding the openssh-key-v1 wire format. Verified live with ssh-keygen.

title: SSH plugin drops shellArgs — sessions run bare 'ssh'
description: The SSH plugin returns shellCommand='ssh' + shellArgs=[...] separately, but session-service.ts:639 only uses shellCommand. tmux runs bare 'ssh' which prints usage and exits. Fix: combine into a single shell-quoted string in shellCommand (matching agent plugin's buildAgentCommand convention). Reproduced in production after PR #233 deploy.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #238 (merged). SSH plugin now combines all args into a single shell-quoted shellCommand string, matching the agent plugin convention. Bare 'ssh' bug fixed; sessions now connect properly.

title: Non-tmux resume returns 410 auto-deletes singleton tabs
close_reason: Fixed: ResumeSessionUseCase now consults plugin useTmux flag; SessionContext 410 branch only auto-deletes tmux-backed sessions. Added 19 server + 10 client regression tests.

title: Non-tmux session resume returns 410 → client auto-deletes singleton tabs
description: Smoking-gun repro captured in user's dev logs: POST /api/sessions/{id}/resume returns 410 for non-tmux terminal-type sessions (settings/profiles/recordings/prefs/secrets). Client at SessionContext.tsx:608 interprets 410 as 'tmux session gone, auto-close' and DELETEs the session. Fix: (1) server resume endpoint must detect non-tmux sessions and just mark status=active without tmux attach; (2) client should check terminalType/useTmux before the 410 auto-delete branch. This is why 'clicking Settings from sidebar closes it' — the sync effect fires resume on the newly-activated singleton, server 410s, client deletes.
close_reason: Fixed in 6927f94 (PR #204). Server: ResumeSessionUseCase skips tmux probe when useTmux===false. Client: SessionContext.resumeSession only auto-deletes on 410 for tmux-backed terminal types. Regression test added in ResumeSessionUseCase.test.ts.

title: Adversarial review of refactor plans via codex
description: Have codex critique the full refactor plan set before Phase 1 begins. Coverage: (a) schema design: polymorphic node_preferences + legacy_folder_id bridge + project_id soft-transition columns, (b) migration algorithm: classification by depth+contents, topo-sorted inserts, auto-Default projects, Workspace wrap of root leaves, (c) groups-can-be-active rollup queries (recursive CTE correctness), (d) phase ordering and coupling, (e) risk/gotchas. Produce a prioritized list of blockers and revisions.
design: Review all 7 plan files under docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-*.md. Produce prioritized blocker/revision list before Phase 1 begins.
close_reason: Codex review complete. Findings: 4 P0 blockers (link-table schema mismatch, node_preferences.githubRepoId type mismatch, no cycle/orphan preflight, rollback story missing), 5 P1 concerns (recursive CTE cycle/scale, dual-write scope limited to SessionService, /api/sessions missing projectId parsing, dual-API window too narrow, integration tests missing), 2 P2 suggestions. All 11 findings incorporated into plan updates: Phase 1 (Task 1.1 self-ref FK, Task 1.3 text FK, Task 2.14/2.15 rewrite, Task 2.16 deletion, Task 4b preflight with DFS tricolor, Task 11.1 INHERITABLE_KEYS/PROJECT_ONLY_KEYS, Steps 14.5a/14.5b crash+preflight tests), Phase 3 (Task 2.1 UNION+depth guard, Task 8a projectId parsing, Task 10 dual-write scope table for 11 services, Step 10.7a checklist, Step 11.4/11.5 rollup+concurrent tests, new Exit Criteria), Master (forward-only cutover, Section 6a column appendix, Risk Register rewrite), Phase 6 (folderRepositories removed from drop list). Plans at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-*.md.

title: Harden PWA service worker: per-build cache versioning + update-available toast
description: The SW (src/app/sw.js/route.ts) uses a static CACHE_NAME ('remote-dev-v1') and cache-first script handling, so after a deploy already-open clients render the stale bundle until manually reloaded. Harden it: (1) version CACHE_NAME per build so each deploy purges old caches on activate; (2) make updates WAIT (drop unconditional skipWaiting, add SKIP_WAITING message handler); (3) ServiceWorkerRegistration shows a sonner 'New version available — Reload' toast on update, posts SKIP_WAITING on click, and reloads once on controllerchange (guarded, first-install silent). Surfaced when a user still saw the removed mic button due to SW cache.

title: Forward deploy never copies slot→live .next/standalone (regression from #342/yxvy): deploys report success but serve stale code
description:
ROOT CAUSE of beads fix (#341) not going live despite a green deploy. PR #342 (yxvy) moved the build from PROJECT_ROOT into an isolated deploy-src worktree → blue/green slot. But the FORWARD deploy path in scripts/deploy.ts (the deploy() success flow, ~L1063-1095) only does: buildSlot(inactiveSlot) → stopCurrentServers() → runMigration() → restartViaRdvAsync(). It NEVER copies the freshly-built slot's standalone over the live serving dir (PROJECT_ROOT/.next/standalone), which is where the prod next-server actually runs from (cwd confirmed). Only the ROLLBACK path calls restoreSlotToLive()/restoreStandalone(). Pre-#342 the build happened IN PROJECT_ROOT so the live dir was always fresh and a bare restart sufficed; post-#342 the live dir is never updated, so restartViaRdvAsync() reloads STALE code. The deploy still reports success because the /login + /api/healthz health gates return 200 on the stale build.

IMPACT: every forward deploy since #342 (08:50 2026-06-03) — #342, #343, #341 — built correctly into the slot, recorded the right activeCommit, passed health checks, and reported success, but the running server kept serving the pre-#342 build (PROJECT_ROOT/.next/standalone, mtime 08:21). Silent. deploy:status shows the correct commit (read from deploy-src HEAD) so it looks fine.

MANUAL REMEDIATION applied 2026-06-03 ~09:55 to unblock: disabled watchdog (launchctl bootout dev.remote.app.watchdog) → rdv stop prod → restoreStandalone(blue/standalone → PROJECT_ROOT/.next/standalone) → rdv start prod → verified /login+/api/healthz=200 + live dir contains the fix → re-enabled watchdog. blue=2b63365 includes #341/#342/#343 so all three are now live.

FIX: in the forward deploy success path, after buildSlot() and before/around restart, copy the built slot's standalone over PROJECT_ROOT/.next/standalone (reuse restoreStandalone(), the same tested helper rollback uses) — i.e. call restoreSlotToLive(inactiveSlot) before restartViaRdvAsync(). Add a regression test in tests/deploy-lib.test.ts asserting the forward path restores the slot to live. Consider also having the health gate probe a route that would catch stale server code (hard in general).

Related: [[deploy_untracked_blocks_merge]] yxvy, j0x5 (rollback restore).

title: Beads sidebar shows 'not set up' for beads-enabled projects (bd 1.0.5 dolt schema drift)
description:
The Beads sidebar reported 'Beads is not set up for this project' for projects that DO have beads. Two compounding bugs:

1) Backend (root cause): bd 1.0.5 restructured the dolt 'dependencies' table — column depends_on_id renamed to depends_on_issue_id (plus new depends_on_wisp_id/depends_on_external), and epic->child links migrated from type 'child-of' to 'parent-child'. src/services/beads-service.ts still queried the old column, so every /api/beads request threw 'column "depends_on_id" could not be found' → HTTP 500 (216 errors in logs in one morning).

2) Frontend (the misleading text): BeadsSidebar checked !initialized before error; since initialized starts false and a failed first fetch never flips it, the 'not set up' branch masked the real 500.

Fix: rename column across all dependency queries + row type/mapper; widen epic-children query to type IN ('child-of','parent-child'); add NULL-target guards for future wisp/external deps; reorder sidebar so error renders before !initialized (with Retry); surface the server's real error in BeadsContext. Validated against live dolt; gates green. Follow-up: remote-dev-4tp9 (type-blind dependency grouping).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #341 (merged to master 2b633653)

title: Roll out working-dir fix (PR #340) to rdv.joyful.house instance images
description:
PR #340 / commit fb24b244 fixes terminal+agent sessions ignoring a configured working directory outside the server's $HOME (validatePath in src/server/terminal.ts now accepts any existing dir + warns instead of silently falling back to home). Merged to master and verified LIVE on dev.bryanli.net.

BUT the bug was reported on rdv.joyful.house/dev — the k3s homelab INSTANCE platform, a separate deployment whose rdv app runs in its own container image built via Forgejo CI -> Harbor -> ArgoCD (source=forgejo, NOT github). That image must be rebuilt from master and the /dev instance rolled to it before the fix takes effect where the user tested.

Action: ensure master reaches Forgejo, rebuild the instance/dev-env image, roll the /dev instance, then re-verify: create a project with a working dir under the instance user's home, launch a terminal+agent, confirm both start in that dir (not home). Check Settings -> Logs for the new 'Working directory ...' warn if it still lands in home.

title: Per-instance persistent package provisioning (layered baseline + PVC manifest)
description: Anything an agent installs at runtime (apt/npm -g/pip/cargo) lands on the ephemeral container layer and is wiped on pod restart/image bump. Add: (1) PVC-backed prefixes under $RDV_DATA_DIR/provision (NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX, CARGO_HOME, RUSTUP_HOME, PIPX_HOME/BIN) exported in docker/entrypoint.sh BEFORE servers start so they propagate via getCleanEnvironment (terminal.ts:49) into agent PTYs -> ad-hoc agent global installs persist automatically. (2) Declarative manifest: supervisor baseline via SUPERVISOR_INSTANCE_BASELINE_PACKAGES (JSON) -> RDV_PROVISION_BASELINE env (readProvisionEnv/buildInstanceEnv), layered with per-instance $RDV_DATA_DIR/provision/packages.yaml; merged+installed in the existing backgrounded entrypoint block (non-blocking). apt re-applied each boot (ephemeral); npm/pip(pipx)/cargo install to PVC prefixes (persist); rustup bootstrapped into PVC on demand for cargo. Add python3/python3-venv/pipx to runtime image. paths.ts helpers + docs + CHANGELOG. Follow-up: per-instance dashboard-editable column. Base branch: fix/instance-login-redirect.
close_reason: Built + VERIFIED on rdv-dev (image 1631b92). PVC prefixes (npm/cargo/rustup/pipx under /var/lib/rdv/provision) created on boot; per-instance packages.yaml merged with supervisor RDV_PROVISION_BASELINE. Test: apt:[ripgrep]+npm:[cowsay] -> ripgrep@/usr/bin/rg (apt, ephemeral re-applied each boot), cowsay@/var/lib/rdv/provision/npm-global/bin (npm, PVC). PERSISTENCE PROVEN: removed cowsay from manifest, restarted -> still present+runs (survived on PVC, not reinstalled). Layered baseline env wired in provisioner (untested-on-cluster; needs supervisor redeploy+env to exercise).

title: [bug] deploy:rollback restarts but does NOT restore the served build (rolls back state, not code)
description: Observed in prod 2026-06-03. A bad deploy (d0b5e0e6: relative Location in proxy.ts → '/' 500'd with TypeError: Invalid URL) was rolled back via 'bun run deploy:rollback'. The rollback flipped state.json (green→blue) + restarted via rdv.ts and logged 'Rollback to blue successful' — but '/' STAYED 500. Root cause: the server runs from PROJECT_ROOT/.next/standalone, and deploy builds into that dir then cpSync's it to builds/<slot>/standalone. Rollback flips state + restarts but never copies builds/<targetSlot>/standalone BACK to PROJECT_ROOT/.next/standalone, so the restart serves the still-broken build. Recovery required: git reset --hard <good-commit> + bun run build + rdv restart prod (manual). Fix: rollback must cpSync builds/<targetSlot>/standalone (+ static/public) → PROJECT_ROOT/.next/standalone before the restart — the mirror of the build→slot copy. Compounded by the healthz-only gate (remote-dev-2cd4) which returns 200 while SSR '/' 500s, so both the deploy AND the rollback reported green on a broken build.
close_reason: Fixed + validated in d13265dd (on master, live on prod). rollbackTo now restores the target slot's standalone over PROJECT_ROOT/.next/standalone before restart; healthCheckLocal gates on healthCheckSSR (probes / →2xx/3xx and /login →200). Unit tests (tests/deploy-lib.test.ts, 6 assertions) + a live good→good rollback that demonstrably restored the build (live mtime 20:46→21:11) and ran the SSR probe; the landing deploy also exercised the probe.

title: [bug] Instance OIDC: desktop login loops back to /<slug>/login after successful auth
description: Post-OIDC-callback the user lands back on /<slug>/login showing the login form even though the session cookie is now valid → looks like a login loop on desktop. Root cause: the proxy '!scoped' guard (commit 4e06c8f) correctly stopped the proxy bouncing /login→/ on mere cookie presence, but nothing replaced the logged-in→home redirect for a VALID session in scoped mode, and signIn('oidc') has no callbackUrl so NextAuth returns to the sign-in-initiating page (/<slug>/login). Fix: (1) login/page.tsx async getAuthSession()→redirect('/') (authoritative, correct-env); (2) signIn('oidc',{callbackUrl:prefixPath('/')}); (3) auth.ts pages.signIn=`${BASE_PATH}/login` so error redirects stay in-instance instead of the supervisor root. Part of PR #333 / epic remote-dev-jvcx.
close_reason: Fixed in branch fix/instance-login-redirect (5ea6b5ac + 71bc5374); verified end-to-end on homelab dev instance (real login lands in app, account linked, no JWE errors)

comments.author: bryanli
comments.text: PROVEN on live image 3f44cbc via forged valid-session cookie (real user 63d19460): GET /dev → 200 (session valid, user IS logged in), but GET /dev/login → 200 rendering the login form = the exact desktop loop (OIDC dumps the freshly-authed user on /<slug>/login post-callback). Fix 71bc5374 (login/page.tsx getAuthSession→redirect) must turn /dev/login+valid-cookie into 307→/dev. Build #151 in flight.

comments.author: bryanli
comments.text: VERIFIED FIXED on image 71bc537 (demo+dev rolled+ready). Forged valid-cookie probe: [A] /dev=200, [B] /dev/login=307→/dev/ (was 200 render = the loop), [C] /dev garbage=307→/dev/login, [D] /dev/login garbage=200 (no loop). All pass.

comments.author: bryanli
comments.text: DEFINITIVE root cause from real-login HAR (127 entries): the instance login page's signIn('oidc') hit the ROOT /api/auth/* (providers, csrf, signin, callback) — NOT /dev/api/auth/*. The authorize request carried client_id=rdv-SUPERVISOR-... and redirect_uri=https://rdv.joyful.house/api/auth/callback/oidc (no /dev). So the SUPERVISOR completed the OIDC login and set a session cookie signed with ITS secret; the dev instance then failed to decode it → server logs show 'JWTSessionError: Invalid Compact JWE' on every /dev request → bounce to /dev/login (loop), and account stayed 0 on the instance (linked in supervisor DB instead). Cause: the login route skips AppShell, which is the only place that sets the next-auth/react client basePath; signIn defaulted to /api/auth (root=supervisor under the single front door). Fix 5ea6b5ac wraps the login form in SessionProvider basePath=${runtimeBasePath()}/api/auth (mirrors AppShell). The earlier redirect/callbackUrl/pages.signIn fixes (71bc5374) remain correct but were secondary. Building as run #152 (tag 5ea6b5a).

comments.author: bryanli
comments.text: VERIFIED FIXED end-to-end on image 5ea6b5a. User did a real incognito login → landed in the app. Instance DB: account rows 0→1 (oidc → bryan@joyful.house), 'Invalid Compact JWE' errors stopped (0 in 5m). Server-side authorize URL now uses client_id=rdv-instances-… + redirect_uri=…/dev/api/auth/callback/oidc (was rdv-supervisor-… + root). Fix = SessionProvider basePath on the login route (5ea6b5ac) + the redirect/callbackUrl/pages.signIn hardening (71bc5374). Branch fix/instance-login-redirect pushed; PR-to-master HELD per user (homelab runs from branch images). Removed temp LOG_LEVEL=debug from rdv-dev sts.

title: instance: schema-bootstrap-on-boot (fresh PVC has no tables → scheduler fails → not Ready)
description: Provisioned instances boot but never reach readyz: the fresh PVC DB has no schema (entrypoint only materializes basePath + starts servers; image has no drizzle-kit/drizzle.config; only incremental migrations 0013-0018, no full baseline). Scheduler init fails (session_schedule missing) → terminal /health 503 → readyz 503. Manually applying the 59-table DDL (drizzle-kit export) + restart → readyz ok. FIX: bake a full IF-NOT-EXISTS schema (generated at build via drizzle-kit export) + apply via node+@libsql/client in the entrypoint before starting servers. NOTE: bun crashes loading the @libsql node binding (exit 133), so bootstrap+seed must run under node, not bun (db:seed/seed Job use bun → will crash).
close_reason: Shipped + validated: fresh-PVC instances auto-bootstrap schema on boot; dev/demo reach readyz with no manual step. In image fcdd4ce (PR #333).

title: instance image: @libsql/linux-x64-gnu missing — fix Dockerfile COPY (was overlaying whole scope onto symlinked @libsql/client)
description: ROOT CAUSE (empirically reproduced): @libsql/linux-x64-gnu is a transitive platform-optional of libsql. In this large bun workspace, bun install --frozen-lockfile does NOT hoist it to top-level node_modules/@libsql/ (full-project amd64 repro: @libsql/ had only 'client'). The esbuild-bundled dist-terminal does a bare require('@libsql/linux-x64-gnu') → MODULE_NOT_FOUND → CrashLoop. Build #139 (whole-scope COPY) failed on BuildKit symlink collision; #140 (leaf cp) failed because the leaf isn't hoisted to copy. FIX (verified on amd64): declare @libsql/linux-x64-gnu as a root optionalDependency (forces hoist) + regen bun.lock; Dockerfile stages the leaf into the runtime image's existing @libsql/ dir (standalone trace omits it); smoke test now requires the binding directly to gate regressions. PR #333.
close_reason: Image fix validated: rdv-instance:d80c2a2 boots with 0 restarts and reaches readyz {db,tmux,terminal all ok} once schema exists. @libsql/linux-x64-gnu hoist + leaf-copy confirmed working end-to-end on the homelab.

title: watchdog: /api/healthz returns 200 while SSR pages 500 — blind to broken builds
description: 2026-06-01 outage: prod served HTTP 500 on /login (and every SSR page) due to a missing client-reference-manifest, but GET /api/healthz still returned 200. scripts/watchdog.sh probes only /api/healthz, so it never triggered a restart and the outage sat undetected (root cause: PROJECT_ROOT/.next was deleted out from under the running next-server; the orphaned-inode process answered API routes from memory but failed per-route lazy loads). healthz proves the Node process is up, not that the build is intact. Fix: have the watchdog probe a real SSR route (e.g. GET /login expecting 200) and/or assert the active build's *_client-reference-manifest.js files exist, so this failure mode auto-recovers. Relates to remote-dev-yxvy.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #342 (merged 462b4056). watchdog.sh now probes /login (SSR) in addition to /api/healthz; non-200 SSR triggers restart; restart-storm hardened.

comments.author: bryanli
comments.text: Live evidence 2026-06-03: a prod deploy that 500'd on '/' (SSR) passed BOTH the deploy health gate AND the rollback health gate because they only check /api/healthz (200) — never the SSR '/' route. The bad build stayed live through a 'successful' rollback. Health gate must probe at least one SSR page (e.g. '/' expecting 3xx/200, not 500) before declaring success/rollback-success. See sibling rollback bug.

comments.author: bryanli
comments.text: Deploy/rollback gate portion now addressed (d13265dd): healthCheckSSR probes SSR '/' + '/login' so a /-500 build fails BOTH deploy and rollback (previously both passed on /api/healthz alone). The WATCHDOG still has the same healthz blindspot — it can reuse scripts/deploy-lib.ts isAcceptableSsrStatus + an SSR probe. Leaving this open for the watchdog side.

title: Startup command first char eaten by oh-my-zsh update prompt
description:
createSession() types the startup command via tmux send-keys into a freshly-spawned interactive zsh. When oh-my-zsh's periodic auto-update prompt ([oh-my-zsh] Would you like to update? [Y/n]) is on screen during shell init, it consumes the FIRST typed character (e.g. 'claude' -> 'laude'). Affects all startup commands (shell + agent). Time-triggered (~13d oh-my-zsh interval), not a repo code change.

Fix: in createSession, when a startup command will be typed, (1) inject DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATE=true + DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT=true via tmux new-session -e so the shell reaches a clean prompt, and (2) wait for the pane to go quiescent (poll capture-pane, ~75ms interval, ~1.5s cap) before send-keys, hardening against init-output races. Verified via A/B repro with capture-pane.
close_reason: Closed

title: deploy: webhook reports success even when the working-tree git merge aborts (silent failed deploy)
description:
## Problem
`scripts/deploy.ts` runs `git fetch` + `git merge --ff-only origin/main` in the **live working tree** (`/Users/bryanli/Projects/btli/remote-dev`) before building into the inactive blue/green slot. If that tree contains an **untracked file whose path an incoming commit adds as tracked**, git aborts the merge ("The following untracked working tree files would be overwritten by merge ... Aborting") and the deploy fails.

The failure is **invisible from CI**: the GitHub `Deploy to Production` workflow only POSTs the webhook and checks for a 2xx, so it shows **green** even though the server-side deploy aborted. The active slot never advances — only `bun run deploy:status` and `~/.remote-dev/deploy/deploy.log` reveal it. Blue/green keeps the previous slot serving, so prod stays healthy on the **old** commit while the new one silently never goes live, and every subsequent deploy keeps failing until the stray file is removed by hand.

## How it surfaced
Shipping PR #309 (docs overhaul): an orphaned untracked `docs/MOBILE_ARCHITECTURE.md` collided with the now-tracked version. Workflow run 26703134698 was green; `deploy:status` showed the active commit stuck on the prior PR (#310). Recovered by removing the orphan and re-running `bun run deploy` (manual CLI path).

## Impact
- Silent false-positive deploys: green CI, stale prod.
- Any leftover untracked file in the deploy tree (the checkout accumulates `~/`, `.beads/*`, `docs/claude/plans/*`) is a latent landmine that bricks the deploy pipeline.
- No feedback loop from the async server-side deploy back to the webhook caller / CI.

Found via the docs-overhaul ship. See memory `deploy_untracked_blocks_merge`; related to the deploy-resilience (#307) and watchdog (#310) work.
design: Options (pick/refine): (1) Build from a clean 'git worktree add' of origin/master into the inactive slot instead of 'git merge --ff-only' in the live tree, so untracked cruft can't block it. (2) Before merging, 'git stash --include-untracked' or relocate the specific colliding paths. (3) Preflight in /api/deploy or deploy.ts that detects untracked-file collisions and refuses/cleans before returning 202. (4) Add a feedback path so the async server-side deploy outcome is observable from the webhook caller / CI, not just deploy.log.
acceptance_criteria: A deploy whose working-tree merge would abort on untracked files either (a) safely cleans/relocates them and proceeds, or (b) fails visibly (non-zero exit + log + a signal CI/operator can observe). The GH 'Deploy to Production' workflow result and 'deploy:status' active commit can no longer disagree silently. Gotcha documented in docs/DEPLOYMENT.md.
notes:
FOLLOW-UP FIX — build deploys from an isolated git worktree (this issue's design option 1; supersedes the 1oxx `reset --hard` workaround).

Current state after remote-dev-1oxx (PR #311): buildSlot() syncs with `git fetch` -> `git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/master` (divergence guard) -> `git reset --hard origin/master`, all run in PROJECT_ROOT (scripts/deploy.ts:48 = the LIVE dev checkout /Users/bryanli/Projects/btli/remote-dev). That fixed the untracked-collision abort, but the ancestry guard only protects un-pushed COMMITS — `reset --hard` still discards uncommitted/dirty tracked changes AND deletes colliding untracked files in the live checkout. Because that checkout is also the interactive dev/agent working tree ("parallel work is always happening" here), a deploy can silently wipe in-progress work.

Proper fix: never mutate the dev checkout during a deploy. Build each blue/green slot from a deploy-OWNED git worktree pinned to origin/master, isolated from PROJECT_ROOT:
- `git worktree add --detach <deploy-src> origin/master` (or a persistent deploy worktree that runs `git fetch` + `reset --hard origin/master` INSIDE the isolated tree, where reset --hard is unambiguously safe), then build the slot from there.
- Benefits: (a) deploys can't clobber the developer's uncommitted work; (b) dirty/untracked cruft in PROJECT_ROOT (.beads/issues.jsonl churn, stray ~/ dir, orphaned docs) is irrelevant to deploys; (c) deploy is decoupled from concurrent edits in the main tree; (d) keeps THIS issue's untracked-collision root cause solved.
- Considerations: worktrees share .git objects (cheap); materialize per-worktree node_modules/build artifacts via the existing scripts/worktree-warm.sh clonefile path; keep the deploy worktree under a gitignored location (e.g. .worktrees/ or under ~/.remote-dev) and prune stale ones; move the divergence guard into the isolated worktree.

Relates to remote-dev-1oxx (the reset --hard it improves on). Not yet implemented.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #312 (deploy-outcome feedback loop: last-deploy.json + HMAC-authed GET /api/deploy/status + workflow polling) and PR #313 (proxy bypass for /api/deploy/status + jq errexit-safety). Verified end-to-end live: deploy d6acb8b2 → workflow run 26712311613 GREEN, last-deploy.json status=success, deploy:status agrees. Workflow result and deploy:status can no longer disagree silently.

title: watchdog spuriously restarts healthy prod: probes CF-Access URL, 302 misread as failure
description: scripts/watchdog.sh health-checks the public CF-Access-fronted URL (https://dev.bryanli.net/api/sessions) and accepts only 200/401/403 as alive. CF Access intercepts at the edge and returns 302 (redirect to cloudflareaccess.com login; auth_status=NONE, service_token_status=false) because the watchdog's local API key is an APP Bearer token, not a CF Access service token. So every probe returns 302 -> counted as FAIL -> after MAX_FAILURES=3 consecutive (~15 min) the watchdog restarts HEALTHY prod via 'rdv.ts restart prod'. Combined with the instance-lock deadlock (remote-dev-i85i, now fixed), each spurious restart became a dual-writer outage. This is a co-driver of the recurring prod impact. FIX: probe the LOCAL origin health via the unix socket (curl --unix-socket ~/.remote-dev/run/nextjs.sock http://localhost/api/healthz), accept 200; TCP fallback 127.0.0.1:$PORT for dev. Local healthz is unauthenticated and bypasses CF Access entirely. Remove the now-dead API-key/CF-Access probe logic. Discovered during remote-dev-i85i watchdog re-enable.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #310 (commit 1a67e77c): watchdog health-checks local origin over unix socket instead of CF-Access URL. Deployed, manual run reports OK(200), watchdog re-enabled without spurious restarts.

title: Production deploy restart wedges: terminal socket never ready + rollback fails (blind)
description:
Auto-deploy (master push → /api/deploy → deploy.ts blue-green → rdv.ts) intermittently fails: terminal server never creates its socket within the 90s health check, rollback re-runs the same start and ALSO fails → prod down, manual fresh rdv:prod recovers (cf. memory rdv_restart_bad_state). Recurring (also 05-21, pre-instance-lock).
ROOT/ENABLER: all child stdio is discarded (rdv inherit → login-shell → /api/deploy spawns deploy.ts stdio:'ignore'); logs.db empty in window → blind.
CONTRIB: deploy.ts stop clears sockets+pidfiles but NOT instance.lock; terminal shutdown() is unbounded → exceeded 10s stop timeout → SIGKILL → releaseInstanceLock never ran → orphaned lock.
FIX: (1) rdv.ts prod redirect child stdio to ~/.remote-dev/logs/{terminal,nextjs}.log; (2) bounded shutdown() exits <grace; (3) deploy.ts stop unlinks instance.lock; (4) tests + local restart-cycle validation.
notes: Real fix in progress on fix/instance-lock-single-host (worktree .worktrees/lock-singlehost, off bfb2feef).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #308 (commit 0c3c63f9): instance lock no-ops single-host + readyz checks terminal over unix socket. Deployed to prod (293351d3), verified healthz+readyz=200, no lock file, clean restart, watchdog re-enabled.

title: Fix broken FCM push notifications in new Flutter mobile app
description:
Push notifications don't work in mobile/ (Flutter) but worked in archive/mobile-flutter/. Multiple code bugs prevent FCM from working even after Firebase config files are dropped in.

Root causes:
1. push_token_registrar.dart posts to /api/push-tokens but server is at /api/notifications/push-token. DELETE also wrong.
2. PushTokenRegistrar.start() is never called — token never sent to server.
3. android/settings.gradle.kts + android/app/build.gradle.kts missing com.google.gms.google-services plugin.
4. AndroidManifest.xml missing FCM meta-data (default_notification_icon, default_notification_color, default_notification_channel_id=rdv_notifications).
5. Missing ic_notification drawables and notification_accent color resource.
6. main.dart does not register firebaseMessagingBackgroundHandler.

Out of scope (user responsibility): adding com.remotedev.app Android + iOS apps to existing Firebase project (no new keys), downloading google-services.json + GoogleService-Info.plist.

title: Fix WebGL terminal rendering artifacts during scrolling in long-running sessions
description:
Long-running terminal sessions show stale/wrong glyphs during scrolling (TUI output or mouse-wheel), persisting after prior fix d5438d9 (clear atlas on scroll + lineFeed threshold).

Root cause (xterm.js addon-webgl 0.19.0): TextureAtlas._mergePages() rewrites glyph indices when the atlas hits 4+ pages. The merge sets _requestClearModel=true, but:
- Merge happens INSIDE _updateModel during a render — cells already bound in the same frame have stale page indices in the vertex buffer
- The per-cell skip in _updateModel ('if model[i] matches cell, continue') prevents rebinds for subsequent frames where the cell content hasn't changed
- The 'next frame must fire to honor _requestClearModel' contract relies on the render scheduler firing again; if output stops, the stale state can persist

Matches xterm.js #5847 (active, exact reproduction), with related context in #4480, #4534, #4351.

Why current fix misses it:
- visibilitychange/DPR clears: handle separate scenarios (tab restore, display moves), not atlas merge
- 2s-throttled scroll-up clear: fires only when scrolled up; misses merges at bottom-of-buffer; first wheel after each 2s window is the only signal
- 5000-line-feed clear: arbitrary, not tied to actual merge events

Fix: Listen to webglAddon.onRemoveTextureAtlasCanvas — fires only inside _mergePages._deletePage(). On the next animation frame after a remove event, call webglAddon.clearTextureAtlas() to zero the model + atlas and force every cell to rebind with current page indices.

Also remove the redundant scroll-up throttle clear and 5000-line-feed clear; keep visibility/DPR clears.

Update Terminal.fontRace.test.tsx FakeWebglAddon to stub onAddTextureAtlasCanvas / onRemoveTextureAtlasCanvas (returning {dispose}) and FakeTerminal to stub onLineFeed already there.
close_reason: Shipped in 7a1018e: WebglAddon.onRemoveTextureAtlasCanvas listener with rAF-debounced clearTextureAtlas. Replaces 2s throttled scroll-up clear and 5000 line-feed threshold.

title: Fix mobile notifications post-refactor: match legacy contract and route to session
description: Mobile notifications broken after refactor from archive/mobile-flutter. Need to: (1) accept the same notification contract (payload shape from FCM data), (2) route to the correct session when a notification is tapped. Investigate diff between archive/mobile-flutter/lib/infrastructure/push/push_notification_service.dart and mobile/lib/infrastructure/push/{fcm_push_service.dart, notification_tap_handler.dart}.
notes:
Root cause: NotificationTapHandler is defined at mobile/lib/infrastructure/push/notification_tap_handler.dart but NEVER instantiated. main.dart only calls FcmPushService().initialize() (Firebase+perms) and container.read(appLinkListenerProvider) (custom-scheme deep links only). FirebaseMessaging.onMessageOpenedApp and getInitialMessage are never subscribed to.

Server FCM data payload (src/services/notification-service.ts dispatchPush): {notificationId, type, sessionId?, sessionName?}. Legacy handler also called markNotificationsRead([notificationId]) on tap (archive/mobile-flutter/lib/infrastructure/push/push_notification_service.dart:157). New handler drops that read-sync.

Fix: (1) wire notificationTapHandlerProvider eagerly from main.dart so getInitialMessage and onMessageOpenedApp fire; (2) call NotificationsApi.markRead([notificationId]) on tap when active server is available.
close_reason: Closed

title: P3.3: onTokenRefresh handler with multi-server fan-out (fix from deprecated app)
description: FCM issues ONE token per app installation, not per server. The deprecated app's bug: it only re-registered with the active server on token rotation. Otherwise users with multiple servers stop receiving notifications from non-active servers after a token rotation.

title: P1.5.3: Implement keyboard layout pattern (mandatory)
description:
LOAD-BEARING — without this the WebView resizes when the soft keyboard rises and xterm.js fires a resize event causing PTY SIGWINCH and content reflow.

Pattern:
- Scaffold(resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false) on SessionView
- MediaQuery.viewInsetsOf(context).bottom drives input bar bottom offset
- WebView height EXPLICITLY constrained: total - statusBar - smartKeys - inputBar - keyboardInset (hard constraint, NOT Expanded)
- iOS: keyboardDisplayRequiresUserAction: false, disallowOverScroll: true
- Android: useHybridComposition: true
- SafeArea ONLY around input bar (top: false), not for keyboard

Verify on iOS + Android physical devices.
notes: Spec §4 keyboard layout pattern (mandatory)

title: P1.6: Dio API client + AuthInterceptor (cookie injection)
description:
lib/infrastructure/api/. Implement RemoteDevClient using Dio bound to active server. Implement _AuthInterceptor that reads CF_Authorization from SecureStoragePort and injects 'Cookie: CF_Authorization=<value>' on every outbound request.

LOAD-BEARING ARCHITECTURAL RULE: Dio NEVER reads from the WebView cookie store. On Android, WebView.CookieManager and okhttp (Dio) are completely independent stores. The WebView is the source-of-truth during *auth*; flutter_secure_storage is the relay; Dio reads from secure storage on every request.
notes: Spec §2.2 rule 3, §3
close_reason: Closed

title: P1.5: CookieReader — extract CF_Authorization from WebView
description: lib/infrastructure/auth/. After WebView's onLoadStop fires on the active server's origin, read cookies via flutter_inappwebview's CookieManager.getCookies(url). Use Uri.parse(serverUrl).origin for the URL argument (scheme + host + port). Add 200–400ms post-onLoadStop delay (or retry with backoff) to handle WKHTTPCookieStore async timing on iOS. Persist CF_Authorization to SecureStoragePort keyed by serverId. Background-killed-app FCM token refresh therefore has access to a (possibly stale) cookie.
notes: Spec §3
close_reason: Closed

title: P1.4: WebView host with onWebViewCreated handler registration
description:
Implement lib/infrastructure/webview/ — WebView factory + bridge channel. CRITICAL ARCHITECTURAL RULE: all addJavaScriptHandler() registrations happen in onWebViewCreated, NEVER in onLoadStop. (Lazy registration produces TypeError: window.flutter_inappwebview.callHandler is not a function during page init.)

iOS InAppWebViewSettings: keyboardDisplayRequiresUserAction: false, disallowOverScroll: true.
Android: useHybridComposition: true (required for keyboard/overlay interaction in Flutter hybrid views since 3.x).

Implement navigation policy: shouldOverrideUrlLoading allows only <serverOrigin>/m/*. CF Access redirects explicitly allow-listed. All other URLs intercepted and routed to onLinkOpen handler. Without this a URL in terminal output could navigate the WebView away from the terminal mid-session.

WebView construction is DEFERRED until biometric gate resolves (avoids cold-start jank on Android — WebView init on platform thread).
notes: Spec §2.2 rules 1, 4, 5

title: Phase 3: Push notifications (FCM) + tap-nav into native session route
description:
Wire FCM end-to-end. Preserves payload contract from deprecated app: data.sessionId, data.channelId, data.notificationId, data.kind. Server-side push_token table + PushNotificationGateway already exists.

Required:
- Firebase setup (firebase_core + firebase_messaging)
- requestPermission + getToken + POST /api/push-tokens (cookie auth)
- onTokenRefresh handler with multi-server fan-out (re-register with ALL saved servers, not just active — fix from deprecated app)
- iOS setForegroundNotificationPresentationOptions(alert/badge/sound: true)
- Tap-nav: sessionId → push native SessionScreen; channelId → native ChannelScreen; neither → Notifications tab
- Mark-as-read on tap: PATCH /api/notifications {ids: [notificationId]}
- MethodChannel('com.remotedev.remote_dev/notifications') for Android cancelAll
- Cross-device dismissal listener via existing PWA WebSocket

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-flutter-app-redesign-design.md §5
notes: Ship gate: receive a push, tap, land on the right session/channel; mark-read syncs to web.
close_reason: Phase 3 shipped via feat/mobile-phase-3 branch. 113 tests pass + 1 skipped. APK builds in 22s. All 7 P3 tasks complete.

title: Phase 2: Native Sessions tab + bottom tab bar + native session-view chrome
description:
Native Flutter widgets for the Sessions surface and the chrome around the embedded terminal WebView. Largest phase — Sessions tab + project tree sheet + new-session sheet + native SessionStatusBar + SmartKeyStrip + MobileInputBar (native!) + pinch-zoom gesture wrapper. All wired to rdv-bridge (input, key, paste, setFontSize, scrollToBottom).

Keyboard layout pattern is mandatory (spec §4):
- Scaffold(resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false)
- MediaQuery.viewInsetsOf bottom drives input bar offset
- WebView height explicitly constrained
- Android: useHybridComposition: true

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-flutter-app-redesign-design.md §2, §4
notes: Ship gate: open a session from the native Sessions tab, type into the terminal via the native input bar, see characters appear in the WebView terminal.
close_reason: Phase 2 shipped via feat/mobile-phase-2 branch. 95 tests pass + 1 skipped. APK builds. All 10 P2 tasks complete.

title: Phase 1.5: Bridge smoke-test spike — de-risk Phase 2
description:
1–2 day spike with the absolute minimum native chrome (single TextField, no smart keys, no status bar) wired to the rdv-bridge. Validates the riskiest assumptions before building Phase 2's full native chrome:
- rdvBridge.input(text) reaches xterm.js and renders in terminal
- onTerminalReady arrives in Dart and unlocks input
- Native TextField + WebView coexist without keyboard reflowing the terminal (the load-bearing keyboard layout pattern from spec §4)
- Tested on iOS + Android physical devices (not just simulators)

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-flutter-app-redesign-design.md §4 (keyboard layout pattern), §12 Phase 1.5
notes: Output: a thin proof-of-concept screen + go/no-go decision on Phase 2 layout strategy. If keyboard issues found, document the chosen mitigation here before Phase 2 starts.
close_reason: Phase 1.5 shipped via PR #249. 30 tests pass + 1 skipped (manual device test). APK builds in 29s. Manual physical-device test plan documented for human follow-up.

title: Phase 1: Flutter shell scaffold + WebView host + in-WebView CF Access auth
description:
Scaffold the new Flutter project at mobile/ at repo root. Set up flutter_inappwebview-based WebView host. Implement in-WebView CF Access auth: WebView loads /m/* URL → CF challenge happens inside WebView → cookie extracted via CookieManager.getCookies() → persisted to flutter_secure_storage → Dio uses it for native API calls. Server picker scaffold (no UI polish yet). Native splash via flutter_native_splash.

Architectural rules (load-bearing — see spec §2.2):
- All addJavaScriptHandler registrations in onWebViewCreated
- Native-to-WebView calls gated on onTerminalReady
- Dio NEVER reads from WebView cookie store; reads from flutter_secure_storage
- WebView construction deferred until biometric gate resolves
- Navigation policy: only allow <serverOrigin>/m/*

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-flutter-app-redesign-design.md §2, §3, §9, §11
notes: Ship gate: can sign in via CF Access in WebView, cookie extracted to native, /m/session/<id> renders inside WebView.
close_reason: Phase 1 shipped via PR #248. 25/25 tests, debug APK built.

title: Phase 0: PWA mobile-embed routes & rdv-bridge JS adapter
description:
PWA-side precondition for the new Flutter app. Add narrow mobile-only routes /m/session/<id>, /m/channel/<id>, /m/recording/<id> that render only their target surface (no MobileShell, no bottom tab bar). Add window.rdvBridge JS adapter (versioned v1) the native Flutter shell will drive via evaluateJavascript. Independently shippable — routes render in desktop browser for testing.

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-flutter-app-redesign-design.md §10
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-08-flutter-app-phase-0-pwa-routes.md
notes: Ship gate: routes render in desktop browser, window.rdvBridge.version === 1, all unit tests pass.
close_reason: Phase 0 implementation shipped via PR #247. All 10 tasks (P0.1–P0.10) completed via subagent-driven development workflow with two-stage review per task and one final whole-branch review. Manual smoke test deferred to PR reviewer.

title: Flutter app redesign aligned with mobile PWA
description:
Replace the deprecated archive/mobile-flutter/ Flutter app with a new hybrid native + WebView Flutter app aligned with the mobile PWA. Native widgets own tab bar, lists, sheets, dropdowns, dialogs, session input, smart-keys, biometric lock, splash, deep links. WebView hosts terminal canvas, channel view, and recording playback via narrow /m/<surface>/<id> PWA routes. Preserves the FCM payload contract and Android signing env-var contract from the deprecated app.

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-flutter-app-redesign-design.md
Plan (Phase 0): docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-08-flutter-app-phase-0-pwa-routes.md
notes: Parent epic. Six phases: Phase 0 (PWA prep), Phase 1 (Flutter shell), Phase 1.5 (bridge spike), Phase 2 (native session view), Phase 3 (push), Phase 4 (notifications/channels/profile/deep-links), Phase 5 (biometric, multi-server, store submission).
close_reason: Flutter app redesign complete. 6 phases shipped (PRs #247-#252 + this). 49 tasks. APK builds successfully. The 'runs without issue' physical-device test is owner-driven via docs/mobile-bridge-spike-test-plan.md + docs/mobile-firebase-setup.md + docs/mobile-store-submission.md.

title: Mobile terminal disappears on Pixel Fold unfold (MobileShell strips height wrapper above 768px)
description: When a UA-mobile device (e.g. Pixel 10 Pro Fold) unfolds, viewport crosses 768px. MobileApp keeps rendering MobileSessionView (UA-based useMobile is true), but MobileShell's pass-through path (useIsMobileViewport false) strips its 'h-[100dvh] flex flex-col' wrapper. MobileSessionView's outer 'h-full' then has no bounded ancestor, so the terminal's 'flex-1 min-h-0' collapses to 0. User sees only the status bar and input bar; terminal is invisible. Not related to focus-promotion cooldown. Fix: ensure MobileShell provides a bounded-height wrapper even when bypassing its mobile chrome (or remove the pass-through, since MobileShell is only mounted under MobileApp which only mounts on UA-mobile).
close_reason: MobileShell now keeps h-[100dvh] flex-col wrapper above 768px (drops only tab bar + safe-area chrome). Fixes Pixel Fold unfold collapsing terminal to 0. Merged from fix/mobile-shell-height-9rvt.

title: Resize-on-focus not firing — missing xterm terminal.onFocus hook
description: The focus-based primary promotion (PR #229, beads remote-dev-nlfm) only sends client_focus on window.focus and visibilitychange. These DO NOT fire when the user clicks into a different terminal in the same already-focused window — the most common 'switch active terminal' case. Need to wire terminal.onFocus()/onBlur() from xterm.js so per-terminal focus changes also send client_focus to the server, triggering primary promotion + tmux resize.
close_reason: Wired per-terminal focus via xterm textarea focus/blur — catches clicks between panels in same window. Merged from worktree-agent-a055186bf98f74fb4.

title: Garbled text persists during scroll on long sessions (visible tab)
description: Earlier fix (remote-dev-ljnc) handled visibility/DPR/context-loss cases but artifacts still appear in production when the tab stays visible throughout. Need a stronger atlas-refresh strategy: clear on user scroll (throttled), or on terminal buffer events, or some other reliable trigger that doesn't depend on tab visibility changes.
close_reason: Hybrid fix: clearTextureAtlas on scroll-up (throttled 2s) + onLineFeed every 5000 lines. Merged from worktree-agent-a3e9c69ae006f4463.

title: mobile/terminal: adversarial-review fixes for touch selection
description:
Adversarial review of the touch-interactions hook surfaced four high-confidence bugs:

1. Selection rows are viewport-relative but xterm.select() expects buffer-absolute rows. When user has scrolled scrollback, long-press on viewport row N selects buffer row N — usually off-screen content, not what the user touched. Fix: add terminal.buffer.active.viewportY to row computed from viewport coords for both start and end of selection.

2. Multi-touch landing during selection invalidates state but leaves the xterm visual selection painted. reset() doesn't call terminal.clearSelection(), so selection persists visually while mode flips to idle and other handlers (scroll, pinch) take over. Fix: when reset() runs while there's an active selection, also call terminal.clearSelection().

3. The Terminal.touch-scroll.test.ts 'ignores multi-touch' test constructs an event but never invokes handlers.handleTouchMove on it. Test passes trivially without exercising the bail-out path. Fix: actually invoke the handler.

4. handleTouchEnd resets state to idle whenever ANY finger lifts, even if other fingers are still down. A two-finger gesture where one finger lifts will then leave the remaining finger unable to scroll/tap/long-press because mode is idle. Fix: only reset when e.touches.length === 0; otherwise leave state and let the remaining gesture continue.
close_reason: Merged via PR #230.

title: mobile/terminal: selection-vs-scroll, tap not reaching TUI, tap doesn't deselect
description:
Follow-up bugs from PR #227:

1. Selection drag is fighting scroll. When long-press has activated selection mode, subsequent vertical finger movement also triggers the touch-scroll handler, so the user can't extend the selection up/down without the viewport scrolling. The interactions touchmove listener is currently passive — it can't preventDefault, and the scroll handler isn't aware of selection mode.

2. Tap doesn't reach the TUI as a click, AND doesn't scrollToBottom either. User reports neither path fires. Possible causes: (a) appOwnsMouse detection always reports true so scrollToBottom is gated off, (b) the synthesized mousedown/mouseup target lookup fails or the events don't reach xterm's actual handler, (c) the tap-detection thresholds reject real taps.

3. Tapping outside an active selection does not clear it. Standard mobile UX: tap clears any existing selection and does not fire the click that turn (or does, depending on platform — pick one).

Fix:
- Make the interactions touchmove listener {passive: false} so it can preventDefault when mode === selection. Coordinate with touch-scroll.ts: either share state through a mode flag, or have one handler run first and short-circuit the other.
- Investigate tap path. Verify dispatch target with a real browser. Verify appOwnsMouse logic. Add a fallback: if tap with no active selection, ALWAYS scrollToBottom in addition to dispatching the synthetic click — the click is harmless when mouse mode is off, and scrollToBottom should be the user's expected fallback.
- On tap with terminal.hasSelection() === true, call terminal.clearSelection() and skip the click synthesis. The next tap goes through normally.
close_reason: Merged via PR #228.

title: mobile: replace ansi-to-html <pre> output with xterm.js
description:
MobileSessionView renders WebSocket data via ansi-to-html appended to a growing <pre> block. This breaks all TUIs (Claude Code, vim, etc.) because cursor moves, line clears, and screen redraws stack instead of overwriting. Symptom: garbled overlapping characters in any agent session.

Fix: Render terminal output via TerminalWithKeyboard (the same xterm.js wrapper desktop uses). Keep MobileSessionView chrome (SessionStatusBar, banner, SmartKeyStrip, MobileInputBar, SessionMetadataSheet, AgentExitScreen, AuthErrorOverlay). Drop the ansi-to-html pipeline, output entries state, append-only rendering, and useViewportDimensions (xterm + FitAddon already handles dimensions).

Acceptance:
- Claude Code, vim, htop render correctly on mobile
- Pinch-to-zoom still adjusts font size and persists
- prefs.fontFamily / fontSize honored on mount
- SmartKeyStrip + MobileInputBar still drive sendInput
- Suspended/disconnected/reconnecting banners still show
close_reason: Merged via PR #224.

title: Mobile session view: terminal doesn't resize, ignores font preferences
description: MobileSessionView hardcodes MOBILE_COLS=120, MOBILE_ROWS=24 and never calls sendResize, so the PTY thinks the terminal is 120 cols regardless of phone viewport. Lines wrap visually mid-stream, breaking TUI layouts. Also fontFamily is hardcoded to JetBrainsMono Nerd Font Mono — desktop reads prefs.fontFamily from PreferencesContext (SessionManager.tsx:2137-2138). Mobile must do the same. Fix: (a) compute cols/rows from viewport width / charWidth(fontSize) and viewport height / lineHeight, (b) wire ResizeObserver + fontSize change to call sendResize, (c) read prefs.fontFamily and use it for the pre block style + measurement, (d) consider seeding initial fontSize from prefs.fontSize when no localStorage key set.
close_reason: Merged via PRs #222 and #223.

title: Mobile single-session view: terminal WebSocket URL not wired (stuck on Reconnecting)
description: MobileApp.tsx mounts MobileSessionView without passing wsUrl. The hook default (ws://localhost:3001) is wrong both on localhost (project default port is 6002) and on remote deploys (no localhost from phone). Desktop's SessionManager.tsx:222-232 computes the correct URL from window.location; mobile needs the same. Fix: extract a shared useTerminalWsUrl hook (or pass wsUrl through MobileApp) and wire it.
close_reason: Merged via PR #221; dev hot-reload confirmed mobile WebSocket connects.

title: Settings tab 'closes' after click-away then click on sidebar row (persists after #201/#202)
description: User reports: open Settings → navigate to another tab → click the 'Settings' row in the sidebar Global section → the Settings tab closes/disappears instead of re-activating. Fixed in #200 for the gear button, in #201 for prefs tabs, but the sidebar-row path still reproduces. handleSessionClick (SessionManager.tsx:1373) calls setActiveSession + maybeAutoFollowFolder but not setActiveView('terminal') — if activeView is 'chat' or similar, the tab appears gone. Or another close path is firing (onSessionClose / swipe / keyboard). Trace the actual path and fix.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #203 — setActiveView('terminal') on handleSessionClick

title: Prefs pages close on click-away-click-back (regression)
description: openProjectPrefsSession / openGroupPrefsSession / openSecretsSession lack the client-side fast-path guard that openSettingsSession / openRecordingsSession / openProfilesSession have (added in PR #200). Every click hits POST /api/sessions and races with the active↔suspended sync in SessionManager:521, producing 'Cannot resume from state active' errors and making the tab un-reopenable after click-away. Fix: add the same fast-path guard to the 3 non-singleton prefs openers.
close_reason: Closed

title: PR #183 review fixes
description: Consolidated findings from code-reviewer + Codex adversarial on PR #183. Must-fix before merge: agent startup command regression, client upsert-on-dedup, typeMetadataPatch null round-trip, singleton tab project pinning, Settings deep-link on reuse, PR body 100-cap, dedup race UNIQUE constraint, Settings Escape UX.
close_reason: All 8 review findings addressed. 689 tests passing, 0 typecheck/lint errors. Pushed as 712309b on PR #183.

title: A7: retire activeView="settings" branch in SessionManager
description:
User confirmed Settings becomes a tab. Remove the activeView="settings" conditional layout, settingsOpenCount remount trick, and dedicated settings render branch in SessionManager.tsx (lines 188, 1535-1755, 1815). Settings will be reached via a session with terminalType='settings' (B5).
Depends on: A0, A2.

title: A5: lifecycle delegation (resume/close/exit/restart) via server registry
description: CRITICAL per Codex review. Audit session-service.resumeSession (line 920), closeSession (line 1049), markAgentExited (line 768), markAgentRestarting (line 789) for hard-coded terminalType guards. Replace with server-registry lookups. Loop type currently falls through agent-only paths — fix while we're in there.

title: A0: split TerminalTypePlugin into server/client plugin + two registries
description: CRITICAL per Codex review. Split src/types/terminal-type.ts: TerminalTypeServerPlugin (createSession, onSessionExit, onSessionRestart, onSessionClose, validateInput, useTmux/shell config) — server-safe, no React imports. TerminalTypeClientPlugin (component: React.ComponentType<{session, props}>, icon, title) — client-only. Two separate registries. session-service imports only server registry. Rationale: plugins today mix Lucide icons + client modals with server config, which would bundle client code into server if we routed session-service through the combined registry.

title: A3: extend CreateSessionInput with generic typeMetadata
description: Add typeMetadata?: Record<string, unknown> to CreateSessionInput in src/types/session.ts. Route it through the POST /api/sessions handler and SessionService. Keep filePath as a typed alias for backward compatibility. Allow plugins to validate their own metadata shape via plugin.validateInput().
notes: REVISED per Codex: in addition to generic typeMetadata, add a first-class scope_key column to terminal_session (indexed, nullable). Server-side dedup in POST /api/sessions. Client-side dedup-by-list scan is unreliable.

title: A1: route session-service through plugin.createSession()
description: Refactor src/services/session-service.ts so createSession/resumeSession/closeSession delegate useTmux, shellCommand, and initial typeMetadata to plugin.createSession() from the registry. Delete the hard-coded terminalType string guards at lines 413, 921, 1050. Update shell/agent/file/browser plugins to return correct SessionConfig (shellCommand, useTmux, initial metadata). Add test coverage for plugin dispatch.
notes: CRITICAL per Codex review. Only after A0 is landed (server registry exists). session-service must not import client plugins.

title: A2: route SessionManager render through plugin.renderContent()
description: Refactor SessionManager.tsx lines 1593-1727 to look up the plugin via registry and call plugin.renderContent(session, props) instead of the file/loop/browser/shell switch. Keep the key={session.id} remount semantics. Update TerminalTypeRenderer.tsx similarly or delete if redundant.
notes: REVISED per Codex: do NOT call plugin.renderContent() — replace switch with a client-side component registry lookup (clientRegistry.get(type).component returns React.ComponentType). Hooks belong in components, not plugin methods. Remove renderContent() from plugin interface in A0 split.

title: Fix codex adversarial review findings from PR #180 branch
description: Three findings from /codex:adversarial-review on feat/sidebar-menu-updates: (1) Empty Permanently UI calls cleanup endpoint that only removes expired items; (2) DeleteProjectGroup force path leaks descendants because project.group_id changed to ON DELETE SET NULL; (3) RootContextMenu wraps entire tree, competing with row menus.
close_reason: All three codex findings fixed on feat/sidebar-menu-updates

title: Port SessionMetadataBar (branch / ahead-behind / PR / ports) into SessionRow
description: Legacy sidebar rendered a metadata chip bar under each session name showing: git branch name, ahead/behind counts, linked PR, allocated ports. Component still exists at src/components/session/SessionMetadataBar.tsx (unchanged, 82 lines) but no consumer in the new tree. Wire it into SessionRow.tsx beneath the session name div. Pipe through the same isCollapsed gate as legacy.
design: Files: src/components/session/project-tree/SessionRow.tsx, src/components/session/ProjectTreeSidebar.tsx. Render <SessionMetadataBar session={s} isCollapsed={props.isCollapsed} /> inside SessionRow after the name row. SessionRow's name div currently wraps in 'flex-1 min-w-0' — restructure to stack name + metadata bar vertically. Keep icons/right-side controls on the right.
close_reason: Landed in 23a2279

title: Align sidebar row badges in fixed vertical columns
description: Right-side badges on GroupRow + ProjectRow (PR count, issue count, changes dot, session count) currently float based on which are present, making numbers jump between rows. User wants consistent alignment. Fix: reserve fixed-width columns for each metric type (right-anchored). Empty slots render nothing but hold their width so numbers line up vertically across rows.
design: Files: src/components/session/project-tree/GroupRow.tsx, ProjectRow.tsx. Layout: flex container with fixed-width slots, e.g. 4 slots [PR | Issue | Changes | Sessions] each min-w-[16px], right-aligned text inside. If stats are null the slot is an empty spacer of the same width. Alternative: 2x3 grid section if preferred by user.
close_reason: Landed in ac44531

title: Move to Group… submenu on GroupContextMenu and ProjectContextMenu
description: Groups and projects lack a menu equivalent of the session's 'Move to Project…' submenu. Add one. For groups: list eligible groups (excluding self + all descendants via collectDescendantGroupIds; current parent disabled); 'Move to Root' stays as-is but could be folded into the submenu as a separate option. For projects: list all groups (current parent disabled); if mtv7.1 lands, add 'Move to Root' too.

Same items get added to the header + dropdown in Sidebar.tsx:276-312 alongside existing session actions. New Group triggers tree.createGroup({parentGroupId: null}); New Project triggers tree.createProject({groupId: null}) — requires mtv7.1 for the null groupId support.
design: Files: src/components/session/ProjectTreeSidebar.tsx (add onContextMenu on outer wrapper div when target===currentTarget), src/components/session/Sidebar.tsx (extend header + dropdown), new src/components/session/project-tree/RootContextMenu.tsx with dual-export pattern. Wire to creating state (existing inline-create flow).

description: Model change prerequisite for B1 (root-space menu 'New Project'). Currently projects must live in a group (groupId: string). Widen to string | null throughout: TypeScript domain entity + context type, POST /api/projects validation, moveProject to null handling, DnD drop-on-root for projects, ProjectTreeSidebar rendering of root-level projects (outside any group section), sortOrder keyed by groupId+root bucket.
design: Files: src/domain/entities/Project.ts, src/contexts/ProjectTreeContext.tsx, src/app/api/projects/route.ts, src/app/api/projects/[id]/move/route.ts, src/services/project-service.ts, src/db/schema.ts (projects.groupId → .onDelete cascade-compatible nullable), src/components/session/ProjectTreeSidebar.tsx (add root-level projects render block alongside root-level groups), useTreeDragDrop for drop-on-root.

title: Sidebar context menus — update for group/project paradigm
description: The ProjectTree context menus added in Phase D need updates for the new group/project paradigm. Gaps surfaced by audit: no root-level New Group/Project entry points, no Move to Group submenus, missing Collapse/Expand, stale 'folder' naming in sidebar plumbing, trash affordances dropped in Phase G. Parent issue; children track individual items (BLOCKING + SHOULD).
close_reason: All BLOCKING + SHOULD sidebar menu updates complete (mtv7.1–.8)

title: Couple projects strictly to folders
description: Major refactor: strictly couple folder and project. Parent folders become groupings only; child folders are projects. Remove decoupled project abstraction.
design: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-project-folder-refactor-master.md — master plan with all shared decisions (naming, hierarchy, ownership matrix, groups-as-active rollup, migration strategy, risk register).
notes:
DECISIONS (2026-04-20):
- Hierarchy: Groups nestable (any depth), Projects are leaves only
- Ownership: Groups hold preferences only. Projects own sessions, tasks, channels, secrets, git, profile, account binding
- Migration: Auto-classify by depth + contents. Leaf folders -> projects; non-leaf -> groups. Sessions on group-like folders move to auto-created 'Default' project
- Naming: Full rename. New tables project_group + project. Types Project/Group. APIs /api/projects + /api/groups. FK renames (folderId -> projectId on task/channel/etc)
Master plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-project-folder-refactor-master.md (updated with codex review findings: forward-only cutover policy, Section 6a column appendix, 8-risk register, phase-specific integration tests)
close_reason: All 6 phases complete: domain+application (NodeRef/ProjectGroup/Project), services+API (repos, group-service, project-service, node-preferences routes), UI (ProjectTreeContext, GroupPreferencesModal, ProjectPreferencesModal, sidebar), rdv CLI (group/project subcommands), cleanup (legacy tables dropped, folder_id removed, docs refreshed). typecheck clean (baseline); 314/315 tests pass; cargo build + 11 rdv CLI tests green.

title: Epic: Clean Architecture Tmux Environment & Profile Refactor
description: Refactor tmux session creation to use native set-environment for persistent environment variables. This is an architectural refactor using clean architecture patterns with domain entities, value objects, and proper separation of concerns.

All 277 tests pass.

CREATE TABLE agent_profile (

  description TEXT,
  icon TEXT,
  color TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP,

);

  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  profile_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agent_profile(id),
  agent_type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- claude, gemini, opencode, codex

  created_at TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP,
  UNIQUE(profile_id, agent_type)
);

title: Lightweight session work-context (auto) + read-only beads join

Explicitly NOT a new task tracker (beads owns task state) and NOT a heavy presence board. Foundation for the start digest (x386.12) and collision nudges (x386.14). Shares underlying branch/worktree/status capture with the E5 session-tree metadata (related to remote-dev-n6uc).

title: Port proxy for k3s instances + port-management completion
description: Two tracks. Track A (control plane): complete the half-built port-management subsystem — runtime portClaims table, wire the dead PortMonitor via DI, session-lifecycle port claims, live isActive in GET /api/ports, a GET /api/ports/proxyable seam, runtime conflict-on-save, interactive port chips, mobile PortsScreen. Track B (data plane): HTTP+WS port proxy so https://rdv.joyful.house/<slug>/proxy/<port>/ serves an in-pod port. Architecture: HYBRID IN-POD — HTTP via an instance Next.js route to 127.0.0.1:port (zero router change; router already forwards /<slug>/* to :6001), WS via one router decideRoute rule (/<slug>/proxy/<port> upgrades → :6002) + an in-pod terminal-server bridge to ws://127.0.0.1:port. Path-based URLs; allowlist (block 6001/6002/privileged; proxyable = listening ∪ claimed) + owner-scoped via the instance's existing auth; new-tab Open UX. Plan: docs/claude/plans/2026-06-03-port-proxy-and-port-management.md. Related: remote-dev-jvcx (k3s supervisor).
