title: [vault] Survive host/pod restart
description: On session recreate after tmux itself dies (host/k8s pod restart), auto-inject resume from the stored native session id so the agent conversation returns. Directly addresses the documented restart-pain incidents.

title: [vault] Persist resume intent durably
description: Persist agentFlags/resume binding so the terminal server can relaunch resumed (not fresh) after its own restart. Strip sensitive env from the stored binding (cmux Vault strips secrets).

title: [vault] Survive terminal-server restart
description: On terminal-server restart + tmux reattach, if the agent process is gone, relaunch it with resume from stored state instead of leaving an empty pane or starting fresh.

title: [auto] Tests + docs: schedules, triggers, Crown, warm pool
description: Tests for agentSchedules, webhook trigger executor + dedupe/supersede, Crown fan-out/judge/auto-PR, and warm-pool claim/GC; plus docs in docs/.

title: [auto] Supervisor agent-launch API (per-instance)
description: Add a supervisor endpoint to trigger an agent on a specific instance slug (e.g. POST instances/<slug>/session forwarded to the instance API). Today the router only proxies already-existing paths.

title: [auto] Warm pool for agent-run environments
description: Pre-provision pods/instances from the golden image, prewarm on demand (e.g. when a task is being composed), claim-ready at launch, with TTL-based GC. Maps manaflow warmPool.ts onto k3s.

title: [auto] Crown best-of-N fan-out
description: Extend rdv teams to run the same prompt across N worktree branches concurrently and collect each branch git diff (filtering lockfiles/build artifacts). Foundation for run-and-compare.

title: [auto] Crown judge + auto-PR winner
description: LLM judge scores the N diffs on correctness/completeness/meaningful-change -> {winner, reason}; store crownReason; auto-open the winner PR; allow manual override. Mirrors manaflow crown/actions.ts + crown/pullRequest.ts.

title: [auto] Golden dev-env image (all 5 agents + toolchains)
description: Prebuilt OCI image with all 5 agent CLIs, language toolchains, sudo/apt, and auto-update, so agent-run envs and supervisor instances boot ready in seconds instead of cold installs. Serves both this epic and remote-dev-jvcx instance provisioning.

title: [auto] GitHub event webhook endpoint (HMAC)
description: HMAC-verified GitHub webhook endpoint (model on src/app/api/deploy/route.ts) that receives pull_request/issues/check events, verifies signature, and dedupes by delivery id. Replaces the current poll-only GitHub integration for triggers.

title: [auto] GitHub trigger config (model + UI)
description: Config model + UI to define triggers: on PR labeled X / issue opened / CI failed -> launch agent with template T on a worktree or instance. Stores repo+event->template mappings.

title: [auto] Triggered-run executor + state machine
description: Executor that provisions a worktree/instance, launches the agent, and posts results (PR comment/status). Run state machine pending->running->completed/failed/superseded, deduped per head-SHA (manaflow Preview pattern).

title: [auto] agentSchedules: cron + prompt -> real agent launch
description: New agentSchedules (schema + service + orchestrator) that, at a scheduled time, CREATES a session and launches an agent with a prompt template. Distinct from the existing keystroke-only sessionSchedules (src/services/schedule-service.ts). e.g. run a nightly security audit agent.

title: [chat] Tests: delivery reliability, subscription, lifecycle posting
description: Tests: ack/replay delivery, MCP subscription + reconnect replay, channel-subscription routing, auto work-context capture + read-only beads join, read-peers start digest, gotcha-note broadcast, check-in/check-out posting, and overlap/collision detection.

title: [chat] Channel subscription model for agents
description: Add a channel_subscription concept so an agent can subscribe to a channel and have messages auto-delivered, instead of only direct/broadcast toSessionId routing. Update peer-service/channel-service routing.

title: Check-in / check-out via lifecycle hooks (agent as system speaker)
description: Lifecycle hooks post to a per-project agent-coordination channel (auto-create e.g. #agents) as a first-class agent speaker: a CHECK-IN on session start (auto-context: branch, worktree, folder, claimed bd issue) and a CHECK-OUT + short summary on Stop/done. Auto-context is system-filled (depends on x386.11); the done-summary is agent-supplied. This is the discipline that makes peers aware when an agent starts and finishes work in the folder. Reuses the lifecycle hooks in crates/rdv/src/commands/hook.rs and channel-service.

title: [chat] @mention -> reliable delivery + injected tool message
description: Wire existing @mention resolution to the subscription path so mentioning an agent reliably delivers (and, for subscribed agents, surfaces as an MCP tool message) instead of waiting for the next poll.

title: [chat] Agent-facing chat usage docs + profile guidance
description: Document the agent coordination discipline in docs/AGENTS.md, docs/RDV_CLI.md, and the generated per-profile CLAUDE.md: on start, read the peer digest + unread gotchas; report gotchas/heads-ups as you go; check out with a short summary when done; rely on BEADS for task state (chat is for in-flight awareness, not task tracking). Explain when/why to use each command. Agents underuse chat partly because nothing tells them the norm.

title: [chat] Poll-fallback for non-MCP providers
description: Codex/Gemini/OpenCode/Antigravity have no MCP. Keep rdv peer messages reliable and hook-driven with a persisted since-cursor, and document the cadence, so chat is not broken for 4 of 5 providers while MCP subscription serves Claude.

title: [notif] Tests: classification, coalescing, focus-suppression, liveness
description: Unit/integration tests for severity classification, coalescing + clear-boundary, focus-aware suppression, push gating, and the PID-liveness sweep.

title: [notif] Gate FCM push by severity + per-type opt-out
description: In the push path of src/services/notification-service.ts, only push actionable/error severities by default and honor per-type push opt-out, so passive completions never reach mobile/web push.

title: [notif] Richer notification payload + client routing
description: Add duration, result summary, and a deep-link/CTA to the notification payload (DB + FCM + in-app) so a notification routes to the relevant session and shows context-aware actions instead of a generic title/body.

title: [notif] PID-liveness reconciliation sweep
description: Add a periodic process-alive check (kill(pid,0) equivalent) in the terminal server to clear stale running/waiting states whose agent died without a clean hook (crash/SIGKILL), and emit one 'exited unexpectedly/stuck' signal. cmux uses a 30s sweep; this is a better signal than a noisy always-on completion notice.

title: [notif] Focus-aware suppression
description: Skip OS/push notification when the user is actively viewing that session; flash/in-app only and auto-mark-read. Needs a client active-session signal plumbed to the broadcast path in src/server/terminal.ts. cmux gates on appFocused + focusedPanel.

title: [notif] Real coalescing + clear-boundary
description: Replace the weak 5s debounce in src/services/notification-service.ts with per-(session,type) coalescing that collapses completion cascades and drops pending alerts once the user opens that session (cmux generation/clear-boundary pattern).

title: [notif] User preferences: per-type + per-session muting + quiet hours
description: Extend userSettings beyond the global notificationsEnabled toggle: per-type suppression, per-session mute, and quiet-hours/DND windows, enforced in the delivery path and editable in Settings.

title: [notif] Distinguish passive Stop from actionable Notification/PermissionRequest
description: Map Claude Stop hook to a passive idle status (no push) and Notification/PermissionRequest to an actionable, push-worthy needs-input state, mirroring cmux. Today both collapse into one signal. Update hook handlers + status pill rendering.

title: Centralized model-key proxy
description: Agents currently hold real provider API keys in their profile env. Adopt manaflow's broker model: agents use a placeholder key + a per-session token, and a proxy injects the real provider key and forwards via a gateway, giving central billing, caching, rate-limiting, usage/cost observability, and removing secrets from agent envs. High value for multi-tenant supervisor instances.

title: Agent session durability and resume (Vault)
description: Verified gap: tmux keeps the pane but the agent CONVERSATION is lost on agent process death / terminal-server restart / pod restart. We capture Claude session id in typeMetadata.claudeSessionId and pass --resume only on manual new-session creation, but RestartAgentUseCase.ts:145 relaunches bare claude with NO flags (also agent-plugin-server.ts onSessionRestart passes empty flags), and only Claude is captured. Wire the existing resume into the restart/recreate path and extend native-session-id capture+resume to all 5 providers, with a declarative per-provider resume registry.

title: Agent automation and orchestration platform
description: Take automation to the next level on the supervisor+k3s base. Today sessionSchedules send keystrokes (not agent launches), GitHub is polled (no webhook triggers), there is no best-of-N, no warm pool, and agents launch cold. Build a manaflow-style platform: scheduled agent runs (cron+prompt), GitHub-webhook-triggered agent runs, Crown best-of-N run-and-compare with an LLM judge and auto-PR, a golden dev-env image, warm pools, scale-to-zero, a supervisor agent-launch API, and cross-instance delegation. Infra pieces cross-link to epic remote-dev-jvcx rather than duplicate it.
design:
# Agent Automation & Orchestration Platform — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers.** Execute this with `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` — one subagent per `### Task` below, each in its own git worktree (`./scripts/worktree-warm.sh`), per-step TDD added at execution time (`superpowers:test-driven-development`). The main agent coordinates and merges; it does not edit `src/`, `apps/`, `crates/`, or `deploy/` directly (CLAUDE.md worktree rule).

**Goal:** Take automation to the next level on the supervisor+k3s base — scheduled *agent runs* (cron+prompt, not keystrokes), GitHub-webhook-triggered runs, Crown best-of-N run-and-compare with an LLM judge + auto-PR, a golden dev-env image, warm pools, scale-to-zero, a supervisor agent-launch API, and cross-instance delegation. Epic **remote-dev-oyej** (12 children). Infra pieces (.7/.8/.9) **cross-link** epic **remote-dev-jvcx** (supervisor lifecycle) — they extend it, do not duplicate it.

**Architecture:** Three tiers. (1) **Main app** (Next.js + terminal server): new `agentSchedules`/`agentRuns` tables + an `AgentRunService` that performs a *real* non-interactive agent launch by POSTing the same `CreateSessionInput` (`terminalType:"agent"`, `autoLaunchAgent:true`, `agentFlags`) the existing session-create path consumes; a `SchedulerOrchestrator`-style cron loop and an HMAC GitHub-event webhook (mirroring `/api/deploy`) drive those launches; Crown extends the `rdv teams` fan-out with a diff collector + LLM judge + `gh pr create`. (2) **Supervisor** (`apps/supervisor`): a per-instance agent-launch API that proxies into an instance's data plane, a `warm_pool` table + reconciler arm that pre-provisions/claims/GCs envs, a scale-to-zero reaper, and a cross-instance delegation API. (3) **Image/k8s** (`deploy/k8s/supervisor`, root `Dockerfile`): the golden dev-env image (already bakes 5 agents + sudo/apt + gh — formalized + auto-update) and a reaper CronJob.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Next.js 16 / React 19, Drizzle ORM + libsql (main app `src/db/schema.ts`; supervisor `apps/supervisor/src/db/schema.ts`), croner, Vitest, `@kubernetes/client-node` (supervisor), Rust + clap (`crates/rdv`), Bun. Server logging is `createLogger` from `@/lib/logger` **only** — never `console.*`.

---

## Build Sequence

Dependency order from the bead graph (`oyej.4` deps `.3,.1`; `.3` deps `.2`; `.6` deps `.5`; `.8` deps `.7`; `.11` deps `.10`):

- **Group A — Schedules + Triggers** (sequential within, parallel-safe vs B/C/D after schema lands):

  3. **oyej.3** trigger config (model + UI) — *after* `.2`.
  4. **oyej.4** triggered-run executor + state machine + per-head-SHA dedupe — *after* `.3` and `.1`.
- **Group B — Crown** (parallel to A after the shared run scaffolding; B reuses `AgentRunService` only loosely — it has its own tables):
  5. **oyej.5** Crown fan-out (extends `rdv teams`).
  6. **oyej.6** Crown judge + auto-PR — *after* `.5`.
- **Group C — Infra / warm pool** (jvcx-adjacent; parallel to A/B):
  7. **oyej.7** golden dev-env image.
  8. **oyej.8** warm pool — *after* `.7`.
  9. **oyej.9** scale-to-zero + reaper CronJob.
- **Group D — Supervisor APIs** (parallel to A/B/C):

- **Group E — Tests + docs:** **oyej.12** lands incrementally with each task's TDD; the doc pages + changelog are written last.

## Group A — Schedules & Triggers

### Task — agentSchedules: cron + prompt → real agent launch
**Bead:** remote-dev-oyej.1
**Files:**
- Create: `src/types/agent-run.ts`, `src/services/agent-run-service.ts`, `src/services/agent-schedule-service.ts`, `src/services/agent-scheduler-orchestrator.ts`, `src/app/api/agent-schedules/route.ts`, `src/app/api/agent-schedules/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/agent-runs/route.ts`
- Modify: `src/db/schema.ts`, `src/lib/scheduler-client.ts`, `src/server/scheduler-control.ts`, `src/server/index.ts`
- Test: `src/services/__tests__/agent-run-service.test.ts`, `src/services/__tests__/agent-schedule-service.test.ts`

**Why a new table (not `sessionSchedules`):** `sessionSchedules`+`scheduleCommands` send **keystrokes via tmux** to an *existing* session (fire-and-forget; "success" only means the keystrokes were sent — see the EXECUTION MODEL block at `src/services/schedule-service.ts:556-585`). An agent *run* must **create a fresh session** with `terminalType:"agent"`, `autoLaunchAgent:true`, an optional worktree, and a prompt delivered to the agent. Distinct lifecycle → distinct tables.

**Steps:**
1. **Schema** (`src/db/schema.ts`, after the scheduled-commands block ~line 831). Reuse the column conventions verified in `sessionSchedules` (`scheduleType`, `cronExpression`, `timezone`, `enabled`, `status`, `maxRetries`, `nextRunAt`, `consecutiveFailures`):

```ts
export type AgentRunStatus =
  | "pending" | "running" | "completed" | "failed" | "superseded";

/** Scheduled REAL agent launches (distinct from keystroke-only sessionSchedules). */
export const agentSchedules = sqliteTable(
  "agent_schedule",
  {
    id: text("id").primaryKey().$defaultFn(() => crypto.randomUUID()),
    userId: text("user_id").notNull().references(() => users.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    projectId: text("project_id").notNull().references(() => projects.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    name: text("name").notNull(),
    // Launch template
    agentProvider: text("agent_provider").notNull().default("claude"),
    agentFlags: text("agent_flags").notNull().default("[]"), // JSON string[]
    prompt: text("prompt").notNull(),                         // delivered to the agent after launch
    worktreeType: text("worktree_type"),                      // null => no worktree
    baseBranch: text("base_branch"),
    // Cron (mirrors sessionSchedules)
    scheduleType: text("schedule_type").$type<ScheduleType>().notNull().default("recurring"),
    cronExpression: text("cron_expression"),
    scheduledAt: integer("scheduled_at", { mode: "timestamp_ms" }),
    timezone: text("timezone").notNull().default("America/Los_Angeles"),
    enabled: integer("enabled", { mode: "boolean" }).notNull().default(true),
    status: text("status").$type<ScheduleStatus>().notNull().default("active"),
    maxRetries: integer("max_retries").notNull().default(0),
    nextRunAt: integer("next_run_at", { mode: "timestamp_ms" }),
    lastRunAt: integer("last_run_at", { mode: "timestamp_ms" }),
    consecutiveFailures: integer("consecutive_failures").notNull().default(0),
    createdAt: integer("created_at", { mode: "timestamp_ms" }).notNull().$defaultFn(() => new Date()),
    updatedAt: integer("updated_at", { mode: "timestamp_ms" }).notNull().$defaultFn(() => new Date()),
  },
  (t) => [
    index("agent_schedule_user_idx").on(t.userId),
    index("agent_schedule_next_run_idx").on(t.enabled, t.nextRunAt),
  ],
);

/** One agent-run instance, regardless of trigger source (schedule | webhook | manual | crown). */
export const agentRuns = sqliteTable(
  "agent_run",
  {
    id: text("id").primaryKey().$defaultFn(() => crypto.randomUUID()),
    userId: text("user_id").notNull().references(() => users.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    projectId: text("project_id").notNull().references(() => projects.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    // Provenance — exactly one of these is set (others null).
    scheduleId: text("schedule_id").references(() => agentSchedules.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
    triggerConfigId: text("trigger_config_id").references(() => triggerConfigs.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
    source: text("source").$type<"schedule" | "trigger" | "manual" | "crown">().notNull(),
    // Launch params snapshot
    agentProvider: text("agent_provider").notNull(),
    agentFlags: text("agent_flags").notNull().default("[]"),
    prompt: text("prompt").notNull(),
    // The session this run created (null until launched).
    sessionId: text("session_id").references(() => terminalSessions.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
    // Dedupe key for trigger runs: the GitHub head SHA (null for schedule/manual).
    headSha: text("head_sha"),
    status: text("status").$type<AgentRunStatus>().notNull().default("pending"),
    errorMessage: text("error_message"),
    startedAt: integer("started_at", { mode: "timestamp_ms" }),
    completedAt: integer("completed_at", { mode: "timestamp_ms" }),
    createdAt: integer("created_at", { mode: "timestamp_ms" }).notNull().$defaultFn(() => new Date()),
  },
  (t) => [
    index("agent_run_user_idx").on(t.userId),
    index("agent_run_schedule_idx").on(t.scheduleId),
    index("agent_run_trigger_idx").on(t.triggerConfigId),
    index("agent_run_status_idx").on(t.status),
    // Per-(triggerConfig, headSha) dedupe — see TriggerService.
    uniqueIndex("agent_run_trigger_head_idx").on(t.triggerConfigId, t.headSha),
  ],
);

export type AgentScheduleRow = typeof agentSchedules.$inferSelect;
export type AgentRunRow = typeof agentRuns.$inferSelect;
```
> The `agent_run_trigger_head_idx` unique index is **nullable-composite**: in libsql/SQLite, rows where `headSha IS NULL` (schedule/manual runs) do **not** collide (NULLs are distinct), so the index only enforces dedupe for trigger runs. This is the per-head-SHA dedupe primitive `.4` relies on. Run `bun run db:push`.

2. **`agent-run-service.ts` — the real launch + state machine.** The launch reuses the **existing** session-create path (verified at `src/services/session-service.ts:483-497` + `src/app/api/sessions/route.ts:54-151`), so the agent CLI is started exactly as an interactive agent session, then the prompt is sent:

```ts
import { createLogger } from "@/lib/logger";
import { db } from "@/db";
import { agentRuns, type AgentRunRow } from "@/db/schema";
import * as SessionService from "./session-service";
import * as TmuxService from "./tmux-service";
import { eq } from "drizzle-orm";

const log = createLogger("AgentRun");

export interface LaunchAgentRunInput {
  userId: string;
  projectId: string;
  source: AgentRunRow["source"];
  agentProvider: string;
  agentFlags: string[];
  prompt: string;
  worktreeType?: string | null;
  baseBranch?: string | null;
  scheduleId?: string | null;
  triggerConfigId?: string | null;
  headSha?: string | null;
}

/** pending → running → completed|failed (and superseded by a newer same-key run). */
export async function launchAgentRun(input: LaunchAgentRunInput): Promise<AgentRunRow> {
  const [run] = await db.insert(agentRuns).values({
    userId: input.userId, projectId: input.projectId, source: input.source,
    agentProvider: input.agentProvider, agentFlags: JSON.stringify(input.agentFlags),
    prompt: input.prompt, scheduleId: input.scheduleId ?? null,
    triggerConfigId: input.triggerConfigId ?? null, headSha: input.headSha ?? null,
    status: "pending",
  }).returning();

  try {
    // REAL launch — same input the interactive create path consumes.
    const { session } = await SessionService.createSessionWithDedupFlag(input.userId, {
      name: `run/${input.source}/${run.id.slice(0, 8)}`,
      projectId: input.projectId,
      terminalType: "agent",
      agentProvider: input.agentProvider as never,
      autoLaunchAgent: true,
      agentFlags: input.agentFlags,
      createWorktree: !!input.worktreeType,
      worktreeType: input.worktreeType as never,
      baseBranch: input.baseBranch ?? undefined,
    });

    // Deliver the prompt once the agent CLI is up. tmux sendKeys uses \r (NOT \n)
    // — the Claude/Codex TUI requires \r to submit (see project memory).
    await waitForPaneReady(session.tmuxSessionName);
    await TmuxService.sendKeys(session.tmuxSessionName, input.prompt, true);

    const [running] = await db.update(agentRuns)
      .set({ status: "running", sessionId: session.id, startedAt: new Date() })
      .where(eq(agentRuns.id, run.id)).returning();
    log.info("agent run launched", { runId: run.id, sessionId: session.id, source: input.source });
    return running;
  } catch (err) {
    await db.update(agentRuns)
      .set({ status: "failed", errorMessage: String(err), completedAt: new Date() })
      .where(eq(agentRuns.id, run.id));
    log.error("agent run launch failed", { runId: run.id, error: String(err) });
    throw err;
  }
}

/** Mark older non-terminal runs with the same dedupe key superseded (per-head-SHA). */
export async function supersedePriorRuns(triggerConfigId: string, headSha: string, keepRunId: string): Promise<number> { /* UPDATE … status='superseded' WHERE triggerConfigId=? AND headSha=? AND id<>? AND status IN ('pending','running') */ }
```
`waitForPaneReady` polls `TmuxService.capturePane` for a quiescent prompt before sending the prompt — this is the same pane-quiescent guard the project added in `createSession` (memory: `startup_cmd_first_char` — a shell-init prompt ate the first keystroke). Do **not** skip it.

3. **`agent-schedule-service.ts`** — CRUD; import `validateCronExpression`/`calculateNextRun` from `schedule-service.ts` (already exported, verified `:54-81`). On create with `enabled`, compute `nextRunAt`. Validate the agent provider against `agent-providers` and reject unknown providers.

4. **`agent-scheduler-orchestrator.ts`** — clone the shape of `scheduler-orchestrator.ts` (`Map<id, Cron>`, `start/stop/addJob/removeJob/updateJob`), but the cron callback calls `AgentRunService.launchAgentRun()` instead of `executeSchedule`. Export a singleton `agentSchedulerOrchestrator`. Start it in `src/server/index.ts` next to `schedulerOrchestrator.start()`.

5. **API routes** — `withApiAuth` (dual session/API-key, verified `src/lib/api.ts:95-137`). `POST /api/agent-schedules` validates + `notifyAgentScheduleCreated(id)` fire-and-forget (mirror `src/app/api/schedules/route.ts:73-77`). `POST /api/agent-runs` does an immediate `launchAgentRun({source:"manual"})`.

**Test command:** `bun run test:run src/services/__tests__/agent-run-service.test.ts src/services/__tests__/agent-schedule-service.test.ts`
**Expected:** all pass. Cases: launch inserts `pending`→`running`, sets `sessionId`; launch failure → `failed` with `errorMessage`; `supersedePriorRuns` flips only matching non-terminal rows; cron validation rejects bad expressions; `nextRunAt` computed for recurring. Mock `SessionService.createSessionWithDedupFlag` + `TmuxService` (no real tmux). Then `bun run lint && bun run typecheck`.

### Task — GitHub event webhook endpoint (HMAC)
**Bead:** remote-dev-oyej.2
**Files:**
- Create: `src/app/api/webhooks/github/route.ts`, `src/lib/github-webhook-auth.ts`
- Test: `src/app/api/webhooks/github/__tests__/route.test.ts`

**Steps:**
1. **`github-webhook-auth.ts`** — reuse the existing constant-time verifier (verified `src/lib/deploy-webhook-auth.ts:11-22`); add a typed event parser:

```ts
export { verifySignature } from "@/lib/deploy-webhook-auth";

export interface GithubEvent {
  event: string;                 // X-GitHub-Event: pull_request | issues | check_suite | …
  action?: string;               // labeled | opened | completed | …
  repoFullName?: string;         // owner/name
  headSha?: string;              // PR head sha / check_suite head_sha (dedupe key)
  prNumber?: number;
  issueNumber?: number;
  labels: string[];
  conclusion?: string;           // check_suite/check_run: success | failure | …
}

export function parseGithubEvent(headers: Headers, body: unknown): GithubEvent { /* read X-GitHub-Event + body shape; normalize labels[]; pull head sha from pull_request.head.sha | check_suite.head_sha */ }
```

2. **`route.ts`** — structurally mirror `/api/deploy` (verified `src/app/api/deploy/route.ts:40-170`): raw-body read **before** JSON parse, HMAC over the raw bytes, env-secret gate. Hand the parsed event to `TriggerService.handleEvent` (oyej.3). Always answer fast (202) — dispatch is async/detached so GitHub's 10s delivery budget is never blocked.

```ts
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { createLogger } from "@/lib/logger";
import { verifySignature, parseGithubEvent } from "@/lib/github-webhook-auth";
import * as TriggerService from "@/services/trigger-service";

const log = createLogger("api/webhooks/github");

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  const secret = process.env.GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET;
  if (!secret) { log.error("GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET not configured"); return NextResponse.json({ error: "Webhooks not configured" }, { status: 503 }); }

  const rawBody = Buffer.from(await request.arrayBuffer());
  const signature = request.headers.get("x-hub-signature-256") ?? "";
  if (!verifySignature(secret, rawBody, signature)) {
    log.warn("Invalid webhook signature", { ip: request.headers.get("x-forwarded-for") ?? "unknown" });
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Invalid signature" }, { status: 401 });
  }

  const eventName = request.headers.get("x-github-event") ?? "";
  if (eventName === "ping") return NextResponse.json({ message: "pong" });

  let body: unknown;
  try { body = JSON.parse(rawBody.toString("utf-8")); }
  catch { return NextResponse.json({ error: "Invalid JSON body" }, { status: 400 }); }

  const event = parseGithubEvent(request.headers, body);
  // Fire-and-forget so GitHub's delivery isn't blocked on agent launch.
  void TriggerService.handleEvent(event).catch((err) =>
    log.error("trigger dispatch failed", { error: String(err), event: event.event, action: event.action }));

  return NextResponse.json({ message: "accepted", event: event.event, action: event.action ?? null }, { status: 202 });
}
```

**Test command:** `bun run test:run src/app/api/webhooks/github/__tests__/route.test.ts`
**Expected:** valid sig + `pull_request`/`labeled` → 202 and `TriggerService.handleEvent` called once (mocked); tampered body → 401; missing secret → 503; `ping` → 200 `pong`; non-JSON → 400. Use a known secret + `crypto.createHmac("sha256", secret).update(raw).digest("hex")` to build a valid header.

### Task — Triggered-run executor + state machine (per-head-SHA dedupe)
**Bead:** remote-dev-oyej.4 — **depends:** oyej.3, oyej.1
**Files:**
- Create: `src/services/trigger-service.ts`
- Modify: `src/services/agent-run-service.ts` (use `supersedePriorRuns`)
- Test: `src/services/__tests__/trigger-service.test.ts`

**Steps:**
1. **`trigger-service.ts` — match → dedupe → dispatch:**

```ts
import { createLogger } from "@/lib/logger";
import { db } from "@/db";
import { triggerConfigs, triggerEvents, githubRepositories, type TriggerConfigRow } from "@/db/schema";
import { and, eq } from "drizzle-orm";
import * as AgentRunService from "./agent-run-service";
import type { GithubEvent } from "@/lib/github-webhook-auth";

const log = createLogger("Trigger");

const KIND_FOR: Record<string, (e: GithubEvent) => boolean> = {
  pr_labeled: (e) => e.event === "pull_request" && e.action === "labeled",
  issue_opened: (e) => e.event === "issues" && e.action === "opened",
  ci_failed: (e) => e.event === "check_suite" && e.action === "completed" && e.conclusion === "failure",
};

function filterMatches(cfg: TriggerConfigRow, e: GithubEvent): boolean {
  const f = JSON.parse(cfg.filter) as { label?: string };
  if (cfg.kind === "pr_labeled" && f.label) return e.labels.includes(f.label);
  return true;
}

export async function handleEvent(e: GithubEvent): Promise<void> {
  // Resolve repo → enabled configs whose kind+filter match.
  const repo = e.repoFullName
    ? await db.query.githubRepositories.findFirst({ where: eq(githubRepositories.fullName, e.repoFullName) })
    : null;
  const configs = await db.select().from(triggerConfigs)
    .where(and(eq(triggerConfigs.enabled, true), repo ? eq(triggerConfigs.githubRepoId, repo.id) : undefined as never));

  for (const cfg of configs) {
    const matched = (KIND_FOR[cfg.kind]?.(e) ?? false) && filterMatches(cfg, e);
    if (!matched) { await record(cfg.id, e, false, null); continue; }

    // Per-head-SHA dedupe: the unique (triggerConfigId, headSha) index means a
    // duplicate delivery (GitHub redelivers) hits ON CONFLICT → we skip + supersede.
    try {
      const run = await AgentRunService.launchAgentRun({
        userId: cfg.userId, projectId: cfg.projectId, source: "trigger",
        triggerConfigId: cfg.id, headSha: e.headSha ?? null,
        agentProvider: cfg.agentProvider, agentFlags: JSON.parse(cfg.agentFlags),
        prompt: renderTemplate(cfg.promptTemplate, e), worktreeType: cfg.worktreeType,
      });
      if (e.headSha) await AgentRunService.supersedePriorRuns(cfg.id, e.headSha, run.id);
      await record(cfg.id, e, true, run.id);
    } catch (err) {
      if (/unique/i.test(String(err))) { log.info("duplicate trigger delivery ignored", { cfg: cfg.id, headSha: e.headSha }); await record(cfg.id, e, true, null); }
      else throw err;
    }
  }
}
```
`renderTemplate` substitutes `{{repo}}`/`{{prNumber}}`/`{{issueNumber}}` in `promptTemplate`. `record(...)` inserts a `triggerEvents` row.

**Test command:** `bun run test:run src/services/__tests__/trigger-service.test.ts`
**Expected:** `pr_labeled` with matching label dispatches one run + writes a matched `triggerEvents` row; wrong label → no dispatch, `matched:false`; same `headSha` redelivered → second `launchAgentRun` rejects on the unique index → caught, no second run; `ci_failed` only fires on `conclusion:"failure"`. Mock `AgentRunService` + DB. Then `bun run lint && bun run typecheck`.

### Task — Crown judge + auto-PR winner
**Bead:** remote-dev-oyej.6 — **depends:** oyej.5
**Files:**
- Create: `src/services/crown-judge.ts`, `src/app/api/crown/[id]/route.ts`

**Steps:**
1. **`crown-judge.ts` — LLM judge, routed through the model-key proxy.** The judge model call **must** route via the existing model-key proxy that agent sessions already use (`resolveProxyEnv` → `litellm-process-manager`/`litellm-service`, verified `src/services/session-service.ts:74-113`) — this is the realization of the aehq cross-link (`remote-dev-aehq`, "Centralized model-key proxy"). Resolve `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` + key from `litellm-service.getActiveDefaultModel(userId)`; if no model is configured, fall back deterministically (largest non-trivial diff wins) so Crown never hard-fails on missing keys.

```ts
export interface CrownJudgeResult { winner: string; reason: string; } // winner = candidateId

export async function judge(opts: {
  userId: string; prompt: string;
  candidates: { id: string; branch: string; diff: string }[];
  model?: string;
}): Promise<CrownJudgeResult> {
  const { resolveJudgeEndpoint } = await import("./crown-judge-endpoint"); // wraps litellm-service
  const endpoint = await resolveJudgeEndpoint(opts.userId, opts.model);
  if (!endpoint) return fallbackJudge(opts.candidates); // no model configured

  const system = "You are a senior engineer judging candidate patches for the SAME task. Reply ONLY as compact JSON {\"winner\":\"<candidateId>\",\"reason\":\"<one sentence>\"}.";
  const user = renderJudgePrompt(opts.prompt, opts.candidates); // task + each candidate's id + filtered diff
  const res = await fetch(`${endpoint.baseUrl}/v1/messages`, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "content-type": "application/json", "x-api-key": endpoint.apiKey, "anthropic-version": "2023-06-01" },
    body: JSON.stringify({ model: endpoint.model, max_tokens: 512, system, messages: [{ role: "user", content: user }] }),
  });
  return parseJudgeJson(await res.text(), opts.candidates); // tolerant parse; throws → fallbackJudge
}
```

2. **`crown-service.ts`** — after `waitForCandidates`: set `judging`, call `judge(...)`, store `winnerCandidateId` + `crownReason`, then **auto-PR the winner**: `gh pr create --head <winnerBranch> --base <base> --title … --body "<crownReason>"` via `execFile("gh", …)` (the runtime image already ships `gh` — verified `Dockerfile:205-210`). Store `prUrl`, set `completed`.
3. **`api/crown/[id]/route.ts`** — `GET` returns the run + candidates + diffs + judge result; `POST {action:"pr", candidateId}` is the **manual override** (open a PR for an operator-chosen candidate regardless of the judge).
4. **`crown pr <id> --candidate <cid>`** in `crown.rs` → the override POST.

### Task — Scale-to-zero + wake-on-traffic for idle agent envs
**Bead:** remote-dev-oyej.9 — **relates:** jvcx
**Files:**
- Create: `apps/supervisor/src/lib/idle-detector.ts`, `apps/supervisor/src/app/api/internal/reaper/route.ts`, `deploy/k8s/supervisor/reaper-cronjob.yaml`
- Modify: `apps/supervisor/src/controller/reconciler.ts`, `deploy/k8s/supervisor/supervisor.yaml`
- Test: extend `apps/supervisor/src/controller/__tests__/reconciler.test.ts`

**Steps:**
1. **`idle-detector.ts`** — `isInstanceIdle(row, nowMs)`: an instance is idle when it has no active terminal sessions and its last activity is older than `SUPERVISOR_AGENT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS`. Probe the instance data plane via `instance-proxy.ts` (oyej.10) `GET /<slug>/api/sessions?status=active`; treat an unreachable instance as **not idle** (never suspend on a failed probe — fail safe).
2. **Scale-to-zero** — in the reconciler's `ready` arm, when `isInstanceIdle` and the instance is a scale-to-zero candidate (a `warmPool`-claimed or labeled agent-run env), **request** `suspended`. jvcx's `reconcileSteadyState(...,0)` then scales the StatefulSet to 0 with the PVC retained (verified `reconciler.ts:556-619`). **Wake-on-traffic:** the supervisor agent-launch / delegation API (oyej.10/.11) requests `resume` (→ jvcx scales back to 1) before dispatching; the existing router blip on resume (~10-30s, 502/503) is **accepted** (same class as jvcx's §9 image-rollout blip — documented at `reconciler.ts:560-569`).
3. **`api/internal/reaper/route.ts`** — `POST` gated by `SUPERVISOR_REAPER_SECRET` (copy the `authorizeInternalRequest` pattern verified `apps/supervisor/src/app/api/internal/routes/route.ts:50-83`; refuse unauthenticated in prod). Runs idle-suspend + `warmPool.gcExpired` once and returns counts. This lets the suspend/GC sweep run on a schedule even if the 30s reconciler is conservative.
4. **`reaper-cronjob.yaml`** — k8s `CronJob` (every 5 min) that curls the reaper endpoint with the shared secret:
```yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: rdv-reaper
  namespace: rdv-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/5 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          restartPolicy: Never
          containers:
            - name: reaper
              image: curlimages/curl:latest   # auto-update tag (Docker policy)
              command: ["sh", "-c"]
              args:
                - >
                  curl -fsS -X POST
                  -H "x-supervisor-internal-secret: $REAPER_SECRET"
                  http://supervisor.rdv-system.svc.cluster.local:6003/api/internal/reaper
              env:
                - name: REAPER_SECRET
                  valueFrom:
                    secretKeyRef: { name: rdv-supervisor, key: reaper-secret }
```
5. **`supervisor.yaml`** — add `SUPERVISOR_AGENT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS` + `SUPERVISOR_REAPER_SECRET` (from the `rdv-supervisor` Secret).

**Test command:** `bun run test:run` (in `apps/supervisor`) targeting `reconciler.test.ts`; `kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f deploy/k8s/supervisor/reaper-cronjob.yaml`.
**Expected:** an idle `ready` agent-run instance is transitioned toward `suspended` (audited); a busy or unreachable instance is left `ready`; the manifest validates. Mock the idle probe.

## Group D — Supervisor APIs

### Task — Supervisor agent-launch API (per-instance slug)
**Bead:** remote-dev-oyej.10
**Files:**
- Create: `apps/supervisor/src/lib/instance-proxy.ts`, `apps/supervisor/src/app/api/instances/[id]/agent/route.ts`
- Test: `apps/supervisor/src/app/api/instances/[id]/agent/__tests__/route.test.ts`

**Steps:**
1. **`instance-proxy.ts`** — `instanceFetch(row, path, init)`: build `https://${SUPERVISOR_INSTANCE_HOST}/${row.slug}${path}` (the instance image is slug-aware, serving under `/<slug>` — verified by the router design `apps/supervisor-router/src/index.ts:1-13`), attach the instance's programmatic credential as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. The instance API key is provisioned per instance; resolve it from a supervisor-held secret/env (`SUPERVISOR_INSTANCE_API_KEY` for the homelab single-key model) and document the per-instance-key upgrade path.
2. **`api/instances/[id]/agent/route.ts`:**
```ts
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { eq } from "drizzle-orm";
import { db } from "@/db";
import { instance } from "@/db/schema";
import { withSupervisorAuth } from "@/lib/auth";
import { canManageInstance } from "@/lib/roles";
import { instanceFetch } from "@/lib/instance-proxy";
import { requestResume } from "@/lib/lifecycle-actions";

export const POST = withSupervisorAuth("operator", async (request, { user, params }) => {
  const row = await db.query.instance.findFirst({ where: eq(instance.id, params!.id) });
  if (!row || !canManageInstance(user, row)) return NextResponse.json({ error: "Not found", code: "NOT_FOUND" }, { status: 404 }); // 404 not 403 (owner-scope, matches existing routes)
  const body = await request.json() as { projectId: string; prompt: string; agentProvider?: string; agentFlags?: string[]; worktreeType?: string };
  if (row.status === "suspended") await requestResume(user, row.id);   // wake-on-traffic
  if (row.status !== "ready" && row.status !== "suspended")
    return NextResponse.json({ error: `instance not launchable (${row.status})`, code: "NOT_READY" }, { status: 409 });
  // Proxy into the instance's REAL agent-run launcher (oyej.1).
  const res = await instanceFetch(row, "/api/agent-runs", {
    method: "POST", headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
    body: JSON.stringify({ source: "manual", ...body }),
  });
  return NextResponse.json(await res.json(), { status: res.status });
});
```
Uses `withSupervisorAuth("operator")` + `canManageInstance` (owner-scope, 404 not 403 — verified in the existing instance routes) and `requestResume` (verified `apps/supervisor/src/lib/lifecycle-actions.ts`, used by `/resume`).

**Test command:** `bun run test:run` (in `apps/supervisor`) targeting the new route test.
**Expected:** operator launches → 202 with the proxied run handle; viewer → 403; non-owner → 404; suspended instance → `requestResume` called then proxied; terminating instance → 409. Mock `instanceFetch` + `lifecycle-actions`. Then `bun run lint && bun run typecheck` in `apps/supervisor`.

## Risks & Open Questions

1. **Model-call routing for the judge (aehq).** oyej.6's judge call routes through the **existing** model-key proxy (`litellm-process-manager`/`litellm-service` + `resolveProxyEnv`, verified `session-service.ts:74-113`) — the same plumbing agent sessions use for `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`/`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. This *is* the aehq cross-link (`remote-dev-aehq`). If aehq lands a dedicated server-side `model.complete(...)` port, swap `crown-judge-endpoint.ts` to it (isolated seam). **Decision needed:** is the judge billed to the run's owner's configured model (current plan) or a system judge key? Plan assumes owner's model with a deterministic fallback so Crown never hard-fails.
2. **Webhook + agent-launch API security.** The GitHub webhook reuses the **constant-time** HMAC verifier (`deploy-webhook-auth.ts`), refuses unconfigured (`503`) — same posture as `/api/deploy`. The supervisor agent-launch/delegation APIs are `withSupervisorAuth("operator")` + owner-scope (404-not-403). The reaper endpoint copies the `/api/internal/routes` shared-secret gate (refuse unauthenticated in prod). **Open:** per-instance API keys vs a single `SUPERVISOR_INSTANCE_API_KEY` for the instance proxy — plan ships the single-key homelab model with a documented per-instance upgrade path.
3. **jvcx overlap boundary.** oyej.7/.8/.9 **must not** re-implement provisioning, scaling, suspend/resume, or the state machine — they add a `warmPool` table, a reconciler arm, a reaper, and a golden flavor. Suspend/resume + StatefulSet scaling are jvcx's `reconcileSteadyState`/`lifecycle-actions`; scale-to-zero only *requests* `suspended`. **Risk:** a future jvcx refactor of `ReconcilerDeps` could break the warm-pool arm — mitigated by injecting through the same `ReconcilerDeps` interface and adding the arm's cases to the shared `reconciler.test.ts`.
4. **Cost of warm pools.** A pre-warmed instance holds a PVC + (until idle-suspended) a running pod = real spend. Mitigations baked in: `SUPERVISOR_WARM_POOL_SIZE` defaults to **0** (opt-in); `ttlExpiresAt` GCs unclaimed envs; scale-to-zero suspends idle claimed envs (PVC retained, pod gone). **Open:** should claimed-but-finished envs be torn down or returned to the pool? Plan tears down (simpler; warm-pool rows are single-use) — revisit if cold-start latency dominates.
5. **Run completion detection.** Like the keystroke scheduler, an agent run's "done" is inferred from the session's `agentExitState` (same signal `rdv teams wait` uses), not a real exit code — long-running agents may sit `running` until the TUI exits. Crown's `waitForCandidates` therefore needs a timeout (default 30 min) after which un-exited candidates are judged on their diff-so-far or marked `failed`. **Open:** expose the timeout per Crown run (plan: yes, `--timeout`).
6. **`headSha` dedupe + nullable unique index.** The per-(triggerConfigId, headSha) unique index relies on SQLite treating NULLs as distinct (so schedule/manual runs with `headSha IS NULL` never collide). Verified behavior for libsql/SQLite; the `trigger-service.test.ts` redelivery case locks it in.

---

## Self-Review (writing-plans)

**Coverage vs all 12 beads:** oyej.1 (agent schedules → real launch) ✓ · oyej.2 (HMAC webhook) ✓ · oyej.3 (trigger config + UI) ✓ · oyej.4 (executor + state machine + per-head-SHA dedupe) ✓ · oyej.5 (Crown fan-out) ✓ · oyej.6 (judge + auto-PR + override) ✓ · oyej.7 (golden image) ✓ · oyej.8 (warm pool) ✓ · oyej.9 (scale-to-zero + reaper CronJob) ✓ · oyej.10 (supervisor agent-launch API) ✓ · oyej.11 (cross-instance delegation) ✓ · oyej.12 (tests + docs) ✓. Dependency edges respected in Build Sequence (.4 after .3+.1; .3 after .2; .6 after .5; .8 after .7; .11 after .10).

**Placeholder scan:** No `TODO`/`FIXME`/`...`/`<placeholder>` in code sketches. Every `### Task` has explicit Create/Modify/Test paths, concrete steps, real Drizzle/handler/manifest code, and a real test command + expected result. The fenced bodies marked with `/* … */` (`supersedePriorRuns`, `parseGithubEvent`, `renderTemplate`/`record`, `crown-diff-collector` internals) are intentionally-elided **bodies of fully-specified signatures** whose behavior is pinned by the adjacent prose + the test's expected cases — not unspecified work.

**Type-name consistency (fixed inline during review):**
- Run status union is `AgentRunStatus = "pending"|"running"|"completed"|"failed"|"superseded"` everywhere (schema, `agent-run.ts`, service).
- Tables: `agentSchedules`/`agent_schedule`, `agentRuns`/`agent_run`, `triggerConfigs`/`trigger_config`, `triggerEvents`/`trigger_event`, `crownRuns`/`crown_run`, `crownCandidates`/`crown_candidate`, `warmPool`/`warm_pool` — camelCase export + snake_case SQL, matching the repo convention (`sessionSchedules`/`session_schedule`).
- Crown judge result is `CrownJudgeResult { winner: string /* candidateId */; reason: string }`; stored as `crownRuns.winnerCandidateId` + `crownRuns.crownReason` (the bead's `crownReason`).
- Env vars consistent: `GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, `SUPERVISOR_WARM_POOL_SIZE`, `SUPERVISOR_WARM_POOL_TTL_MS`, `SUPERVISOR_AGENT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS`, `SUPERVISOR_REAPER_SECRET`, `SUPERVISOR_INSTANCE_API_KEY`, `AGENT_AUTO_UPDATE`, `RDV_IMAGE_FLAVOR`.
- **Cross-app schema caveat (fixed):** `agentSchedules`/`agentRuns`/`triggerConfigs`/`triggerEvents`/`crownRuns`/`crownCandidates` live in the **main app** `src/db/schema.ts`; `warmPool` lives in **`apps/supervisor/src/db/schema.ts`** (separate Drizzle SQLite app). Each is pushed with its own `bun run db:push` in its own app root. The four interdependent main-app tables (FK edges agentRuns↔triggerConfigs↔triggerEvents) are pushed together to avoid FK-ordering failure — noted in oyej.1 and oyej.3.

title: Agent-native chat and coordination
description: Beads is the work TRACKER (tasks, claims, status, deps); chat is the in-flight AWARENESS layer beads does NOT hold. Agents in the same project/folder share gotchas and heads-ups as work proceeds ('refactoring auth, dont touch src/auth.ts'; 'build breaks if you bump X') and gain awareness of who is touching what. Today chat is a browser silo: agents only see messages via the PreToolUse poll (rdv peer messages --since) so idle agents never get them; src/server/mcp-push.ts is fire-and-forget; non-Claude providers have no MCP. Build: (1) reliable delivery = MCP subscription + durable inbox, with poll fallback; (2) lightweight AUTO work-context (branch/worktree/folder/status) with a READ-ONLY join to the agent's claimed bd issue -- NOT a re-implementation of task tracking and NOT a heavy presence board; (3) a check-in -> read-peers -> check-out discipline via lifecycle hooks posting to a per-project agent-coordination channel; (4) gotcha/heads-up/progress notes; (5) overlap/collision nudges when two active agents share a branch/worktree/bd-issue.

title: Notifications: signal-vs-noise overhaul
description: Agent-lifecycle notifications are too noisy. crates/rdv/src/commands/hook.rs handle_stop fires an agent_exited 'Session ended normally' push on every clean stop, with only a 5s (user,session,type) debounce in src/services/notification-service.ts, no severity, no muting, no quiet hours, no focus-awareness, and it broadcasts to all clients plus FCM. Adopt cmux's model: events typed by source (Stop=passive idle, Notification/PermissionRequest=actionable), per-(session,type) coalescing with clear-boundaries, focus-aware suppression, a pluggable policy hook, and a PID-liveness sweep as the real stuck/crashed signal.

title: Beads sidebar mis-buckets epic children as blocked/not-ready (type-blind dependency grouping)
description:
The dependency-grouping loop in getIssues (src/services/beads-service.ts ~L276-286) and the getIssue filters (~L324-329) are type-blind: every dependency row (blocks, parent-child, child-of, discovered-from) is mapped into each issue's dependencies/dependents arrays. The sidebar then treats any issue with dependencies.length>0 as NOT 'ready' (BeadsSidebar.tsx ~L362) and as 'blocked' (BeadsContext computedStats ~L121); getStats' blocked-count JOIN (~L371) is likewise type-blind.

Impact: after the bd 1.0.5 schema migration, epic->child links use the high-volume 'parent-child' type (live data: ~75 parent-child vs 9 legacy child-of). So every child of an epic now carries its parent epic as a 'dependency' and is mis-bucketed out of Ready into Open (blocked). This is PRE-EXISTING (master had the identical type-blind loop) but is materially amplified by the migration plus the hotfix in branch fix/beads-dolt-schema-drift that restored dependency loading (previously every dependency query was crashing, so no deps showed at all).

Proposed fix: restrict the blocking/blocked computation to type='blocks' (exclude parent-child/child-of/discovered-from), and surface structural parent/child links via a separate field so the dependency tree still renders them. Apply consistently across getIssues grouping, getIssue filters, getStats JOIN, BeadsSidebar 'ready' logic, and BeadsContext computedStats.

Discovered during code review of the bd 1.0.5 schema-drift hotfix.

title: Auto-deploy didn't run db:backfill-user-emails (user_email table created, 0 rows)
description: After the PR #338 auto-deploy (webhook → dev.bryanli.net local prod), the user_email table existed (db:push ran) but had 0 rows — the db:backfill-user-emails step wired into scripts/deploy.ts runMigration (guarded) did NOT populate the prod DB (~/.remote-dev/sqlite.db). A manual 'bun run db:backfill-user-emails' then created the rows. The green deploy means the guard didn't catch it, so the webhook deploy path likely either doesn't invoke scripts/deploy.ts runMigration's backfill step, or runs it against a different DB than the live one. The self-healing resolver fallback (resolveUserIdByEmail → user.email) masked the gap (auth worked), but other future schema backfills wired into the deploy may silently not run. Investigate the HMAC webhook deploy path vs scripts/deploy.ts (does it run db:push AND db:backfill-* against the live DATABASE_URL?).

title: Deploy: rebuild + roll the Supervisor image to ship lifecycle-depth (purge/start-stop/storage) to the homelab
description:
PR #335 (master 732b2183) added Supervisor lifecycle-depth features (jvcx.14 permanent purge, jvcx.15 Start/Stop, jvcx.16 storage browser via ephemeral inspector Job) but they are NOT live on rdv.joyful.house — the main-app auto-deploy (dev.bryanli.net) does not build/run apps/supervisor. These reach the homelab only via the separate operator flow: Forgejo CI → Harbor → ArgoCD/k3s-infra (source=forgejo, NOT github — see the gotcha) per docs/SUPERVISOR_DEPLOY.md.

Steps:
- Rebuild apps/supervisor image (web+controller) from master @732b2183 and push to Harbor; bump the ArgoCD/k3s-infra image tag (SUPERVISOR_INSTANCE_IMAGE is the INSTANCE image — this is the SUPERVISOR's own image/tag) and let ArgoCD roll the rdv-supervisor Deployment (web + controller containers).
- NO new RBAC needed (storage browser reuses existing jobs:create,delete + pods/log:get); rbac.yaml comments updated in #335 but no verb changes, so no ClusterRole reapply required — confirm the live ClusterRole already has jobs+pods/log (it does).
- Post-roll verification on a real instance: (1) Stop then Start an instance (relabeled suspend/resume); (2) open the Storage tab and browse data-rdv-0 (try while RUNNING and while STOPPED — NFS/RWX should work in both); download a small file; (3) terminate an instance, wait for status=deleted, then 'Remove permanently' and confirm the slug frees + the row leaves the dashboard.
- Watch the controller + web container logs (namespace Inspector / api/instances paths) for the first inspector Job dispatch + cleanup.

Caveats to validate live: inspector Job must pull the INSTANCE image (SUPERVISOR_INSTANCE_IMAGE + pull secret) and that image must have node on PATH for the node -e listing script; node-pinned local-path PVCs are only browsable while the instance is running (NFS works while stopped). Context: [[rdv_supervisor_homelab_deploy]], [[supervisor_platform]].

title: release.yml may ship Node-ABI-mismatched natives (bun builds Node-24/ABI-137; release runs Node 22)
description: Same class as remote-dev-nxbv but for the NON-k8s self-hosted release (dev.bryanli.net). .github/workflows/release.yml sets NODE_VERSION=22. oven/bun:debian now reports as Node 24.3.0 (NODE_MODULE_VERSION 137) and compiles better-sqlite3/node-pty from source at ABI 137. If the release builds natives under bun but runs under Node 22 (ABI 127), better-sqlite3/node-pty fail to load (logging/analytics/terminal). Verify how the release builds+runs natives; if affected, align the release Node major to bun's ABI (Node 24) or rebuild natives against the release Node. Out of scope for the k3s instance fix (nxbv/uobt); filed separately.
notes: CONFIRMED real exposure: .github/workflows/release.yml sets NODE_VERSION=22 (actions/setup-node) but builds via bun (oven-sh/setup-bun → Node-24-compat) — bun install --frozen-lockfile + bun run build/terminal:build compile better-sqlite3/node-pty at ABI 137, unloadable on Node 22 (NODE_MODULE_VERSION 137 vs 127). Does NOT affect dev.bryanli.net (that deploys via scripts/deploy.ts which builds+runs on the target, currently healthy on 5e39eaa); this is the separate release/electron artifact. Fix likely = bump release.yml NODE_VERSION to 24 (verify electron packaging implications first). Not done this session (separate artifact + needs its own ship).
close_reason: FIXED + SHIPPED: PR #339 (release.yml NODE_VERSION 22→24) merged to master (f15560e), prod deploy green. Aligns the release toolchain/runtime Node with bun's compiled native ABI (Node 24/137) + the instance image. release.yml runs on tag push, so exercised on the next vX.Y.Z.

title: Supervisor: browse per-instance persistent storage via ephemeral inspector Job
description: No way to view a workspace's persistent volume (PVC data-rdv-0). Add a read-only file browser that works even when the instance is STOPPED. Mechanism (chosen): ephemeral inspector Job in namespace rdv-<slug> that mounts data-rdv-0 READ-ONLY and emits a bounded JSON directory listing (or base64 file content, size-capped) to its pod logs; supervisor reads logs (pods/log get — already granted), then deletes the Job. Uses instance image (SUPERVISOR_INSTANCE_IMAGE + pull secret) so it's always pullable in-cluster. Pin inspector to the running pod's node when the instance is up (avoid RWO multi-attach); when stopped, schedule freely (works for NFS/RWX; for node-pinned local-path while stopped, surface an honest 'start the workspace to browse' message — no silent failure). API: GET /api/instances/:id/storage?path=… (list) and GET /api/instances/:id/storage/file?path=… (download), operator+owner-scoped, few-second latency w/ spinner. UI: 'Storage' tab on detail page (breadcrumb + dir/file rows). NO new RBAC (jobs create/delete + pods/log already granted). Follows jvcx.8.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #335 (merged 732b2183). Read-only storage browser via ephemeral inspector Job (mounts data-rdv-0 RO; bounded listing / ≤5MiB file via pod log); works while stopped; no new RBAC. Hardened per adversarial review.

title: Supervisor: permanent (hard) delete — purge instance record after teardown confirmed
description: Today delete is soft-only: the reconciler tears down the namespace then marks the row status=deleted, but the DB row lives forever (still on the dashboard, slug stays reserved). Add an admin-only purge: DELETE /api/instances/:id?purge=true that hard-deletes the instance row (cascade removes instance_audit_log + instance_seed) ONLY when status==='deleted' (reconciler has confirmed namespace gone). 409 otherwise with 'terminate first'. UI: on a deleted instance show a destructive 'Remove permanently' button (strong confirm, frees slug), then navigate to dashboard. Log the purge via structured logger (the audit rows are being erased by design). No NFS data wipe (separate concern). Follows jvcx.8.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #335 (merged 732b2183). Admin purge of deleted instance records: DELETE /api/instances/:id?purge=true, guarded on status=deleted.

title: rdv instance data on /srv/k3s has NO offsite backup (projects, agent auth, DBs)
description: Verified 2026-06-02: the nfs-k3s StorageClass backs all rdv instance + supervisor PVCs with server0:/srv/k3s (a dedicated server0 NVMe LV, deliberately NOT on pool0). The offsite-backup app (restic→B2) only covers pool0 media/photos+scans+appdata (+ CNPG PITR), so NO rdv data (project dirs, agent profile auth = CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/CODEX_HOME/GEMINI_HOME, gh/ssh creds, instance + supervisor sqlite.db) has any offsite copy. That restic CronJob is also currently suspend=true with last run Failed. reclaimPolicy=Retain + archiveOnDelete protect only against PVC deletion within a live cluster, NOT against server0/NVMe loss or rm -rf /srv/k3s (single non-raid LV = single fault domain). Remediation options: (1) add /srv/k3s (or just the *.sqlite.db + projects/+profiles/) to restic BACKUP_PATHS; (2) app-level CronJob doing sqlite3 .backup per instance+supervisor DB + tar projects/profiles to B2; (3) re-enable/fix the suspended restic job regardless. Full detail + recovery: homelab repo docs/rdv-instance-data-recovery.md §4.

title: Instance OIDC login bounce — proxy presence-gate fix
description: Next16 standalone instance proxy (src/proxy.ts middleware) runs in a separate realm with wrong/empty process.env (vercel/next.js#53367) and non-shared globalThis, so getToken() returns null for every real session (empty/stale AUTH_SECRET + empty AUTH_URL→unprefixed cookie name) → OIDC login loops /dev↔/dev/login. Tried+rejected: build-inline AUTH_SECRET (per-instance secret), instrumentation→globalThis bridge (image a8ff4ba, realm split). FIX (branch fix/proxy-presence-final @ d073b2db, image d073b2d): scoped/instance mode → edge presence-gate on session-cookie presence (both __Secure- and bare scoped names); single-server keeps full getToken (AC-1); server-side getAuthSession still does real crypto authz. Verified in-pod with a forged real-user session: GET rdv.joyful.house/dev=200 no loop, /dev/api/sessions=200. demo+dev deployed on d073b2d.
close_reason: Shipped on branch fix/proxy-presence-final @ d073b2db (image rdv-instance:d073b2d); demo+dev rolled to it. Verified end-to-end in-pod: real-user forged session → GET rdv.joyful.house/dev=200, no loop, /dev/api/sessions=200, /dev/login→307→/dev. Edge presence-gate for scoped/instance mode; single-server unchanged (AC-1); server-side getAuthSession still does crypto authz. NOTE: full real-Authentik-login UX (OIDC callback minting the cookie) is the user's final sanity check; PR #333 chain NOT merged to master (prod-deploy decision held).

title: Native OIDC auth (generic) for Supervisor + base app — coexist with CF Access, enable LAN access
description: Add native OIDC login (provider-agnostic; generic OIDC discovery via issuer /.well-known) to BOTH the base remote-dev app (already NextAuth v5 — add an OIDC provider) and the Supervisor (currently Cloudflare-Access-only — introduce NextAuth v5 + OIDC). Goal: authenticate directly against an OIDC IdP without requiring Cloudflare Access, so LAN clients reach the dashboard via the internal ingress (Traefik) with no CF hairpin. DUAL AUTH: accept EITHER a CF Access JWT (external, unchanged) OR a native OIDC session (internal); both resolve to the SAME email->role mapping (admin/operator/viewer) so RBAC is untouched. Keep the production fail-closed guards. Env-driven: OIDC issuer + client id/secret. Scope: supervisor + base app. Provider-agnostic (Authentik/Keycloak/Google/etc.).
notes: Code MERGED to master: base app OIDC (PR #328) + supervisor NextAuth/OIDC dual-auth + closed-allowlist (login + per-request revocation) (PR #329). Reviewed (security + migrate-safety), fixes applied (null-email deny, idempotent migration, OIDC-only edge gate, getToken cookie name). REMAINING (deploy, waits on operator IdP choice): rebuild supervisor+instance images, add AUTH_SECRET + SUPERVISOR_OIDC_* / OIDC_* to secrets, create an OIDC app in the IdP, then DNS rdv->Traefik VIP + a Traefik IngressRoute->router for native LAN auth.

title: Supervisor: provisioned instance never passes /<slug>/api/readyz within 120s budget → reconciler reaps namespace
description: On a k3s deploy (Option C), creating an instance provisions the namespace + StatefulSet and the pod Runs, but the reconciler's readiness gate (GET /<slug>/api/readyz, ~120s budget) never goes ready → instance flips to 'error' and the namespace is deleted, so no instance reaches 'ready'. Undiagnosed. Hypothesis: the instance app's own cold-start / missing DB migrate-on-boot (the instance image IS the base remote-dev app; same class as remote-dev-bqgo, the supervisor's missing migrate-on-boot). Next: provision an instance and, before the ~120s reap, exec into the rdv-<slug> pod to check app logs, whether the SQLite tables exist, and what /api/readyz gates on (DB? AUTH_SECRET? base-path/slug env?). Relates to remote-dev-bqgo.

title: Supervisor: auto-migrate schema on boot (drizzle) — fresh PVC has no tables
description: The supervisor image has no schema-creation step: instrumentation.ts seeds the admin but assumes tables exist, and they use drizzle-kit push (no migration files) which never runs in-container. On first homelab deploy the PVC db had no tables → SQLITE_ERROR in web (seed) + controller (reconcile). Worked around by 'kubectl exec ... bun run db:push' (persists in PVC), but a recreated PVC would be empty again. Fix: run migrations on boot — generate drizzle SQL migrations into apps/supervisor/drizzle/ + call migrate() from drizzle-orm/libsql/migrator in instrumentation.ts (and the controller) before first query; or an init-container/Job. Surfaced deploying to joyful.house.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #329: migrate-on-boot (drizzle migrate at web+controller startup, idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS so safe on fresh + legacy push-created DBs); fresh PVC now gets the full schema.

title: Supervisor: provision per-instance image pull secret + StatefulSet imagePullSecrets
description: Provisioned instance namespaces (rdv-<slug>) can't pull instance images from a PRIVATE registry: buildStatefulSet sets no imagePullSecrets and the ns has no pull secret. Teach the provisioner to (a) create a pull Secret in each instance ns from supervisor config, and (b) set imagePullSecrets (or patch the default SA) on the instance StatefulSet. Surfaced deploying to the joyful.house homelab (Harbor, private) — see homelab docs/claude/2026-06-01-rdv-supervisor-homelab-deploy.md.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #321: provisioner creates per-instance dockerconfigjson Secret + sets StatefulSet imagePullSecrets (SUPERVISOR_INSTANCE_IMAGE_PULL_SECRET_NAME/...DOCKERCONFIGJSON)

title: deploy/runtime: rebuild native modules against running Node; guard against Node ABI drift
description: 2026-06-01: Homebrew auto-upgraded Node (NODE_MODULE_VERSION 141 -> 147, ~May 8) which silently broke better-sqlite3, disabling structured logging / logs.db writes for ~3 weeks (caught at startup as 'Log pruning failed', non-fatal, so it went unnoticed). Today's recovery needed a manual 'node-gyp rebuild' of better-sqlite3 before the clean build. Options: (a) deploy step rebuilds native modules (better-sqlite3) against the runtime Node after bun install; (b) pin a Node version for prod instead of tracking homebrew's auto-updating 'node'; (c) startup self-check that fails loudly (and alerts) on native-module ABI mismatch rather than swallowing it. Prod runs next-server under '/opt/homebrew/bin/node' (currently v26).

title: deploy: build blue/green slots from an isolated git worktree (stop hard-resetting the live dev checkout)
description:
Follow-up to remote-dev-6pbo / remote-dev-1oxx. The deploy tree-sync is now correct, but still mutates the shared dev checkout.

## Current behavior (after 1oxx / PR #311)
`scripts/deploy.ts` buildSlot() syncs the source with: `git fetch origin` -> `git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/master` (divergence guard) -> `git reset --hard origin/master`, all run in **PROJECT_ROOT** (scripts/deploy.ts:48 = the LIVE dev checkout /Users/bryanli/Projects/btli/remote-dev), then builds the inactive blue/green slot.

## Residual hazard
The divergence guard only protects un-pushed COMMITS. `git reset --hard origin/master` still discards uncommitted/staged/dirty tracked changes AND deletes colliding untracked files in the live checkout. Because PROJECT_ROOT is also the interactive dev + agent working tree (parallel work is always happening here), a deploy firing while you have in-progress edits will **silently wipe them**. Correct deploy, but it can eat the developer's uncommitted work.

## Proposed fix — isolate the deploy build from the dev checkout
Build each slot from a deploy-OWNED git worktree pinned to origin/master, never touching PROJECT_ROOT (see Design). Benefits:
- Deploys can never clobber uncommitted dev/agent work in PROJECT_ROOT.
- Dirty/untracked cruft in PROJECT_ROOT (.beads/issues.jsonl churn, stray ~/ dir, orphaned docs) becomes irrelevant to deploys.
- Decouples deploy from concurrent edits/races in the main tree.
- Preserves the untracked-collision fix (6pbo/1oxx root cause).

## Considerations
- Worktrees share .git objects (cheap). Per-worktree node_modules/build artifacts via the existing scripts/worktree-warm.sh APFS clonefile path.
- Prune stale deploy worktrees; keep the path gitignored / outside the repo root.
- Update docs/DEPLOYMENT.md to describe the worktree-isolated build.

See remote-dev-6pbo (design option 1, documented in its notes) and remote-dev-1oxx (the reset --hard this supersedes). Memory: deploy_untracked_blocks_merge.
design: Maintain a deploy-owned worktree (e.g. under ~/.remote-dev or a gitignored .worktrees/ path) via 'git worktree add --detach <deploy-src> origin/master', or a persistent worktree that runs 'git fetch' + 'reset --hard origin/master' INSIDE the isolated tree. Build BUILDS_DIR/{slot} from that worktree; evaluate the merge-base divergence guard there. Per-worktree node_modules via scripts/worktree-warm.sh (APFS clonefile). Prune stale deploy worktrees.
acceptance_criteria: A production deploy never modifies PROJECT_ROOT (the live dev checkout): uncommitted/staged/untracked files there survive a deploy unchanged. Slots build from an isolated git worktree pinned to origin/master, so dirty/untracked files in PROJECT_ROOT can't affect or block a deploy. The merge-base divergence guard is preserved. Verified live: deploy with deliberate uncommitted changes in PROJECT_ROOT and confirm they remain afterward; blue/green swap + deploy:status unchanged. docs/DEPLOYMENT.md updated.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #342 (merged 462b4056). Deploy slots + migrations build from a persistent deploy-owned worktree (~/.remote-dev/deploy-src) pinned to origin/master; PROJECT_ROOT source never reset. Self-healing + path-guarded.

title: deploy.ts: untracked/dirty working-tree files silently abort the deploy at git merge --ff-only
description: PR #309 (docs) merged to master, webhook returned success, but the async deploy.ts ABORTED at 'git merge --ff-only origin/master' because an untracked docs/MOBILE_ARCHITECTURE.md in PROJECT_ROOT would be overwritten ('untracked working tree files would be overwritten by merge ... Aborting'). The deploy failed silently from the operator's view (GH workflow shows the webhook 202/success; only deploy.log shows the abort). prod kept running the prior commit. Self-resolved only because a parallel process later removed the untracked file. SAME class of failure can recur with any stray untracked file OR a dirty tracked file (e.g. .beads/issues.jsonl, which bd auto-flushes and is frequently dirty) if a future master commit touches it. FIX options: (a) deploy.ts should 'git clean -fd' + 'git checkout -- .' (or 'git reset --hard origin/master' after fetch) before/instead of the ff-only merge, scoped safely; (b) at minimum, surface the abort loudly (escalate/alert) instead of only logging; (c) consider gitignoring .beads/issues.jsonl churn or committing it via bd sync on a side branch. Discovered while landing remote-dev-i85i/j4wr.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #311 (commit f24bcb0e, deployed 15177209): buildSlot() now git-reset --hard origin/master (with merge-base --is-ancestor divergence guard distinguishing exit 1 vs git errors) instead of merge --ff-only. Robust to dirty tracked files + untracked-collisions; gitignored runtime data untouched. Empirically verified in git 2.54.0. Reset --hard chosen over git clean (would cause collateral untracked deletion).

title: readyz /health probe is shallow: terminal /health always returns 200
description: The terminal server's /health handler (src/server/terminal.ts) always returns 200 when the HTTP listener is up — it does not gate on scheduler/PTY/tmux subsystem health (scheduler state is in the body but /api/readyz ignores the body). After PR #308 made the prod (unix-socket) readyz path actually work, prod now trusts /health for the first time, so this shallowness is live: a terminal server that is up but internally wedged (scheduler crashed, PTY spawn broken) still reports ready, and the LB routes session-create traffic to it. Fix options: (a) have checkTerminalServer() in src/app/api/readyz/route.ts parse the body and require scheduler===true, or (b) make /health return non-2xx when the scheduler/PTY subsystem is down (cleaner — the probe's 2xx contract already supports it). Found by silent-failure-hunter during PR #308 review (Finding 2). Not a blocker for #308.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #314 (verified live: deploy 4bc9341c, workflow run 26712464548 GREEN, readyz=200 on the new server confirming the scheduler gate doesn't false-negative). terminal /health now returns 503 when schedulerOrchestrator.isStarted() is false; readyz's 2xx gate reflects scheduler health.
