title: terminal_session.agent_provider defaults to claude on every row
description:
Root cause for the shell-shows-agent-chip class of bugs (just patched cosmetically in remote-dev-8mw5).

Problems:
1. src/db/schema.ts:345 — agentProvider column has .default("claude"), so every terminal_session row (shell, file, browser, ssh, etc.) gets agent_provider='claude' in the DB.
2. src/services/session-service.ts:255 (plugin stub) and :768 (DB insert) coalesce input.agentProvider ?? 'claude' regardless of terminalType.
3. src/services/session-service.ts:283 already computes mergedAgentProvider correctly for agent/loop terminals, but the INSERT at line 768 ignores it and reuses raw input.agentProvider — a latent bug.
4. src/app/api/sessions/[id]/spawn/route.ts:57 hardcodes 'claude' as the last fallback when spawning a child of any terminal type.

Fix:
- Remove .default("claude") from db/schema.ts (column already nullable).
- Gate agentProvider writes in session-service.ts on isAgentTerminal = terminalType === 'agent' || 'loop'. Use mergedAgentProvider at the insert site so the merge logic is actually honored.
- In spawn route, change last fallback from 'claude' to null/undefined — the service handles the agent-terminal default.
- One-time migration: SET agent_provider = NULL WHERE terminal_type NOT IN ('agent','loop'). Add scripts/migrate-agent-provider.ts + package.json db:migrate-agent-provider script.

All downstream consumers (Sidebar.tsx, SessionMetadataBar.tsx, agent-plugin-server.ts, isMCPSupported, etc.) already guard for null, so no consumer changes are needed.

Closing this issue removes the need for terminalType-based UI gating in places like Sidebar.tsx:590 since the data itself becomes correct.
close_reason: Closed

title: SessionMetadataBar: agent chip leaks onto shell sessions + style and ordering fixes
description:
Three problems in src/components/session/SessionMetadataBar.tsx:

1. Shell terminals show a 'claude' badge. Root cause: db/schema.ts:345 defaults agentProvider to 'claude' for every new terminal_session row regardless of terminal_type, and the chip render only checks (session.agentProvider && session.agentProvider !== 'none'), not terminalType. Fix by adding an isAgentTerminal check (terminalType === 'agent' || terminalType === 'loop') to the chip's gate AND the hasAgent computation used to early-return the bar.

2. Chip font/padding is heavier than the sibling git-branch chip. Currently: text-[9px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider border rounded-md px-1.5. Should match the branch chip's tone: text-[10px], lighter padding (px-1 py-0.5), no uppercase/tracking-wider/font-semibold. Keep the AGENT_VISUALS brand color classes for the bg/border tint so the chip is still visually distinct, just lighter.

3. Chip order: branch chip should come first, then PR chip, then agent chip, then port chips. Currently agent is first.
close_reason: Closed

title: embed: no foreground notifications in /m/session — NotificationContext not mounted
description:
FCM push works for background notifications, but src/app/m/session/[id]/page.tsx doesn't mount NotificationContext. So when the user is foregrounded inside an agent session and the terminal server broadcasts a notification event (job done, agent waiting on input, peer message), no toast surfaces. PWA's MobileShell mounts NotificationProvider and toasts via the notification context.

Fix:
- Add NotificationProvider to /m/session/[id]/page.tsx, scoped like PreferencesProvider was scoped
- Hook up TerminalWithKeyboard's onNotification prop in EmbeddedSessionView to forward server events to the context
- Optionally surface via a native bridge call (notifyToNative new handler name) so the Flutter shell can show a native toast if the WebView is occluded

Acceptance:
- Backgrounded process completes -> in-app toast shows in the Flutter app the same as in the PWA
- Peer message arrives -> toast surfaces
- No regression on background FCM push
close_reason: Closed

title: flutter: onLinkOpen is a stub — tapped URLs in terminal go nowhere
description:
session_view_screen.dart:141-149 has an onLinkOpen handler that just debugPrints the URL. The comment admits 'Phase 2 stub: log; Phase 4 wires url_launcher / Custom Tabs.' Tap a URL in agent output (e.g. http link in Claude's reply, GitHub PR link) and nothing happens.

Fix: wire url_launcher (already a likely dep for the auth flow). Open in Custom Tab on Android / SFSafariViewController on iOS so the user stays in the app's context.

Acceptance:
- Tap http/https URL in the terminal -> system browser sheet opens
- mailto:, tel:, etc. handled appropriately
- Internal links (same origin as the server) still navigate inside the WebView (NavigationPolicy already handles this)
close_reason: Merged via worktree branches in this session: pmhg (url_launcher wired in session_view_screen.dart onLinkOpen + WebViewFactory callback, LaunchMode.inAppBrowserView for http/https, externalApplication for mailto/tel); a6jv (SmartKeyStrip rewritten with KEYS/NAV mode toggle, ^C/^D direct keys, punctuation row | / ~ - _ :, Enter/Shift-Enter, raw byte channel for control keys); eygp (SessionStatusBar overflow PopupMenuButton with Suspend/View Recordings/Delete; suspend/delete call SessionsApi + maybePop, recordings push /home/recording/sessionId); e1b9 (onSelectionChange now Clipboard.setData directly, SnackBar removed for PWA parity). flutter analyze + flutter test (345 passing) confirm green.

title: embed: SessionEndedOverlay Restart button is a no-op (onSessionRestart not wired)
description:
EmbeddedSessionView mounts TerminalWithKeyboard without an onSessionRestart prop. When an agent session exits, TerminalWithKeyboard.tsx shows SessionEndedOverlay (line 258-265) whose Restart button calls handleRestart -> await onSessionRestart?.() — which is undefined, so nothing happens. PWA's MobileSessionView wires onSessionRestart via session restart API.

Fix: in src/components/mobile/embed/EmbeddedSessionView.tsx, add an onSessionRestart handler that POSTs to /api/sessions/:id/restart (or the existing restart endpoint MobileSessionView uses — grep there for the canonical path) and pass it through. Same for onSessionDelete.

Acceptance:
- After an agent session exits in the Flutter app, tapping Restart restarts the agent in place
- Tapping Delete closes the session (and the worktree if applicable)
- No regression on the PWA path
close_reason: Closed

title: flutter: voice mic button silently fails — WebView has no onPermissionRequest handler
description:
TerminalWithKeyboard renders VoiceMicButton when terminalType === 'agent' (external chrome path, line 329-333), and the button calls getUserMedia() to capture audio. But mobile/lib/infrastructure/webview/webview_factory.dart registers no onPermissionRequest callback, so the InAppWebView denies microphone access on Android without showing a system prompt. iOS likely needs NSMicrophoneUsageDescription wired through too.

Fix:
- Add onPermissionRequest to InAppWebViewSettings/InAppWebView in webview_factory.dart that grants MICROPHONE (and maybe CAMERA for future image capture) for the Remote Dev origin only.
- Ensure mobile/ios/Runner/Info.plist has NSMicrophoneUsageDescription.
- Verify the iOS path: flutter_inappwebview proxies WKWebView's WKUIDelegate; check that mic permission propagates.

Acceptance:
- Tapping the voice mic on an agent session prompts for mic permission once (Android), then captures audio
- Voice transcription end-to-end works in the Flutter app the same as in mobile Chrome
close_reason: Added onPermissionRequest to WebViewFactory: grants MICROPHONE + CAMERA only when the requesting origin matches the server origin (policy.serverOrigin). Added NSMicrophoneUsageDescription + NSCameraUsageDescription to ios/Runner/Info.plist and RECORD_AUDIO/MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS/CAMERA permissions to android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml. Also set mediaPlaybackRequiresUserGesture=false so iOS prompts on tap-initiated getUserMedia. End-to-end voice transcription requires on-device verification (no Flutter on-device test harness).

title: flutter: WebView height fixed against keyboard inset — tmux never reflows when keyboard opens
description:
SessionViewScreen pins webViewHeight = constraints.maxHeight - statusBarHeight - chromeHeight and uses resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false (session_view_screen.dart:193, 202-208). The comment says 'xterm.js never sees a resize event, never fires SIGWINCH' — but that's exactly the behavior the user expects: when the keyboard opens, the terminal grid should shrink so the active line stays visible.

The PWA gets this for free via visualViewport resize -> fitAddon.fit() -> resize WS message -> tmux resize-window.

Fix: shrink webViewHeight by MediaQuery.viewInsetsOf(context).bottom (still keeping the chrome floating above the keyboard) so the WebView reflows. xterm.js's ResizeObserver/visualViewport listeners will pick it up.

Acceptance:
- Opening the Android keyboard reduces tmux window height
- Closing it returns tmux to full height
- Smart keys + input bar remain visible above the keyboard
close_reason: State cleanup — already shipped in commit 08fcb41b (mobile feature-parity overhaul) per its merge message, but never flipped to closed. WebView height/keyboard inset reflow is live.

title: flutter: PinchZoomWrapper's ScaleGestureRecognizer eats WebView touches (scroll, tap, pinch all broken)
description:
SessionViewScreen wraps the InAppWebView with PinchZoomWrapper, which is a GestureDetector with onScale* callbacks. Flutter's ScaleGestureRecognizer competes in the gesture arena even for 1-pointer touches; the InAppWebView is created without explicit gestureRecognizers (webview_factory.dart:29), so the outer detector always wins. Net effect: scrolling, tap-to-focus, and pinch inside the terminal all fail.

Options:
- Drop PinchZoomWrapper entirely; let xterm.js handle pinch via touch events (PWA already does)
- OR keep native pinch but pass InAppWebView a gestureRecognizers set that claims single-pointer touches

Acceptance:
- 1-finger drag scrolls the terminal viewport (or selects, per xterm.js mobile config)
- Tap focuses the terminal / dismisses keyboard correctly
- Pinch zooms the terminal font (whichever path is chosen)
close_reason: Verified resolved in code: PinchZoomWrapper removed from session_view_screen.dart (6tos), bridge.setFontSize wired through updateUserSettings (d76d), usePinchZoom hook imported and bound in EmbeddedSessionView (kpxd), BeadsSidebar useState now seeds from DB defaults with localStorage hydrated in mount-effect (ge5h), DB sync effects gated on userSettingsLoaded flag (8y2n).

title: embed: mount PreferencesProvider in /m layout so font size + appearance prefs reach embedded terminal
description:
The /m/* mobile-embed layout deliberately omits the desktop providers, but PreferencesProvider was lumped in with the heavy ones. As a result, EmbeddedSessionView passes initialFontSize=undefined and Terminal falls back to its default size — the user's persisted fontSize (and fontFamily) never apply inside the Flutter WebView.

Mount PreferencesProvider in src/app/m/layout.tsx (or scoped to /m/session/[id]/page.tsx) and pass currentPreferences.fontSize + fontFamily through EmbeddedSessionView to TerminalWithKeyboard.

Acceptance:
- Setting fontSize in user prefs takes effect in the Flutter app WebView terminal
- /m/channel and /m/recording embeds still keep their lean bundle if PreferencesProvider isn't needed there
close_reason: Mounted PreferencesProvider in /m/session/[id]/page.tsx (NOT /m layout). EmbeddedSessionView now reads currentPreferences.fontSize + .fontFamily with Number.isFinite guard, passes to TerminalWithKeyboard. Killed unused initialFontSize prop.

title: Mobile app render doesn't resize the tmux window
description: The Flutter mobile app's terminal render doesn't trigger tmux window resize to match the mobile viewport. Investigate how resize is communicated from mobile app to terminal server, and whether server forwards it to PTY/tmux.
close_reason: All four child fixes shipped: doqi (PreferencesProvider mounted), 6tos (PinchZoomWrapper removed), zjsc (WebView height tracks keyboardInset), d76d (bridge setFontSize wired). Single PR.

title: Mobile: CF Access redirect to login HTML not detected as auth failure
description:
When the mobile app's CF_Authorization cookie expires, Cloudflare returns a 302 redirect to a Cloudflareaccess.com login URL. Dio follows the redirect automatically and receives a 200 OK HTML response from the CF login page.

Symptom: 'Failed to load sessions' with inner FormatException 'Unexpected /api/sessions response shape' (from sessions_api.dart:86). The shape check sees a String (HTML) instead of List or Map. Retry doesn't help because the cookie is still expired.

CfAuthInterceptor (mobile/lib/infrastructure/api/cf_auth_interceptor.dart) only fires onReauthNeeded on 401/403 — CF Access's 302→HTML never reaches that path.

Fix: detect CF Access intercept in onResponse and trigger reauth. Signals: 3xx with Location containing 'cloudflareaccess.com', OR 200 with content-type text/html when expecting JSON. Either should fire onReauthNeeded and abort the request with a recognizable exception so the UI routes through /reauth instead of surfacing a FormatException.
close_reason: Closed

title: Mobile release Android AAB: JKS keystore parse error
description:
Mobile-release.yml on mobile-v0.3.16 failed at :app:signReleaseBundle:

> Failed to read key from store "/tmp/remote-dev-release.jks": Tag number over 30 is not supported

JDK can't parse the base64-decoded keystore (ASN.1 tag mismatch). Likely the RDV_ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 secret is a PKCS12 misnamed as JKS, OR was generated with a newer JDK than the runner's. Not a regression from remote-dev-l4q6 — first mobile-v* tag ever pushed, so signing config has never run in CI before.

Fix candidates:
- Re-export keystore as proper JKS with keytool -importkeystore.
- Configure Gradle signing block with storeType='PKCS12' if the keystore is actually P12.
- Pin workflow JDK version to match the keystore origin.

Run: https://github.com/btli/remote-dev/actions/runs/25757550353
close_reason: Code-only fix: set storeType=PKCS12 in mobile/android/app/build.gradle.kts (matches modern keytool default since JDK 9) with override via RDV_ANDROID_KEYSTORE_TYPE env; updated mobile-release.yml to decode to /tmp/remote-dev-release.p12 and pass through optional type. CI verification requires re-tagging a mobile-v* release (e.g. mobile-v0.3.17) and observing the :app:signReleaseBundle step succeed.

title: Fix PWA tap-to-click on Claude TUI mouse mode
description:
On installed PWA (iOS Safari standalone), tap-to-click does NOT reach Claude TUI mouse-mode buttons. Pinch-to-zoom and scroll work fine — only the synthetic mouse-event tap path is broken.

useTouchInteractions.ts:synthesizeTap() dispatches new MouseEvent('mousedown'/'mouseup') on the .xterm-screen element. Events should bubble up to .terminal.xterm where xterm's bindMouse mousedown listener forwards them as SGR mouse reports through the WebSocket.

Existing unit tests pass against a fake xterm. Need to add an integration test against real xterm.js to verify the full synthesize-tap → SGR-report pipeline, and harden the dispatch for iOS PWA quirks (pointer events, more complete event init, possibly dispatching on the host element directly).
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix BeadsSidebar hydration mismatch from localStorage read in useState initializer
description:
BeadsSidebar.tsx reads localStorage inside useState initializers (collapsed, width, activeTab) using a typeof window !== 'undefined' branch. This is the exact anti-pattern React warns about: server renders the collapsed w-12 strip (dbCollapsed default = true), client first-render reads localStorage and returns the expanded shape with style.width = stored value, producing a hydration mismatch and full tree regeneration.

Repro: have localStorage 'beads-sidebar-collapsed' = 'false' but no userSettings loaded yet (or dbCollapsed default). Load the page — error: 'Hydration failed because the server rendered HTML didn't match the client.'

Fix: seed useState from DB defaults only, sync from localStorage in a mount useEffect. Same fix needed for width and activeTab.
close_reason: Verified resolved in code: PinchZoomWrapper removed from session_view_screen.dart (6tos), bridge.setFontSize wired through updateUserSettings (d76d), usePinchZoom hook imported and bound in EmbeddedSessionView (kpxd), BeadsSidebar useState now seeds from DB defaults with localStorage hydrated in mount-effect (ge5h), DB sync effects gated on userSettingsLoaded flag (8y2n).

title: Add bulk dismiss for notifications (web + Flutter mobile)

close_reason: Implemented in ec5f0f3: web Clear all wrapped in AlertDialog confirmation; mobile Flutter gained Clear all AppBar action + AlertDialog + dismissAll() API method. PWA already had this on master.

title: mobile build: gradle ModuleVersionResolveException NPE during assembleDebug

Not introduced by remote-dev-jch1 work (analyze + tests pass cleanly). May relate to multi-worktree Gradle file-watcher interaction (the verbose log shows another worktree being added to the watcher list). Investigate: clean ~/.gradle caches, isolate worktree builds, or pin Gradle/AGP versions.
close_reason: dolt-sync ghosts; truly closed

title: mobile: replace CF login WebView with system browser + Universal Links
description: The current CfLoginWebViewScreen uses an in-app WebView with a spoofed UA to bypass Google's disallowed_useragent 403. This works today but: (a) violates Google's preferred OAuth pattern; (b) future Google detection changes could re-break us; (c) cookies live in the WebView jar instead of the system browser. Proper fix: open <server>/m/login in a Custom Tab (Android) / SFSafariViewController (iOS) via url_launcher, and register an App Link / Universal Link callback that returns the user to the app with the CF cookie. Requires server-side Universal Link config (apple-app-site-association + assetlinks.json) at /api/auth/cf-mobile-callback or similar.
close_reason: System browser flow IS the proper fix that shipped in PR #289 — this issue was the agent's mis-filed 'future' marker before the proper fix was complete. Superseded.

Surface installed agents in a submenu, persist per-agent default flags (--dangerously-skip-permissions, auto mode, etc.), expose config screens for each agent in Preferences, and make the default agent a dropdown.

Remove their type members, config interfaces, SECRETS_PROVIDERS entries, factory branches, and PROVIDER_NOT_IMPLEMENTED error path. Update tests and schema.ts comment.

title: wire mobile cursorBlink into PWA WebView via BridgeController
description:
Phase 6 appearance settings on mobile (#273) ships cursorBlink as a Flutter-side preference but doesn't yet propagate it to the xterm.js terminal inside the WebView.

Add BridgeController.setCursorBlink(bool) that evaluates JS like:
  if (window.term) window.term.options.cursorBlink = value;

…and follow the same setFontSize pattern in bridge_controller.dart.

Then register a ref.listen in SessionViewScreen + RecordingScreen + ChannelScreen on appearanceSettingsProvider that calls bridge.setCursorBlink(value) when the setting changes and once when the bridge marks ready.

Leftover from codex review on PR #273.
close_reason: Phase 8 shipped via PRs #276-277

title: wire mobile fontScale into PWA WebView via BridgeController
description:
Phase 6 appearance settings on mobile (#273) wired fontScale into Flutter chrome via MediaQuery.textScaler in MaterialApp builder. The PWA inside the WebView still uses its own font sizing.

Add BridgeController.setFontScale(double) that evaluates JS like:
  document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--rdv-font-scale', String(scale));

…and have the PWA CSS read --rdv-font-scale where it currently uses fixed font-size for terminal text and chat bubbles.

Then register a ref.listen in WebView host screens (SessionViewScreen, RecordingScreen, ChannelScreen) that calls bridge.setFontScale() on appearanceSettingsProvider changes and on bridge ready.

Leftover from codex review on PR #273.
close_reason: Phase 8 shipped via PRs #276-277

title: mobile: BridgeController.back() should return Future<bool> for handled-pop signal
description:
Codex review on PR #268 found that BridgeController.back() and JS RdvBridgeAdapter.back both return void. RecordingScreen._handleBack unconditionally calls Navigator.maybePop() after bridge.back(), so the PWA can't consume the back gesture (e.g., to close a thread panel) and prevent native pop.

Fix: BridgeController.back() returns Future<bool>; JS bridge returns boolean; native handlers only Navigator.maybePop() when unhandled.

Defer of codex finding from PR #268; non-blocking for Phase 6 close.
close_reason: Phase 7 shipped via PRs #272-274

title: Mobile: smoke test for ChannelScreen back-bridge call
description: ChannelScreen._handleBack invokes _bridge?.back() before Navigator.maybePop, giving the embedded PWA a chance to close threads/modals. There is no widget-test coverage for this path because InAppWebView can't be exercised in widget tests. Worth a unit-style test with a mock BridgeController that asserts back() is called exactly once when the AppBar back icon is tapped. Likely needs to extract a 'bridge factory' so tests can inject a fake. Parent: Phase 6 Epic C (remote-dev-50u1).
close_reason: Phase 8 shipped via PRs #276-277

title: mobile: implement Appearance profile screen
description:
The mobile Appearance screen at mobile/lib/presentation/screens/profile/appearance_screen.dart is a stub showing 'Phase 5 fills this in.'

The mobile app currently hard-codes the Tokyo Night palette (#1A1B26 bg, white text) in every screen. To make Appearance functional, decide first whether mobile theming should:
  (a) mirror the web's per-profile appearance (GET/PUT /api/profiles/:id/appearance), or
  (b) be a local-only mobile preference (font scale, terminal cursor style, dark-only for now).

Suggested initial scope (option b — quickest):
- Riverpod-backed AppearanceSettings store (analogous to BiometricSettingsStore at mobile/lib/domain/biometric_settings.dart)
- Settings: font scale (S/M/L), reduce motion toggle, terminal cursor blink toggle
- Persist via SharedPreferences (existing pattern)
- Apply font scale via MediaQuery wrap in app.dart
- Tokyo Night styled rows mirroring BiometricSettingsScreen

Defer per-profile theme sync until a follow-up — web profile appearance is per-agent-profile, not per-user, and binding mobile to a single agent profile needs design.

Audit context: remote-dev-w5f5 audit (2026-05-10).
close_reason: Phase 7 shipped via PRs #272-274

title: Archive Flutter mobile app
description: Archive the Flutter app at mobile/ to archive/mobile-flutter/ pending a redesign of the mobile experience. Preserve the RDV_ANDROID_* env var contract. Remove build-android from release.yml and the related signing test.
close_reason: Closed

title: Mobile terminal bottom rows clipped on Pixel Fold unfolded
description: On Pixel 10 Pro Fold in unfolded mode, the bottom few rows of the terminal output are not visible — they appear to render behind/below the smart-keys strip + input bar, or off-screen. Header (status bar) and input box ARE visible, but terminal's bottom rows are clipped. Likely the layout uses 100dvh on MobileShell's unfolded wrapper but doesn't account for visualViewport changes / Android Chrome dvh quirks across fold transitions. Possible fixes: (1) listen to visualViewport.resize and force a re-fit, (2) use visualViewport.height directly for the height container, (3) add pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)] to reserve space for the gesture bar.
close_reason: Track visualViewport.height in MobileShell unfold wrapper + listen for visualViewport resize in Terminal.tsx. Merged from worktree-agent-a1cc08deebe4398d5.

title: Defer desktop-only context providers off mobile critical path (close mobile perf to >=90)
description:
gj45 shipped MobileViewportSwitch code-split (PR #242), bringing mobile / from 71 to 84 perf and unused-JS from 366 KB to 99 KB. Mobile target (>=90) still missed by 6 points. Root cause: src/app/page.tsx eagerly imports 18 context providers (SessionContext, ProjectTreeContext, GitHubStatsContext, GitHubIssuesContext, SessionMCPContext, BeadsContext, ScheduleContext, etc). Many are desktop-only — their dependency graphs (and their own initialization effects) ship in the mobile bundle.

Fix sketch: split the provider tree into a 'core' set (Preferences, Session, Notification, Channel, Profile — what mobile actually uses) and a 'desktop-only' set wrapped behind the next/dynamic boundary so it lives in the desktop chunk. Some providers may need their internal eager imports (e.g. xterm-related modules in SessionMCPContext) lazy-loaded too.

Acceptance: Lighthouse mobile / >=90 perf, with no regression on desktop perf or feature behavior. Compare against docs/reports/2026-05-07-lighthouse-mobile-gj45/home-mobile.report.json baseline.
close_reason: Provider deferral shipped via PR #244 (merged). Mobile / 84->86-87 perf, mobile unused-JS 99->82KB, desktop / 92->99 perf (also resolved a pre-existing 0.158 desktop CLS regression). Mobile target (>=90) not fully met (87) — remaining gap is structural LCP from MobileLockScreen, deferred to ruh0. The bead's stated scope (defer desktop-only context providers off mobile critical path) is complete.

title: Phase 7 perf: code-split MobileViewportSwitch desktop branch off the mobile critical path
description:
Lighthouse mobile measurement (k583) shows the / route on mobile scoring 72-73, with a single 470 KB JS chunk that's 72% unused (340 KB). Root cause: MobileViewportSwitch renders both branches in the same client component tree, so the desktop SessionManager dependency graph (xterm, codemirror, sidebars, modals, ...) ships in the initial page bundle even on small viewports.

Fix sketch:
- Wrap the desktop branch with next/dynamic import + ssr:false (or ssr:true with loading) so mobile viewports never download the desktop bundle.
- Mirror for the mobile branch on desktop.
- Re-run Lighthouse from docs/reports/2026-05-07-lighthouse-mobile against this branch and verify perf >=90 on both viewports.
- Watch CLS - make sure the swap doesn't cause layout jump above 0.1.

Acceptance: perf >=90 on Lighthouse mobile / and Lighthouse desktop /. unused-javascript savings on / drop below 100 KB.

Reference: docs/reports/2026-05-07-lighthouse-mobile/README.md (k583 measurement run).
close_reason: MobileViewportSwitch code-split shipped via PR #242. Mobile / 71->84 perf, desktop / 64->92 perf, mobile unused-JS 366KB->99KB (acceptance criterion #3 met). Mobile perf target (>=90) not fully met (84) — remaining gap is the eagerly-imported desktop-only context providers in app/page.tsx. The bead's stated scope (code-split MobileViewportSwitch) is complete; remaining provider-deferral work split out into c9aq for separate scoping. tx71 (login bundle trim) remains open as separate issue.

title: Terminal scroll artifacts on long-running sessions (WebGL atlas)
description: Users report visual artifacts when scrolling long-running terminal sessions. Resizing the browser window clears the garbled text, which strongly suggests the xterm.js WebGL renderer texture atlas is getting stale/corrupted over time. Need to investigate calling webglAddon.clearTextureAtlas() on appropriate events (visibility change, periodic, scroll, etc.) without harming performance.
close_reason: Fixed via WebGL atlas refresh on visibility/DPR change + context-loss recovery. Merged in worktree-agent-a91a2cd97ac1cd31c.

title: Mobile smart-key buttons fail Lighthouse label-content-name-mismatch
description: Live Phase 7 verification surfaced this: the mobile terminal smart-key strip buttons (Esc, Tab, Ctrl, arrows, etc.) fail Lighthouse a11y rule label-content-name-mismatch — the visible text content (e.g., 'Esc') doesn't match the start of the accessible name. Fix: align aria-label with visible text, or drop the aria-label and let visible text be the accessible name. Found in PR #234 verification.
close_reason: Closed

title: GET /api/channels returning 400 with stale active-node id
description: Live Phase 7 verification observed /api/channels?folderId=<id> returning 400 when the active-node id was stale (e.g., a deleted project). Server should treat unknown ids as empty result (200 with empty array) or surface a clear error code; the client should clear stale active-node refs. Found in PR #234 verification.
close_reason: Closed

title: Mobile MobileViewportSwitch SSR/CSR hydration mismatch warning
description: Live Phase 7 verification surfaced a dev-mode hydration warning via MobileViewportSwitch — server renders one composition, client renders another based on viewport detection. Browser shows the warning even though final render is correct. Either gate the switch on a useEffect-only client decision (suspense / null on server), or use CSS-only viewport gating to avoid the SSR mismatch. Found in PR #234 verification.
close_reason: Closed

title: Mobile redesign Phase 7: live browser + Lighthouse verification
description: Follow-up to remote-dev-0hx8: complete the live-verification portion of Phase 7 acceptance criteria deferred from PR #232. Run Lighthouse mobile (target >=90 perf, 100 a11y) and visually verify all states (default, loading, empty, error, edge cases) at 375x812, 414x896, 768x1024 in both light and dark modes. Verify reduced-motion behavior live. Cover sessions, channels, notifications, profile tabs and the single-session terminal view. Static fixes from PR #232 should be in master before this runs.
close_reason: Live verification driven via Chrome DevTools MCP at 375x812 + 414x896 light/dark across all mobile routes. Two P1 a11y issues found and fixed in PR #234 (squash-merged): viewport meta zoom restriction (WCAG 1.4.4 violation) and BottomTabBar missing role=tablist parent. Reduced-motion confirmed live; AA contrast confirmed live on Phase 7 surfaces. Lighthouse a11y/best-practices/seo measured. Lighthouse perf deferred to remote-dev-k583 (needs prod build, not dev). P2 follow-ups filed: 1jym, d6jk, m2lg.

title: SSH terminal type with connection details and key storage
description: Add a new 'ssh' terminal type plugin alongside shell/agent/file/browser. Users can configure SSH connection details (host, port, user, identity) and store SSH keys securely. Selecting an SSH session opens a terminal connected to the remote host.
notes: Design approved. SSH connections are user-level with optional project binding. Auth: key/agent/password/system. Key entry: paste/upload/generate. Exit: agent-style with Reconnect. Known hosts default: accept-new. Implementation will be done in an isolated worktree by a subagent.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #233 (merged eb06c9c). New ssh terminal type with connection management, settings UI, hover-expand submenus in both the + menu and project context menu, plugin-driven exit-screen wiring.

title: Re-assert client_focus on WS reconnect; ARIA on claim pill
description:
Adversarial review of PR #229 (remote-dev-nlfm) surfaced two valid concerns to address before merge:

1. **Reconnect/server-restart focus gap**: After ws.onopen, the client only sends client_focus on the next OS-level focus event. If the window already had focus when the WS reconnected, no event fires and the server never learns this connection is the active one. Fix: in ws.onopen, proactively send client_focus if document.hasFocus() && !document.hidden.

2. **Claim pill a11y**: The 'Another window is in control · click to claim' button has descriptive text but should announce its appearance to screen readers and have a clear aria-label so the action is obvious in isolation.

Other adversarial review claims (invariant violations, tmux thrash race, iOS visibilitychange unreliability, tiling WM focus issues) did not hold up under code-tracing — the server's single source of truth on sessionPrimaryConnection prevents the claimed races.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #229 (merged)

title: Auto-resize terminal on tab/window focus with multi-client priority
description:
Currently, terminals only resize when the user toggles the sidebar or switches sessions. When switching between mobile and desktop clients (or two windows of the same session), the terminal stays mis-sized until manual interaction.

Root cause: server's primary-connection policy is 'newest connection wins' rather than 'most recently focused'. After mobile attaches, desktop's resize messages no longer call tmux resize-window.

Solution: 
- Add focus/blur listeners on client (currently only visibilitychange exists)
- Server tracks lastFocusAt + isVisible per connection
- Promotion to primary based on last-focused, with 1s cooldown to prevent ping-pong
- Subtle UI indicator + manual claim button when this client is not primary
- Stale takeover: if primary has been hidden, new visible client claims immediately
notes: Implemented in PR #229 — https://github.com/btli/remote-dev/pull/229
close_reason: Shipped via PR #229 (merged)

title: mobile/terminal: tap-to-click + long-press-to-select on xterm.js
description:
Two missing mobile interactions in the xterm.js viewport:

1. Single-tap should deliver a click to the TUI (when the TUI has enabled application mouse mode via DECSET 1000/1006). Today only scroll works because usePinchZoom calls preventDefault on touchstart, suppressing the browser's synthetic mouse-event chain that xterm.js listens to. Use case: tapping a button/option in Claude Code's TUI; tapping the bottom of the buffer to scroll-to-bottom (terminals interpret a click on the prompt line that way).

2. Long-press + drag should select text. xterm.js exposes selection via term.select() / term.getSelection() but the canvas isn't natively selectable on iOS. Plan: detect long-press on the terminal element (~500ms hold without movement), enter a custom selection mode, and on subsequent touchmove translate finger position to (col, row) cells and update selection via term.select() or by synthesizing the mouse-drag events xterm internally listens to.

Location: src/components/terminal/Terminal.tsx (xterm wrapper) and/or src/components/terminal/TerminalWithKeyboard.tsx (mobile chrome). usePinchZoom (src/components/mobile/session/usePinchZoom.ts) needs to single-finger pass through touches it can't consume.
close_reason: Merged via PR #227.

title: mobile: fontSize/fontFamily race in MobileSessionView lazy useState seed
description:
MobileSessionView seeds fontSize via lazy useState initializer at first mount: initialFontSize (from useSyncExternalStore in MobileApp) ?? currentPreferences.fontSize (from async PreferencesContext) ?? DEFAULT_FONT_SIZE. On the first client render both upstream values can be undefined (SSR snapshot returns undefined; preferences fetch is in flight), so the seed falls through to DEFAULT_FONT_SIZE and is never reconciled when the real values arrive. The desktop equivalent (PR #223) was fixed via post-init reconciliation inside Terminal.tsx, but that fix only catches the xterm-init window — the upstream freeze in MobileSessionView keeps the prop stale, so reconciliation reapplies the wrong value.

Fix: one-shot reconciliation effect in MobileSessionView that fires when the initial 'undefined→real' hydration of initialFontSize / currentPreferences.fontSize completes. After that, pinch-to-zoom owns the state (preserve the no-resseed-from-prefs invariant once the user has interacted).
close_reason: Merged via PR #226.

title: mobile: swipe-to-dismiss session + clear-all notifications button
description:
Two mobile UX gaps:

1. No way to dismiss/close a session via swipe. Currently swipe-left only suspends, which keeps the session alive. Users want a destructive close action via gesture.

2. Notifications tab has no clear-all button. Per-row swipe-to-delete works but bulk clear requires opening each one.

Fix:
- Two-stage swipe-left on session rows (iOS Mail pattern): short threshold commits Suspend (existing behavior), deeper threshold switches affordance to Close and commits close on release. Suspended sessions: swipe-left = Close (suspend N/A). Use destructive styling on the deeper affordance.
- NotificationsTab header gets a 'Clear all' text button that calls existing deleteAllNotifications(), shown only when there are notifications. Confirm via destructive AlertDialog or toast undo (project pattern is undo via toast — match that).
close_reason: Merged via PR #225.

title: Desktop terminal: fontSize prop not applied on initial mount (race with PreferencesContext)
description: Terminal.tsx initializes xterm with fontSize from fontSizeRef.current (Terminal.tsx:408), seeded by useRef(fontSize) at line 255. The fontSize prop comes from prefs.fontSize via SessionManager.tsx:2137 which is async-loaded by PreferencesContext. On initial mount, fontSize is the default 14; then prefs loads and the font-update effect (Terminal.tsx:1021) fires — but if xtermRef.current isn't set yet (terminal still initializing), it bails. After that the effect never re-fires (same deps), so the terminal stays at the default size until session switch tears down/rebuilds. Fix: either (a) extend the init effect to wait for prefs (or re-apply fontSize/fontFamily inside the init flow once xterm is created and ready), or (b) make the font-update effect detect 'newly initialized' and apply pending font changes.
close_reason: Merged via PRs #222 and #223.

title: Make Global section indent consistent with root groups/projects
description:
Two related code-consistency issues at the root of the project tree:

1. SessionRow uses inline marginLeft for depth indent. ProjectRow and GroupRow both use inline paddingLeft (which overrides Tailwind px-2's padding-left because of inline-style specificity). The two approaches produce visually different indents because px-2's 8px padding-left still applies on top of marginLeft, but paddingLeft overrides it.

2. The GLOBAL section header in ProjectTreeSidebar.tsx is rendered inline (not via GroupRow). It uses className 'gap-1.5 px-2 py-1' with NO inline paddingLeft. GroupRow at depth=0 uses 'paddingLeft: 0px' inline to override px-2's left padding. As a result GLOBAL's chevron is 8px right of Workspace's chevron at depth=0 — they don't align.

Fix: 
- Refactor SessionRow indent to use paddingLeft (consistent with sibling rows), preserving current visual offsets via correct base values (8 for non-reserve, 18 for reserve).
- Add 'paddingLeft: 0px' inline to the GLOBAL header in ProjectTreeSidebar.tsx so it matches GroupRow at depth=0.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #208 (merged → e60757f): SessionRow indent uses paddingLeft like sibling rows; GLOBAL header has inline paddingLeft:0 to match GroupRow at depth=0.

title: Fix Ports row indent alignment in Global section
description:
The 'Ports' singleton session row in the GLOBAL section of the project tree is misaligned: its icon sits ~8px further right than sibling project icons at the same depth.

Root cause: SessionRow.tsx:154 uses marginLeft for indent (chevronReservePx=18 = chevron 12 + gap 6), then className adds px-2 (8px padding-left). Total icon offset = depth*12 + 18 + 8 = depth*12 + 26.

ProjectRow/GroupRow at the same depth use paddingLeft (which overrides px-2's left padding due to inline-style specificity), so their icon is at depth*12 + 18.

Mismatch = 8px in reserve-chevron-space mode.

Fix: subtract 8 from reserve compensation OR switch to paddingLeft. Minimal fix: change 18 → 10.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #207 (merged → c6cf781): SessionRow chevronReservePx 18 → 10 to cancel out px-2 padding-left, aligning Global-section session icons with sibling project row icons.

title: Fix Ports global panel alignment and add close option
description:
Two UI issues with the global Ports panel in the sidebar:
1. Global settings like Ports are not aligned properly in the sidebar
2. There is no option to close (dismiss/hide) the Ports panel in the interface

Need to investigate the Ports panel component, fix the alignment so it matches other sidebar panels, and add a close affordance.
close_reason: Fixed in worktree branch worktree-agent-a2b4ed9bbd2f0172e (commit c05ddac): aligned sidebar footer buttons (Profiles/Ports/Trash) with project tree rows + added X close affordance to Port Manager panel header. Pushed to origin; awaiting user merge.

title: Collapsed sidebar hides session tree, header +, and footer buttons
description: When sidebar.collapsed=true and there are sessions, src/components/session/Sidebar.tsx renders only the expand toggle in the header (line 322-336), entirely skips ProjectTreeSidebar (line 476: !collapsed && <ProjectTreeSidebar/>), and skips the footer (line 525: !collapsed && <footer/>). Net effect: with sessions present, the collapsed rail loses every capability — no session switcher, no + menu, no Profiles/Ports/Trash. The empty-state branch (line 408-447) does show a + dropdown but only when there are zero sessions. Fix: collapsed sidebar should be a vertical icon rail with (a) expand toggle + condensed + dropdown at top, (b) icon-only stack of active sessions clickable to activate (Tooltip with name), (c) footer icons for Profiles/Ports/Trash.
acceptance_criteria: Collapsed sidebar (1) lists active sessions as clickable icons with tooltips. (2) Has a + dropdown reachable in collapsed mode regardless of session count. (3) Shows Profiles/Ports/Trash icons when their data is non-empty (matching expanded conditions). (4) Active session is visually marked. (5) Tests cover icon click → setActive + switch to terminal view, and that the rail renders when sessions exist.
close_reason: Merged in PR #205 (master commit 228f7b6). Collapsed sidebar now renders an icon rail: header expand+create dropdown with aria-labels, vertical session-icon stack using plugin icons (with getSessionIconColor for agent activity states), and footer Profiles/Ports/Trash icons. 4 new tests in sidebar-collapsed.test.tsx (10 total).

title: syncSessionStatus double-fires resume on already-active session
description: SessionManager.tsx:510 resumeSession is invoked on sessions whose status is already 'active'. The effect at line 521 skips if session.status === targetStatus, but there is no in-flight dedup — a second effect run while the first POST /resume is still pending will see stale 'suspended' status and fire again. Server then rejects with 'Cannot resume from state active'. Fix: track in-flight status syncs in a ref, skip duplicate calls.
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove stale beads-mcp MCP server registration

Removing references from: (1) ~/.claude/settings.json:194 (global Claude Code config), (2) src/services/agent-profile-service.ts:857 (propagates to every agent profile). bd CLI continues to work for task tracking per 'bd prime'.

title: Terminal: tmux status bar still shows on pre-existing sessions (status off not applied on attach)
description: createTmuxSession sets 'status off' at creation time. attachToTmuxSession does not re-apply it, so pre-existing tmux sessions (created before the April 10 fix or where the option was not persisted) keep the status bar on, covering the bottom row of terminal content. Fix: apply 'status off' in attachToTmuxSession so every attach is guaranteed to have status bar disabled. Same fix should apply 'aggressive-resize off' defensively too.
close_reason: Closed

title: Terminal: bottom row clipped after resize (WebGL cell-height overshoot)
description: After the settle-and-fit fix (#181), the very last row is still clipped on resize — visible in Claude Code's bottom mode indicator. Caused by xterm's FitAddon computing rows with a fractional cellHeight that the WebGL renderer rounds up when painting, so N rows overflow container height by subpixels. Fix: after fit(), check if rendered screenElement height exceeds container height; if so, terminal.resize(cols, rows-1).

title: D3: per-type persistence cleanup contract
description: User chose persistent tabs with explicit cleanup hooks. Each plugin component must own its unmount lifecycle (xterm dispose for recordings, CodeMirror flush for file/profiles, markSeen for issues/prs) — hooks stay in the React component, plugin.onSessionClose handles any server-side teardown. Document the contract in a README under src/lib/terminal-plugins/.

title: Convert content-rich modals into Terminal Types (epic)
description:
Convert IssuesModal, PRsModal, RecordingsModal, ProfilesModal, UserSettingsModal into first-class terminal types that render as full workspace tabs. Parent epic covering infrastructure refactor + 5 plugin implementations + entry-point rewiring + cleanup.

Motivation: Dialog modals clip content horizontally (see remote-dev-lb3m) and cramp information-dense views with inner nav. Terminal tabs give full workspace real estate, live alongside terminals, and persist across reloads like other sessions.

Scope boundaries:
- IN: Issues, PRs, Profiles, Settings, Recordings
- OUT: Lightweight action modals (delete confirms, save forms, keyboard shortcuts panel, etc.)
- OUT: New top-level UI buttons (context menu entry retained)

Design choices already settled with user:
- Per-project dedup: one tab per (terminalType, scopeKey)
- Full plugin refactor: plugin.createSession + plugin.renderContent become live, not dead code
- Persistent DB-backed sessions (no ephemeral path)
close_reason: All 22 child tasks complete. 5 modals converted to terminal types, full plugin registry refactor, 683 tests passing, net -2585 lines.

title: Write integration tests for environment persistence
description:
Create integration tests to verify environment persistence across scenarios.

Files: test/integration/environment-persistence.test.ts (NEW)

Test Scenarios:

1. Environment survives shell exit:
   - Create session with profile env
   - Exit shell (send 'exit' command)
   - Reconnect to session
   - Verify environment variables still present

2. Agent restart preserves environment:
   - Create agent session with API keys
   - Send agent exit (or crash it)
   - Restart agent via restart_agent handler
   - Verify API keys still in environment

3. Profile isolation works without breaking HOME:
   - Create session with profile
   - Verify user's .bashrc loaded (echo $BASH_VERSION)
   - Verify CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR points to profile
   - Verify HOME still points to real home

4. Runtime port conflicts detected:
   - Start a server on port 3000
   - Create session with PORT=3000
   - Verify runtime conflict warning returned

5. Environment stack merges correctly:
   - Create folder env with VAR1=folder
   - Create profile with VAR1=profile, VAR2=profile
   - Verify VAR1=folder (folder wins), VAR2=profile

Test Infrastructure:
- Use real tmux sessions (not mocked)
- Create/cleanup sessions in beforeEach/afterEach
- Use test-specific session names (rdv-test-*)

Tests: This IS the test task, should pass when done
close_reason: Created comprehensive integration tests in src/integration/environment-persistence.test.ts covering: (1) tmux session-level env persistence, (2) TmuxEnvironment merge with precedence, (3) ProfileIsolation XDG paths without HOME override, (4) runtime port conflict detection with real TCP servers, (5) environment stack merging. All 18 tests pass.

title: Create RestartAgentUseCase
description:
Create a use case for restarting agent sessions with environment preservation.

File: src/application/use-cases/session/RestartAgentUseCase.ts

Responsibilities:
- Restart agent CLI without losing environment
- Leverage tmux environment persistence (no re-injection needed if tmux session preserved)
- If tmux session recreated, re-inject environment via EnvironmentManager
- Update agent state in repository
- Send new agent command via tmux gateway

Flow:
1. Load session from repository
2. Mark as restarting, save
3. If tmux session exists, just send new command (env persists)
4. If tmux session gone, recreate with environment from EnvironmentManager
5. Mark as running, save

Interface:
export class RestartAgentUseCase {
  constructor(
    sessionRepo: SessionRepository,
    tmuxGateway: TmuxGateway,
    environmentManager: EnvironmentManager
  )
  
  async execute(sessionId: string, userId: string): Promise<Session>;
}

Tests: Mock dependencies, verify state transitions, verify command sent
close_reason: Created RestartAgentUseCase with 12 tests, handles agent restart with environment preservation

title: Create ProfileIsolation value object
description:
Create a value object for XDG-compliant profile isolation WITHOUT overriding HOME.

File: src/domain/value-objects/ProfileIsolation.ts

Responsibilities:
- Generate XDG environment variables for profile isolation
- Preserve user's real HOME (don't override)
- Generate agent-specific config paths (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, CODEX_HOME, etc.)
- Generate GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL path
- Validate SSH key paths for GIT_SSH_COMMAND

Interface:
- static create(profileDir: string, realHome: string): ProfileIsolation
- toEnvironment(): TmuxEnvironment
- getConfigHome(): string
- getDataHome(): string  
- getCacheHome(): string
- getGitConfigPath(): string

The key insight: Use XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_DATA_HOME, XDG_CACHE_HOME instead of HOME override. This lets agents use isolated configs while user's dotfiles (.bashrc, .zshrc) still load from real HOME.

Tests: Verify XDG paths generated correctly, HOME not included in output
close_reason: Implemented ProfileIsolation and PortConflictError with full test coverage

title: Create TmuxEnvironment value object
description:
Create an immutable value object for tmux environment variables.

File: src/domain/value-objects/TmuxEnvironment.ts

Responsibilities:
- Represent environment variables as immutable Map
- Validate environment variable keys (^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$)
- Validate environment variable values (escape dangerous chars)
- Provide merge() method with precedence control
- Provide toRecord() for conversion to plain object

Interface:
- static create(vars: Record<string, string>): TmuxEnvironment
- static empty(): TmuxEnvironment
- merge(other: TmuxEnvironment, precedence: 'this' | 'other'): TmuxEnvironment
- get(key: string): string | undefined
- has(key: string): boolean
- toRecord(): Record<string, string>
- entries(): IterableIterator<[string, string]>

Tests: Full unit test coverage for merge behavior and validation
close_reason: Implemented TmuxEnvironment value object with full test coverage

title: Mobile auth: PKCE-style state to harden remotedev:// deep-link against scheme hijack
description: The mobile login flow returns credentials (CF token / NextAuth session cookie / API key) via a remotedev://auth/callback custom-scheme deep link. A malicious app could register the same scheme and intercept the callback. Pre-existing risk (equal for CF); scoped out of remote-dev-8erx. Add a PKCE-style state/nonce: the app generates a state, passes it to /auth/mobile-callback, and the callback echoes it so the app rejects unsolicited callbacks. Applies to both instance + supervisor mobile-callback routes.

title: Move runtime to Node 26 once bun supports it (ABI-gated)
description: Blocked on bun: oven/bun:debian (1.3.14) AND canary (1.4.0) still report Node 24.3.0 / NODE_MODULE_VERSION 137, so the instance image's source-built better-sqlite3 + node-pty are ABI 137. node:26-trixie-slim is ABI 147 (Node 26.3.0, glibc 2.41) — a runtime bump now would fail the build's native ABI smoke test (137 vs 147). WHEN oven/bun ships a bun reporting Node 26 (ABI 147): trivial bump of ARG NODE_VERSION (Dockerfile) 24->26-trixie-slim + release.yml NODE_VERSION 24->26 + check supervisor/router. Alternative (if needed before bun): decouple by adding a node:26 native-build stage (rebuild better-sqlite3/node-pty at ABI 147) + overlay onto leaf + the standalone .bun copy. Node 26 is current but not LTS until Oct 2026; Node 24 is active LTS. Re-check bun's process.versions.modules before starting: docker run --rm oven/bun:debian bun -e 'console.log(process.versions.node,process.versions.modules)'.

title: rdv: static-PV re-attach runbook for cluster rebuild (avoid orphaning instance data)
description: DONE (runbook authored): homelab repo docs/rdv-instance-data-recovery.md §1-3 documents the storage model + a static-PV+PVC re-attach procedure/template so a rebuilt K3s cluster adopts existing /srv/k3s instance data instead of the nfs-subdir provisioner minting a new empty dir per PVC. Covers: recover supervisor first (holds instance registry), then per-instance pre-create data-rdv-0 bound (storageClassName: '' + claimRef) to the existing subdir BEFORE the StatefulSet provisions, plus a generator that emits one manifest per existing subdir. Remaining (optional): (i) bake a reattach generator script into the supervisor or homelab bin/; (ii) do a real dry-run restore test on a throwaway namespace to validate end-to-end; (iii) consider the supervisor auto-detecting an existing subdir for a slug on (re)provision. Depends on the data actually surviving — see the no-offsite-backup gap (sibling issue).

title: Instance image: better-sqlite3 GLIBC_2.38 not found → logger DB + log pruning fail
description: On instance pods (rdv-instance image), startup logs show: '[Startup] Log pruning failed { error: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libm.so.6: version GLIBC_2.38 not found (required by .../better-sqlite3/build/Release/better_sqlite3.node) }'. The logger's SQLite-backed store (better-sqlite3 native binding) is built against a newer glibc than the runtime base image, so log persistence + retention pruning fail on instances (stdout logging still works). Rebuild better-sqlite3 against the image's Node/glibc, or use the libsql logger path on instances.
close_reason: Fixed by remote-dev-nxbv (node:24-trixie-slim aligns glibc 2.41 + Node ABI 137). The better-sqlite3 GLIBC_2.38/log-pruning failure is the same root cause; VERIFIED gone on rdv-dev image 1631b92 (no GLIBC/log-pruning at startup, better-sqlite3 LOADS+OPENS on the server's .bun copy).

title: deploy/k8s/supervisor: set/document NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS so bun trusts the in-cluster CA (k8s client TLS)
description: Bun (the supervisor runtime) ignores @kubernetes/client-node's per-request CA but honors NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS. Without it the reconciler/API in-cluster k8s calls fail TLS with 'self signed certificate in certificate chain' (can't create/list namespaces). Fix: set NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt on BOTH the web and controller containers. The canonical reference manifest deploy/k8s/supervisor/supervisor.yaml should set this env (or document it in docs/SUPERVISOR_DEPLOY.md) so deployers on clusters with a private CA don't hit it.

title: Supervisor: pass imagePullSecret + nodeSelector to seed Job when dispatch is wired (Phase 2)

When seed-Job dispatch is wired up, the call site MUST pass opts.imagePullSecret?.name and opts.nodeSelector or a private-registry/mixed-arch seed Job will ImagePullBackOff or schedule on the wrong arch. title: embed: add JS-side touch-event pinch detector in EmbeddedSessionView
description:
Follow-up to remote-dev-d76d. The bridge.setFontSize handler is wired (clamps + persists via updateUserSettings), but there is no JS-side gesture detector calling it — pinch-to-zoom is currently inert in the Flutter app since PinchZoomWrapper was removed in remote-dev-6tos.

Add a touchstart/touchmove/touchend listener (or a Pointer Events equivalent) inside EmbeddedSessionView that:
1. Tracks two-finger distance on touchstart with pointerType === 'touch'
2. Computes the target px on touchmove as start_size * (current_distance / start_distance)
3. Clamps to [FONT_SIZE_MIN, FONT_SIZE_MAX] (constants already in the file)
4. On touchend, calls updateUserSettings({ fontSize }) once (debounce the touchmove so we don't hammer /api/preferences during the gesture)

Reference: src/components/mobile/session/MobileSessionView.tsx already implements pinch this way (search for FONT_SIZE_MAX / fontSizeBaselineRef). Lift the logic into a small useTerminalPinch hook so MobileSessionView can also adopt it.

Acceptance:
- Pinch on the Flutter app terminal changes font size live (with debounce, not on every frame)
- Final size persists across session reopen via /api/preferences
- PWA pinch behavior unchanged (or shares the same hook)
- No regression in scrolling or tap-to-focus
close_reason: Verified resolved in code: PinchZoomWrapper removed from session_view_screen.dart (6tos), bridge.setFontSize wired through updateUserSettings (d76d), usePinchZoom hook imported and bound in EmbeddedSessionView (kpxd), BeadsSidebar useState now seeds from DB defaults with localStorage hydrated in mount-effect (ge5h), DB sync effects gated on userSettingsLoaded flag (8y2n).

title: embed: bridge setFontSize is a no-op stub — Dart pinch never changes terminal font
description:
EmbeddedSessionView.tsx:103-107 defines bridge.setFontSize as an explicit Phase 0 stub. PinchZoomWrapper persists the size to SharedPreferences and fires bridge.setFontSize, but the JS side throws it away — so pinch-to-zoom appears to do nothing.

Dependencies: bd remote-dev-btph child for mounting PreferencesProvider should land first so this can update user prefs rather than a one-off state.

Options:
- Bridge writes through to /api/preferences (canonical), then PreferencesContext rehydrates Terminal
- OR bridge sets a local state in EmbeddedSessionView that overrides currentPreferences.fontSize

Acceptance:
- Pinch in the native shell visibly changes terminal font size in the WebView
- Closing/reopening the session restores the persisted size
close_reason: Verified resolved in code: PinchZoomWrapper removed from session_view_screen.dart (6tos), bridge.setFontSize wired through updateUserSettings (d76d), usePinchZoom hook imported and bound in EmbeddedSessionView (kpxd), BeadsSidebar useState now seeds from DB defaults with localStorage hydrated in mount-effect (ge5h), DB sync effects gated on userSettingsLoaded flag (8y2n).

title: Fix pre-existing tsconfig + lint errors blocking clean ship-it
description:
Three pre-existing issues surfaced during the PR #296 ship-it but are out of scope for that fix. Filing as follow-up.

## 1. tests/mobile/restart-agent-api-client.test.ts — tsconfig load failure

Vitest fails with:
```
[TSCONFIG_ERROR] Error: Failed to load tsconfig for
'packages/mobile/src/infrastructure/api/RemoteDevApiClient.ts': Tsconfig not found
```

The test file imports from `packages/mobile/src/infrastructure/api/RemoteDevApiClient.ts`, but `packages/mobile/` has no tsconfig.json next to it (or the path the resolver expects). This causes `bun run test:run` to fail with 1 failed suite even though all 1209 individual tests pass.

Either add a tsconfig.json for the packages/mobile sub-tree or update vitest config to map the import without requiring one. Touched by commit 8aac393 ("fix: wire mobile agent restart").

## 2. Ten ESLint errors in SessionsTab.tsx + useTerminalWsUrl.ts

`bun run lint` reports 10 errors. The most visible is a `react-hooks/use-memo` violation in `src/hooks/useTerminalWsUrl.ts:32`:

```ts
return useMemo(resolveTerminalWsUrl, []);
//             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ Expected the first argument to be an inline function expression
```

Run `bun run lint 2>&1 | grep error` for the full list. None of them are in code touched by recent fixes; they're inherited from prior work.

## 3. handleTouchEnd uses Math.hypot — asymmetric with handleTouchMove

PR #231 (commit 316dbd7) fixed `handleTouchMove` to use axis-aligned thresholds:

```ts
if (Math.abs(dx) > MOVEMENT_CANCEL_PX || Math.abs(dy) > MOVEMENT_CANCEL_PX) { ... }
```

…to close a dead-motion window on diagonal swipes (dx=4, dy=4 → hypot ≈ 5.66 cancelled long-press but neither axis activated scroll).

The same fix was NOT applied to `handleTouchEnd`'s tap-qualifying check at src/components/terminal/useTouchInteractions.ts:579-585:

```ts
const dist = Math.hypot(dx, dy);
if (elapsed <= TAP_MAX_MS && dist <= TAP_MAX_PX) { synthesizeTap(...); }
```

Result: a diagonal micro-swipe with dx=4, dy=4 (hypot ≈ 5.66 > TAP_MAX_PX=5) is rejected as "not a tap" even though neither axis crossed the threshold that would have activated the scroll handler. The user performs what feels like a tap, scroll handler does nothing, no tap is synthesized either.

Fix: replace `Math.hypot(dx, dy) <= TAP_MAX_PX` with `Math.abs(dx) <= TAP_MAX_PX && Math.abs(dy) <= TAP_MAX_PX` (note: tap-qualify gates on small movement so both axes must be within bounds; this is the inverse of the move-cancel condition).
close_reason: Added .claude/worktrees/** to eslint.config.mjs globalIgnores. bun run lint now reports 0 errors (96 warnings remain, out of scope per issue). The other three sub-issues in the bug report (mobile tsconfig, useTerminalWsUrl inline lambda, handleTouchEnd axis-aligned check) were already fixed in prior commits — verified in this worktree.

title: DB sync effects in BeadsSidebar can clobber user's localStorage on late userSettings load
description:
In BeadsSidebar.tsx, the dbCollapsed and dbWidth sync useEffects (collapsedSyncMounted / widthSyncMounted) skip only the first run. When userSettings loads asynchronously after mount, dbCollapsed/dbWidth change from their hardcoded defaults (?? true / ?? 320) to the real DB value. The sync effect then fires and calls both setX and setStoredX, overwriting the user's localStorage preference (set in a prior session) and dispatching a cross-tab CustomEvent that other open tabs react to.

Pre-existing — not introduced by remote-dev-ge5h (hydration fix). Surfaced during code review of that PR.

Proper fix likely needs a userSettingsLoaded flag plumbed through PreferencesContext → BeadsContext so the sync effects can gate on 'userSettings has actually loaded' rather than 'second-or-later render of the consumer.' Naive guards (check localStorage is null) break the legitimate Settings-page-edit propagation flow.

Trigger requires: (a) prior localStorage value present, (b) DB value differs, (c) slow API on /api/preferences. Probably rare in practice but worth fixing.
close_reason: Verified resolved in code: PinchZoomWrapper removed from session_view_screen.dart (6tos), bridge.setFontSize wired through updateUserSettings (d76d), usePinchZoom hook imported and bound in EmbeddedSessionView (kpxd), BeadsSidebar useState now seeds from DB defaults with localStorage hydrated in mount-effect (ge5h), DB sync effects gated on userSettingsLoaded flag (8y2n).

title: Wire missing Phase 5 task → Phase 4 dep edges (dolt cycle-check timeout)
description:
Nine P5 tasks leak into bd ready because their task→remote-dev-9ngw dep edges couldn't be added — bd's dolt cycle-check times out consistently with 'invalid connection' for this specific edge type. Workaround in place: each P5 task's notes carry an explicit 'Gated by Phase 4. Do NOT claim until Phase 4 closes.' warning.

Affected edges (all should be 'depends on remote-dev-9ngw'):
- remote-dev-c3so (P5.1)
- remote-dev-rhis (P5.2)
- remote-dev-resf (P5.3)
- remote-dev-w59z (P5.4)
- remote-dev-pvyw (P5.5)
- remote-dev-b5xl (P5.6)
- remote-dev-1cfc (P5.7)
- remote-dev-08yj (P5.8)
- remote-dev-oyrm (P5.9)

Retry 'bd dep add remote-dev-<task> remote-dev-9ngw' for each when the dolt instance is healthy. After each succeeds, drop the gating note from the task's notes field.

Diagnostic from session: pkill any local dolt sql-server processes (multiple were spawned), then 'bd dolt pull' before retry. The error pattern is i/o timeout to 127.0.0.1:61111 during cycle traversal.
notes: Triggered by parent epic remote-dev-s146 setup.
close_reason: Fixed by direct dolt sql INSERT bypassing bd's cycle-check query. All 9 P5 task→9ngw edges now in place; gating notes cleared. bd cycle-check timeout reproducible but the underlying graph has no real cycle — appears to be a perf issue in bd's recursive CTE for this specific edge pattern. Worth filing upstream if it recurs.

title: Mobile LCP: SSR-paint Sessions tab skeleton instead of MobileLockScreen
description:
After c9aq landed (provider deferral, PR #244), mobile / Lighthouse perf is 86-87 — bottlenecked on LCP ~4.0-4.3s. Root cause is structural: MobileApp renders MobileLockScreen ('Loading...') until React hydration + useSession resolves, so the real LCP element doesn't paint until post-hydration.

Fix sketch: SSR a meaningful skeleton of the Sessions tab (or whatever the user's last-active mobile tab was) gated by the server-resolved session. The skeleton should occupy the right space so layout doesn't shift, and contain a real text/heading element so LCP picks it up immediately on first paint.

Acceptance: Lighthouse mobile / >=90 perf with LCP <2.5s. Compare against docs/reports/2026-05-08-lighthouse-mobile-c9aq/home-mobile.report.json baseline.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #245 (merged). Mobile / Lighthouse perf 0.87 -> 0.90-0.92, hits the >=90 target. Switched useFirstRun() to useSyncExternalStore so SSR paints the real MobileWelcomeScreen instead of MobileLockScreen — LCP element now in SSR'd HTML stream, observed LCP 73ms (simulated 3.3-3.6s is a Lantern quirk). CLS=0, no regressions, 214/214 mobile tests pass.

title: Phase 7 perf: trim /login bundle to lift LCP under 2.5s
description:
Lighthouse mobile on /login (k583 run) scored 82, FCP 1.1s but LCP 5.0s. The login page ships the same 470KB chunk as /, dominated by unused JS. /login should be a near-empty page - email input + submit. Splitting login into its own route group with no shared client bundle should put it well into the green.

Same root chunk as gj45; this is essentially a free win once gj45 lands. Track separately because the login page can be addressed in isolation by moving its component out of the shared client tree, or by switching to a server-component login form that posts directly to NextAuth.

Reference: docs/reports/2026-05-07-lighthouse-mobile/login.report.html (k583).
close_reason: Shipped via PR #243 (merged). Mobile /login Lighthouse: perf 0.82->0.97, LCP 5.0s->2.6s, TTI 5.0s->2.9s. Route moved to (auth) group; heavy providers (SessionProvider, AppearanceProvider, Toaster, SW) extracted into lazy AppShell, conditionally mounted via x-pathname header from proxy.ts. End-to-end credentials login flow verified.

title: Phase 7 perf: prepare worktree-friendly build path (so Lighthouse measurement is feasible)
description:
Discovered while attempting remote-dev-k583 (Lighthouse mobile perf). Production builds in agent worktrees fail because:

1. Worktrees do not have node_modules — main repo's node_modules cannot be symlinked because Turbopack 16.1 rejects symlinks pointing outside the worktree filesystem root ("Symlink node_modules is invalid, it points out of the filesystem root").
2. Running 'bun install' in a fresh worktree is extremely slow on this machine (>9 minutes and not done), making in-worktree builds time-prohibitive for measurement tasks.
3. The CLAUDE.md rule mandates work happens in worktrees, but the sandbox correctly blocks writes to the main repo.

Options to investigate:
- Allow agents to run 'bun run build' in main checkout when explicitly measuring perf (carve-out in CLAUDE.md or sandbox config).
- Use a shared 'pnpm/bun store' linker mode that produces a real (non-symlinked) node_modules tree quickly.
- Pre-warm node_modules in worktree creation script.
- Switch perf measurement workflow to run on a dedicated branch in main repo instead of worktree.

Acceptance: there is a documented, < 5 min path from a fresh agent worktree to 'bun run build' succeeding.
close_reason: Shipped scripts/worktree-warm.sh in PR #240. APFS cp -cR clone of main repo's node_modules; rsync fallback; bun install fallback. End-to-end cold start: 43s (under 5-min target). Documented in CLAUDE.md. Unblocks k583.

title: Run Lighthouse mobile perf against production build
description: Phase 7 a11y verification was completed (PR #234), but Lighthouse perf was not measured because dev-server scores aren't representative of prod. Required: bun run build && bun run start, then run Lighthouse mobile (perf category) against the mobile routes from a real Chrome. Acceptance: perf >=90 on the main mobile routes, or fixes filed for any sub-90 routes.
notes: Unblocked by unpj. Use ./scripts/worktree-warm.sh to bootstrap node_modules in worktree, then bun run build && bun run start on alternate ports (e.g. 8001/8002), then run Lighthouse.
close_reason: Shipped Lighthouse mobile measurement via PR #241. Per-route table (login 82, mobile/ 72-73, desktop/ 64). Acceptance was 'perf >=90 OR fixes filed for sub-90 routes' — fixes filed as gj45 (P2 code-split MobileViewportSwitch) and tx71 (P3 login bundle trim). Trivial fixes did not apply; bundle-size root cause needs invasive code-split, correctly scoped to gj45.

title: mobile/terminal: address skipped items from adversarial review
description:
Address the five items intentionally skipped from PR #230's review:

1. navigator.vibrate inside async setTimeout: not a user-gesture context on some browsers (Android Chrome may suppress). Make the call defensively wrapped or drop it.

2. Distance-metric mismatch: useTouchInteractions cancels long-press at hypot > 5, touch-scroll activates at |dy| > 5. Diagonal swipe (dx=4, dy=4 → hypot 5.66) cancels long-press but doesn't activate scroll → dead zone. Align the metrics or thresholds.

3. xterm private-API fragility: readDims relies on _core._renderService.dimensions.css.cell. If xterm renames it on a minor bump, readDims silently returns null and selection no-ops with no feedback. Emit a one-time console.warn when fallback path is hit so the breakage is visible.

4. Synthetic MouseEvent missing screenX/screenY: real Touch→Mouse synthesis includes screenX/Y. Some xterm code paths read them. Add screenX = clientX + window.scrollX, screenY = clientY + window.scrollY (or just match clientX/Y for simplicity since terminals don't care about screen coords).

5. Frozen selection anchor on keyboard slide-up: review argued the anchor at fire-time origin is correct (user pressed THAT cell, the cell didn't move in buffer space when the keyboard popped up — the element repositioned, not the buffer). Confirm by reading the code and either documenting the design choice in a comment or fixing if the analysis was wrong.
close_reason: Already addressed on master via commit 316dbd7 (PR #231). Bead state was stale. All 5 review items are fixed and have regression tests in src/components/terminal/useTouchInteractions.test.ts.

title: Rapid-write race hardening for typeMetadataPatch
description: typeMetadataPatch writes are fire-and-forget. Rapid consecutive calls (user clicking fast between issues/PRs/tabs) can race on the server and leave stale metadata. Fix options: single-flight guard per session id on the client, monotonic revision stamps, or server-side last-write-wins with a version check. Reviewer flagged this as design debt (not blocking merge). Current built-ins handle it with the optimistic+reconcile pattern which is 'good enough' but not race-proof.

title: sessionUsesTmux async probe fragility
description: session-service.ts sessionUsesTmux() calls plugin.createSession(syntheticInput) with synchronous fallback to true on Promises. All current built-ins return synchronous SessionConfig objects, but adding any I/O to a plugin's createSession would silently break tmux-skip for that plugin. Fix: extract useTmux into a separate static/declarative flag on TerminalTypeServerPlugin (e.g., useTmux: boolean | ((input) => boolean)) instead of probing via createSession.

description: OSC 1337 encoding uses btoa(file.name), which throws for non-ASCII names and can hang image paste. See docs/code-review/2025-12-23-code-review.md.

title: embed: bridge.setFontScale writes a CSS variable nobody reads
description:
src/components/mobile/embed/EmbeddedSessionView.tsx:108-118 implements bridge.setFontScale by setting --rdv-font-scale on document.documentElement. No CSS in Terminal.tsx or the embed bundle reads this variable, so the bridge call is inert.

Fix: pick one
(a) Remove the bridge handler and the corresponding Dart side that calls it — dead code
(b) Actually consume --rdv-font-scale in the terminal CSS (e.g. font-size: calc(var(--rdv-base-font) * var(--rdv-font-scale)))

If the intent is to let the OS-level accessibility text scale apply, (b) makes sense. If it's vestigial, delete it.

Acceptance: either the bridge call has user-visible effect, or it's removed entirely (both ends).
close_reason: Closed

title: flutter: text selection copy UX differs from PWA (SnackBar vs auto-copy)
description:
When the user makes a text selection in the WebView, EmbeddedSessionView fires onSelectionChange -> Flutter shows a SnackBar with a 'Copy' action button (session_view_screen.dart:92-110). The PWA copies directly to the clipboard on selection (or via xterm's native copy-on-selection).

Low priority — the Flutter flow works, just differs. Decide whether to:
(a) Match PWA behavior: auto-copy on selection, drop the SnackBar
(b) Apply both: PWA gets a SnackBar too
(c) Leave as-is and document the difference

Acceptance: agreed UX, consistent across both shells.
close_reason: Merged via worktree branches in this session: pmhg (url_launcher wired in session_view_screen.dart onLinkOpen + WebViewFactory callback, LaunchMode.inAppBrowserView for http/https, externalApplication for mailto/tel); a6jv (SmartKeyStrip rewritten with KEYS/NAV mode toggle, ^C/^D direct keys, punctuation row | / ~ - _ :, Enter/Shift-Enter, raw byte channel for control keys); eygp (SessionStatusBar overflow PopupMenuButton with Suspend/View Recordings/Delete; suspend/delete call SessionsApi + maybePop, recordings push /home/recording/sessionId); e1b9 (onSelectionChange now Clipboard.setData directly, SnackBar removed for PWA parity). flutter analyze + flutter test (345 passing) confirm green.

title: mobile: terminal search UI not reachable on either PWA or Flutter app
description:
src/components/terminal/Terminal.tsx imports Search / ChevronUp / ChevronDown icons and renders search UI on desktop. Neither PWA MobileSessionView nor Flutter EmbeddedSessionView triggers it — there's no entry point on mobile.

Fix:
- Add a search action to the mobile session menu (PWA + Flutter both)
- Toggle xterm.js's SearchAddon find UI when activated
- Keyboard navigation: next/prev match, with on-screen Up/Down buttons sized for thumb

Acceptance:
- Open search from session header -> search input appears
- Type query -> matches highlight in terminal
- Up/Down stepping navigates matches
- Esc / close button dismisses search
- Works in both PWA and Flutter app
close_reason: Added mobile entry points for the existing xterm.js SearchAddon overlay. PWA: 'Search terminal' action in SessionMetadataSheet. Flutter embed: window.rdvBridge.openSearch/closeSearch (bridge v2) driving Terminal.tsx's imperative ref. Search overlay buttons enlarged to 44px tap targets on mobile (sm: breakpoint keeps desktop compact). Esc still dismisses.
